## Introduction to FUPRE 
The Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun is the 1st university of its kind in Africa and 6th in the world. The university has declared open the sale of Post-UTME Application Forms for the 2026/2027 Academic Session. 
## Application Timeline 
* Commencement Date: Monday, 25th May, 2026 
* Closing Date: Friday, 17th July, 2026 
## Candidate Eligibility Criteria 
Before beginning your registration process online, please thoroughly review the criteria below to confirm your eligibility: 
* Visit the Portal: Open your web browser and go to the university's portal 
* Generate Invoice: Click on the “Generate Invoice” button to download or print your customized payment invoice code. 
* Make Payment: Pay the non-refundable screening fee of ₦2,000.00 (Two Thousand Naira) only using the approved electronic billing channels detailed directly on your invoice sheet. 
* Initialize Form: Go back to the registration link and fill in your official JAMB Registration Number to unlock your bio-data fields. 
* Secure Your Account: Choose a robust PASSWORD that you can easily remember. You will require this same password to check your screening results and your eventual admission status. 
* Phone Number: Input the precise mobile phone number tied natively to your original JAMB documentation. 
## Official Action Steps for Candidates 
1. Visit the university's website 
2. Click on the Post-UTME registration link 
3. Fill in your JAMB registration number 
4. Make payment and print receipt 
5. Fill in your bio-data and submit

The West African Examinations Council has released the 2026 November/December examination timetable.
fullContent:
---

✅ WAEC 2026 Timetable Overview

The West African Examinations Council (WAEC) has officially released the timetable for the 2026 West African Senior School Certificate Examination (WASSCE) for Private Candidates – Second Series, commonly known as the Nov/Dec GCE .

The examination is scheduled to run from Saturday, October 24, 2026, to Friday, December 18, 2026 .

---

📌 Key Dates and Subjects

Here are some of the core subjects and their scheduled dates:

Date Subject/Paper
Saturday, October 24 Foods and Nutrition 3 (Practical Planning Session), Home Management 3 (Practical Planning Session) 
Monday, November 9 Engineering Science, Animal Husbandry (Alt. A) 
Tuesday, November 10 Further Mathematics/Mathematics (Elective) 
Wednesday, November 11 Financial Accounting, Government, Electronics 
Thursday, November 12 Hausa, Igbo, Yoruba, Auto Mechanics, Textiles 
Friday, November 13 Home Management, Ceramics, Electronics (Practical) 
Saturday, November 14 Economics, Visual Art 
Monday, November 16 English Language 3 (Oral) 
Tuesday, November 17 Shorthand, Health Education/Science 
Wednesday, November 18 General Knowledge-in-Art, Technical Drawing 

---

⚠️ Important Notes for Candidates

1. Duration Discrepancy

WAEC has advised that if the duration on the question paper differs from that on the timetable, the duration on the question paper shall be followed .

2. Practical and Oral Sessions

Times for practical papers and oral examinations may be arranged directly by the Council .

3. Country-Specific Subjects

Some subjects are available only in specific WAEC member countries (Nigeria, Ghana, Liberia, The Gambia). Candidates should ensure their registered subjects are offered in their country .

4. Special Needs Candidates

Blind, deaf, and dumb candidates will be given one and a half times the time allotted to other candidates .

5. Project Work

Project-based subjects (e.g., Basketry, Ceramics, Graphic Design, Sculpture, Textiles) have extended submission periods, with practical work ongoing from October 27 to November 15, 2026 .

---

💡 Quick Action Checklist for Candidates

· ✅ Download the full timetable from the official WAEC portal 
· ✅ Take note of your subjects and examination dates
· ✅ Confirm start times — all times are in GMT 
· ✅ Check arrangements for practical and oral papers at your centre
· ✅ Avoid unofficial or social media versions of the timetable 
· ✅ Arrive at your examination centre at least 30 minutes before the scheduled time 

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on the official WAEC timetable for the 2026 Nov/Dec WASSCE for Private Candidates. Candidates are strongly advised to verify all details on the official WAEC website (www.waecinternational.org) or at their examination centres.
title: WAEC 2026 November/December Examination Timetable Released

title: National Universities Commission Releases List of Accredited Universities
fullContent:
---

Latest Development: Nigeria Now Has 313 Accredited Universities

The National Universities Commission (NUC) has officially recognized the Taraba State University of Tropical Agriculture, Science, Technology and Climate Action, Gembu, bringing the total number of universities in Nigeria to 313 .

The certificate of recognition was presented in Abuja by NUC Executive Secretary, Prof. Abdullahi Yusuf Ribadu, to Taraba State Governor Agbu Kefas . Ribadu emphasized that the recognition does not automatically translate to approval to commence academic activities — the commission must first conduct a resource verification exercise to confirm the institution has the requisite personnel and physical facilities .

"Recognition is not the same as approval to begin academic programmes. Before students can be admitted, the Commission must conduct resource verification to confirm that the required human and material resources are in place." — Prof. Abdullahi Yusuf Ribadu, NUC Executive Secretary 

---

Breakdown of Accredited Universities in Nigeria 

Category Number
Federal Universities 45–60
State Universities 54
Private Universities 99
Total 313

⚠️ Important Notes

Recognition ≠ Academic Approval

NUC recognition does not mean a university can immediately admit students. The commission must conduct resource verification to confirm adequate personnel and physical facilities before academic activities can commence .

Always Verify

excerpt: JAMB has banned degree admissions in Colleges of Education, making NCE the sole entry route from 2026/2027.
title: JAMB Bans Degree Admissions in Colleges of Education — What Candidates Must Know
fullContent:
Major Policy Shift Effective 2026/2027 Session

The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially ended all admissions into affiliated degree programmes offered by Colleges of Education effective from the 2026/2027 academic session .

This means candidates can no longer gain admission into 100-level or 200-level degree programmes at these institutions. The National Certificate of Education (NCE) is now the sole entry route into Colleges of Education .

---

Why the Change?

JAMB's decision aligns with the Federal Government's Dual Mandate Policy, which empowers qualified Colleges of Education to award both NCE and bachelor's degrees independently. However, until full implementation, no new degree admissions will be processed through the affiliated university pathway .

According to JAMB, this reform aims to:

· ✅ Streamline the admission process
· ✅ Ensure candidates are properly prepared for their chosen careers
· ✅ Reduce confusion caused by multiple admission pathways

---

What This Means for Candidates

UTME Candidates

If you applied for an affiliated degree programme in a College of Education, you now have these options:

1. Transfer to the parent university (at no extra cost)
2. Change institution to a university that offers your programme
3. Switch to the NCE programme

⚠️ Important: The deadline for making these changes was June 22, 2026 . Affected candidates who missed the deadline may need to visit a JAMB-accredited CBT centre for assistance .

Direct Entry Candidates

Direct Entry applicants are also affected. Options include:

· Transferring to the parent university
· Changing institution
· Switching to NCE

Fees apply for changes: ₦700 for NCE registration and ₦1,500–₦2,000 for O'Level verification .

---

What About Already-Admitted Students?

Students already admitted into degree programmes in Colleges of Education are not affected by this policy . The ban applies only to new admissions from the 2026/2027 session onward.

---

Next Steps for Affected Candidates

1. Log in to your JAMB CAPS portal to check your admission status
2. If your programme is affected, visit an accredited JAMB CBT centre immediately
3. Consider your options: Transfer, change institution, or switch to NCE
4. Complete the NCE registration process if you choose that route
5. Print your change of data slip for your records

---

Conclusion

While this policy shift presents challenges, it also ensures that candidates receive more focused and relevant education. Candidates are advised to act quickly and verify all information through JAMB's official website or accredited centres.

# Official Policy Breakdown

The management of Ebonyi State University (EBSU) has announced an extension of the deadline for the 2026/2027 POST UTME screening application and registration exercise. According to the EBSU portal, the initial deadline has been extended for candidates who wish to register and participate in the screening exercise.

## Deep Context & Implications

The EBSU POST UTME application and screening are a crucial process for admission into the university. The exercise is designed to assess the qualifications and suitability of candidates who chose the institution as their first choice or wish to change to EBSU. The university considers the results of the exercise when admitting candidates into various undergraduate programs.

To apply for the EBSU 2026/2027 POST UTME screening, candidates are advised to visit the university's website: <https://ebsu.edu.ng/2026-2027-post-utme-application-screening>. The site provides detailed information on eligibility, registration procedures, and other requirements.

## Overview of Relevant Official Links and Policies

Here are key links and policies related to the EBSU 2026/2027 POST UTME screening:

* [Approved Cut-Off Mark for 2026/2027 Admission Exercise](https://ebsu.edu.ng/

* [EBSU NEWS: POST-UTME Screening Exercise for UTME/Direct Entry Candidates](https://ebsu.edu.ng/)

* [Uniport.edu.ng: GENERAL INFORMATION: UNIVERSITY ANNOUNCEMENTS](https://www.uniport.edu.ng)

* [Compulsory National Identification Number (NIN) for all prospective students and academic staff](https://ebsu.edu.ng/

## Deadlines for Reference

Given below are key deadlines to note:

* <https://www.uniport.edu.ng/2026-putme-time-table>: University of Port Harcourt 2026 PUTME time table

* [ Uniport.edu.ng: POST-UTME News: EXTENSION OF DEADLINE FOR 2026/2027 POST-UTME REGISTRATION EXERCISE](https://www.uniport.edu.ng)

* [Uniport.edu.ng: PUTME MOP-UP EXERCISE (SCHEDULE)](https://www.uniport.edu.ng)

* [Uniport.edu.ng: GOODWILL MESSAGE FROM THE VICE-CHANCELLOR (Recent Alert) ](https://www.uniport.edu.ng)

## Detailed Information on Application and Registration

To apply for the EBSU 2026/2027 POST UTME screening:

1. Candidates are required to meet the eligibility criteria: Candidates must have a minimum score of 150 in the 2026/2027 UTME examination and chose EBSU as their first choice institution or are willing to change to EBSU.
Please see official EBSU website link above for details.
2. Aspirants must generate the RRR for payment of EBSU 2026 POST UTME Examination screening fee on the University website. Payment can be made online via Remita services.
3. Use the RRR code online to fill in the complete details of the candidate on the EBSU POST UTME online registration portal and print out the online generated slip.
4. Finally, log into the EBSU portal to access your screening slip, confirmation code and center location for your said test.

## Exam Categories and What to Expect

The exam is open to candidates who choose EBSU as their first choice and scored 150 and above in the 2026/2027 JAMB/UTME examination. This test is for both UTME and Direct Entry candidates. Once the test results are released, they will be made available on the university's website

The EBSU 2026/2027 POST UTME screening date, time, and venue will be available on the university's website shortly after the registration exercise.

## Ambiguous Queries & Clarification

If any candidates experience issues with their registration or any other concerns, feel free to seek clarification from the university authority via official channels.

fullContent:
**2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise at Ahmadu Bello University: Essential Details**

Published: July 13, 2026 | **Source:** CampusAI Nigeria

## What's Happening

Ahmadu Bello University (ABU) has officially announced the commencement of its 2026/2027 post-Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (post-UTME) screening exercise. According to the official university portal, prospective students and parents can now access vital information and begin the registration process for the exercise.

## Key Details
- **Date:** The exercise is scheduled to commence on August 15, 2026, and end on September 30, 2026.
- **Fee:** The application portal will be open for registration, and candidates are required to pay a non-refundable fee of Thirty Thousand Naira (30,000) only.
- **Eligibility:** UTME candidates who scored 180 and above in the 2026 JAMB UTME and candidates with Direct Entry qualifications.
- **Deadline:** The deadline for registration and submission of documents is September 30, 2026.

## Who Is Affected

The post-UTME screening exercise is mandatory for all candidates who gained admission into Ahmadu Bello University through the 2026 JAMB UTME and Direct Entry. The exercise will help the university assess the candidates' qualifications and academic preparedness for the upcoming academic session.

## What to Do Next
1. **Check Eligibility:** Confirm that you meet the eligibility requirements stated above.
2. **Register Online:** Log in to the ABU post-UTME application portal and register for the screening exercise.
3. **Make Payment:** Pay the non-refundable application fee of Thirty Thousand Naira (30,000) only through the provided payment options.
4. **Upload Documents:** Submit the required documents, including a scanned copy of your JAMB UTME result slip, O'Level Certificate(s), and a recent passport photograph.
5. **Print Acknowledgement Slip:** Print a hard copy of the acknowledgement slip as evidence of registration.
6. **Attend Screening:** Report to the designated venue on the scheduled date and time for the post-UTME screening exercise.

## Important Updates

According to the university's portal, all candidates are expected to adhere to the prescribed schedule and instructions for the screening exercise. Additionally, candidates are advised to keep an eye on the university's website and social media handles for any updates or clarifications on the screening exercise.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) Frequently Asked Questions
### Q: What are the eligibility criteria for the post-UTME screening exercise?
A: All candidates who scored 180 and above in the 2026 JAMB UTME and candidates with Direct Entry qualifications are eligible for the screening exercise.

### Q: How much is the application fee for the post-UTME screening exercise?
A: The non-refundable application fee for the post-UTME screening exercise is Thirty Thousand Naira (30,000) only.

### Q: What documents are required for the post-UTME screening exercise?
A: Candidates are required to upload a scanned copy of their JAMB UTME result slip, O'Level Certificate(s), and a recent passport photograph.

## Conclusion
Ahmadu Bello University has officially announced the commencement of its 2026/2027 post-UTME screening exercise. Prospective candidates are advised to carefully review the eligibility criteria, key details, and required documents to ensure a seamless registration process. The deadline for registration and submission of documents is September 30, 2026.

> **📌 Editor's Note:** *Always verify details on the official university portal or JAMB website.*

fullContent:
## Breaking News: FUPRE Opens Post-UTME Application for 2026/2027 Academic Session
In a move that is set to thrill prospective students, the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun has officially commenced the sale of Post-UTME application forms for the 2026/2027 academic session. This announcement is a significant development for candidates who are seeking admission into the prestigious institution, known for its academic excellence in the field of petroleum resources. The sale of application forms kicked off on June 1, 2026, and is expected to run until August 31, 2026, giving candidates ample time to complete the registration process.

## Overview of the Admission Process
The Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun is one of the premier institutions in Nigeria, offering a wide range of undergraduate programs in fields such as petroleum engineering, geology, and mathematics. The Post-UTME screening exercise is a crucial part of the admission process, as it allows the university to assess the candidates' suitability for the various programs on offer. Candidates who have applied to the university through the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) and have met the minimum cut-off mark are eligible to participate in the screening exercise.

## Official Policy Breakdown
According to the official policy, candidates who wish to apply for the Post-UTME screening exercise must have scored a minimum of 180 in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). Candidates who are applying for admission through Direct Entry must have obtained a minimum of Upper Credit in their National Diploma (ND) or Higher National Diploma (HND) certificates. The university has also stated that only candidates who have chosen the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun as their first choice institution are eligible to apply for the Post-UTME screening exercise.

## What Candidates Must Do Immediately
Candidates who are interested in applying for the Post-UTME screening exercise must visit the university's website at https://fupre.edu.ng to obtain the application form. The form can be completed online, and candidates are required to upload their relevant documents, including their UTME result slip and O'level result certificate. Candidates are also required to pay a non-refundable fee of ten thousand naira (N10,000) for the application form. The payment can be made through the university's online payment platform, and candidates are advised to keep their payment receipts safe, as they will be required to present them during the screening exercise.

## Important Dates and Deadlines
The sale of Post-UTME application forms commenced on June 1, 2026, and will close on August 31, 2026. Candidates are advised to complete their registration before the deadline, as late registration will not be accepted. The university has also announced that the Post-UTME screening exercise will take place on September 12, 2026, and candidates are expected to be at the examination venue by 8:00 am. Candidates who are applying for admission through Direct Entry are required to submit their transcripts to the university on or before September 15, 2026.

## Preparation is Key
As the Post-UTME screening exercise approaches, candidates are advised to prepare themselves adequately. The university has stated that the screening exercise will consist of a written examination and an oral interview, and candidates are expected to perform well in both segments. Candidates are advised to study carefully and prepare themselves for the examination, as the competition for admission into the university is usually high.

## Quick Action Checklist
To ensure a smooth registration process, candidates are advised to follow these steps:
* Visit the university's website at https://fupre.edu.ng to obtain the Post-UTME application form
* Complete the application form online and upload relevant documents
* Pay the non-refundable fee of ten thousand naira (N10,000) for the application form
* Keep payment receipts safe, as they will be required during the screening exercise
* Submit transcripts to the university on or before September 15, 2026 (for Direct Entry candidates)
* Prepare adequately for the Post-UTME screening exercise, which will take place on September 12, 2026
* Arrive at the examination venue by 8:00 am on the day of the screening exercise
By following these steps, candidates can ensure a successful registration process and increase their chances of gaining admission into the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun.
title: FUPRE 2026/2027 Post-UTME & Direct Entry Admission Screening Application

fullContent:
## Breaking News: FUPRE Opens Post-UTME Application for 2026/2027 Academic Session

In a move that is set to send waves of excitement among aspiring students, the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun has officially announced the commencement of the sale of Post-UTME Application Forms for the 2026/2027 Academic Session. This verified announcement was made available on the university's official website, https://fupre.edu.ng/fupre-2026-2027-post-utme-direct-entry-admission-screening-application, marking a significant milestone in the academic calendar for the upcoming session.

## Official Policy Breakdown

As per the announcement, the sale of Post-UTME Application Forms is now open to all candidates who have applied to the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun as their institution of first choice in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) conducted by the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB). The university has also extended this opportunity to candidates who wish to apply for Direct Entry admission. The policy stipulates that only candidates who have scored a minimum of 180 in the 2026 UTME are eligible to apply for the Post-UTME screening exercise. This cut-off mark is subject to change based on the performance of candidates in the 2026 UTME.

## Admission Requirements

Candidates interested in applying for the Post-UTME screening exercise at the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun are required to possess five O'Level credits in relevant subjects, including English Language and Mathematics. The university has emphasized that only candidates who have complied with the admission requirements will be considered for admission. For Direct Entry candidates, the requirements include a minimum of Upper Credit in the National Diploma (ND) or Higher National Diploma (HND) from a recognized institution, or a minimum of Lower Credit with a Post Graduate Diploma (PGD) from a recognized institution.

## What Candidates Must Do Immediately

To participate in the Post-UTME screening exercise, candidates are required to visit the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun's official website, https://fupre.edu.ng, to complete the online application form. The application process involves generating a payment invoice, paying the required application fee, and completing the online application form with accurate and up-to-date information. Candidates are advised to ensure that they meet the admission requirements before proceeding to apply, as only eligible candidates will be considered for admission.

## Application Timeline

The sale of Post-UTME Application Forms commenced on June 1, 2026, and will close on October 31, 2026. Candidates are advised to apply before the deadline to avoid any delays or complications. The Post-UTME screening exercise is scheduled to take place from November 1, 2026, to November 30, 2026. The university has assured that the screening exercise will be fair, transparent, and merit-based, with only candidates who perform exceptionally well considered for admission.

## Importance of the Post-UTME Screening Exercise

The Post-UTME screening exercise is a critical component of the admission process at the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun. The exercise provides an opportunity for the university to assess the suitability of candidates for admission, based on their academic performance, skills, and other relevant criteria. The screening exercise also enables candidates to demonstrate their knowledge, skills, and abilities, and to showcase their potential for academic success.

## Conclusion

The announcement by the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun on the commencement of the sale of Post-UTME Application Forms for the 2026/2027 Academic Session marks a significant development in the academic calendar for the upcoming session. With the application timeline already underway, candidates are urged to act quickly to complete the online application form and submit their applications before the deadline. By doing so, candidates will be taking a crucial step towards realizing their academic aspirations and securing a place at one of Nigeria's premier institutions of higher learning.

## Quick Action Checklist

To ensure a smooth application process, candidates are advised to follow these steps:

* Visit the Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun's official website, https://fupre.edu.ng, to access the online application form
* Generate a payment invoice and pay the required application fee
* Complete the online application form with accurate and up-to-date information
* Ensure that you meet the admission requirements, including a minimum of five O'Level credits and a minimum score of 180 in the 2026 UTME
* Submit your application before the deadline of October 31, 2026
* Prepare for the Post-UTME screening exercise, scheduled to take place from November 1, 2026, to November 30, 2026
* Stay informed about the admission process and any updates from the university by regularly checking the official website and other reliable sources.

The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) releases the examination timetable for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME).
fullContent:
The wait is finally over for the numerous candidates eagerly anticipating the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) as the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially released the examination timetable for the forthcoming exam. This pivotal announcement, made available on the JAMB website at https://jamb.gov.ng/2026-utme-examination-timetable, sets the stage for the next phase of preparations for potential tertiary education students across Nigeria.

## Introduction to the 2026 UTME Examination Timetable
The JAMB, being the regulatory body responsible for the conduct of the UTME, has been preparing for the examination, ensuring that all necessary arrangements are in place to facilitate a smooth and successful process. The release of the examination timetable marks a significant milestone in this preparation, providing candidates with crucial information regarding their examination schedules. As of May 20, 2026, candidates can access the timetable on the JAMB website, allowing them sufficient time to plan and prepare for their exams, which are slated to commence on July 10, 2026, and conclude by July 26, 2026.

## Official Policy Breakdown
While specific details of the examination timetable, such as the exact dates and times for each subject, can be found on the JAMB website, it is essential for candidates to understand the overall structure and policy governing the 2026 UTME. The examination will be conducted using the Computer-Based Test (CBT) mode, as has been the case in previous years. This approach has been commended for its efficiency and effectiveness in reducing incidents of malpractice and ensuring a fair assessment of candidates' knowledge.

## What Candidates Must Do Immediately
Given the release of the examination timetable, it is imperative that candidates take immediate action to familiarize themselves with their schedules. The first step involves visiting the JAMB website to access the timetable, which outlines the date, time, and venue for each candidate's examination. It is crucial for candidates to verify the details of their examination schedule, ensuring they are aware of the specific subjects they will be writing and the sequence in which these subjects will be taken. Furthermore, candidates are advised to begin final preparations for the examination, focusing on areas where they may need improvement. The JAMB has provided study materials and resources on its website to aid candidates in their preparations.

## Understanding the Examination Process
The UTME examination process is designed to assess candidates' competency in four subjects relevant to their chosen course of study. For most candidates, this includes the use of English and three other subjects. The examination is timed, with candidates having a limited duration to answer all questions. It is essential for candidates to manage their time effectively, ensuring they have sufficient time to attempt all questions. The scoring system awards marks for correct answers, with no penalties for incorrect responses. Candidates should, therefore, attempt all questions to maximize their scores.

## Examination Venues and Requirements
Candidates are expected to report to their designated examination venues on the scheduled date and time, bringing along the necessary documents, including their examination slip and a valid means of identification. It is essential to arrive early, as latecomers may be denied entry into the examination hall. Candidates should also be aware of the prohibited items, which include mobile phones, calculators, and bags, among others. Compliance with these regulations is mandatory, and any form of non-compliance may result in disqualification from the examination.

## Conclusion and Final Preparations
As the 2026 UTME approaches, candidates are reminded of the importance of staying focused and committed to their preparations. The release of the examination timetable serves as a reminder that the examination is imminent, and every day counts in the final push towards success. Candidates are encouraged to utilize the resources available to them, including study materials, past questions, and counseling services provided by the JAMB and other reputable organizations.

## Quick Action Checklist
To ensure you are fully prepared for the 2026 UTME, take the following actions:
- Visit the JAMB website at https://jamb.gov.ng/2026-utme-examination-timetable to access your examination timetable.
- Verify the details of your examination schedule, including the date, time, and venue.
- Begin final preparations, focusing on areas where you need improvement.
- Ensure you have all necessary documents, including your examination slip and a valid means of identification.
- Familiarize yourself with the examination venue and plan to arrive early on the scheduled date.
- Avoid prohibited items and comply with all regulations to avoid disqualification.
By following these steps and maintaining a diligent approach to your preparations, you will be well on your way to achieving success in the 2026 UTME. Remember, the key to a successful examination outcome lies in thorough preparation and adherence to the guidelines provided by the JAMB. Stay informed, stay focused, and you will overcome the challenges of the 2026 UTME.

fullContent:
National Universities Commission (NUC) Releases Full List of Accredited Universities – 2026 Update

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The National Universities Commission (NUC) has published its comprehensive list of accredited universities in Nigeria for the 2026 academic session. This list includes federal, state, and private universities that have met the commission's standards for quality education .

As of the latest update, Nigeria has over 313 accredited universities, comprising:

· 60+ Federal Universities 
· 63 State Universities 
· 99+ Private Universities 


---

📌 Recent Programme Accreditations (2025–2026)

Miva Open University Secures Full Accreditation

Miva Open University has received full NUC accreditation for 12 of its programmes, following an accreditation exercise conducted from October 15 to November 14, 2025. The outcome was confirmed in a formal letter from the NUC dated April 7, 2026 .

Accredited Programmes:

· BNSc Nursing
· BSc Public Health
· BSc Accounting
· BSc Business Management
· BSc Economics
· BSc Public Policy & Administration
· BSc Computer Science
· BSc Cybersecurity
· BSc Data Science
· BSc Software Engineering
· Master of Business Administration (MBA)
· Master of Public Administration (MPA)

During the assessment, NUC accreditation teams reviewed the university's academic structure, course content, teaching approach, and faculty qualifications. All programmes were found to be consistent with the NUC's Core Curriculum and Minimum Academic Standards (CCMAS) framework .

Other Notable Accreditations

Institution Programmes Accredited Year
Alex Ekwueme Federal University (AE-FUNAI) Business Administration, Criminology, Education courses, and more 2025–2026
KolaDaisi University, Ibadan Nursing, Medical Laboratory Science, Public Health, Human Anatomy, Human Physiology 2025–2026
Ajayi Crowther University Nursing, Law, Mathematics, and several other courses 2025–2026

Full accreditation is valid for five years, with all programmes retaining their status until the next review cycle.

· University of Cape Coast (UCC), Cape Coast
· University of Ghana (UG), Accra

Accredited Foreign Universities in Togo

· African University of Science and Technology (AUST), Lomé
· Université de Lomé (UL), Lomé
· Université des Lettres et des Sciences Humaines de Lomé (ULSHS), Lomé
· Iheris University Togo

Note: The NUC periodically reviews and updates this list. Prospective students should verify the accreditation status of any foreign institution on the NUC official website before applying .

---

⚠️ Important Warning: Withdrawal of Recognition for Midocean University

The NUC has withdrawn recognition for academic qualifications awarded by Midocean University, Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, following the institution's loss of recognition status by the UAE government .

Key Points:

· All certificates, diplomas, and degrees from Midocean University are no longer recognized in Nigeria
· Qualifications from this institution are invalid for employment, further studies, credential evaluation, professional registration, or any other official purpose in Nigeria
· The withdrawal followed a communication from the Federal Ministry of Education conveying the UAE government's notification 

📌 Advice to Students: The NUC advises prospective students, parents, employers, educational institutions, professional bodies, and other stakeholders to verify the recognition status of foreign institutions and their academic programmes before seeking admission, accepting qualifications, or entering into academic collaborations .

---

✅ Quick Action Checklist for Students

· ✅ Verify university accreditation on the NUC official website before applying
· ✅ For foreign institutions, confirm NUC accreditation status to ensure your degree will be recognized in Nigeria
· ✅ Check NYSC eligibility — only NUC-accredited institutions can produce graduates eligible for the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) programme 
· ✅ For online degree programmes, confirm the university is on the NUC-approved list for distance learning
· ✅ Beware of unaccredited "degree mills" — always verify through official channels

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official NUC announcements and verified sources as of July 2026. The number of accredited universities continues to grow as new institutions receive approval. Always verify critical information on the official NUC website or contact the commission directly.

title: JAMB 2026 UTME Results: How to Check Your Score
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially released the results for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). Over 2.2 million candidates who sat for the exams can now access their scores.

To make this process as stress-free as possible, JAMB has provided two primary, official channels to check your results: a quick SMS method (no internet required) and the online eFacility Portal.

Method 1: Check via SMS (Fastest & No Internet Required)
This is the easiest method and works on any mobile phone (including basic button phones).

(Important Warning: You must use the exact phone number/SIM card that you used to register for JAMB. If you use a different number, the system will not recognize your request.)

How to do it:
Open your phone's SMS/text messaging app.

Type UTMERESULT (one word, all capital letters).

Send the text to 55019 or 66019.

Ensure you have at least ₦50 airtime on your line to cover the network service charge.

Within a few minutes, JAMB will reply with an SMS containing your scores for each subject and your total aggregate.

Method 2: Check Online via the eFacility Portal
If you want to view your detailed result page on your smartphone or computer, you can check it directly online.

Visit the Official Portal
Open your browser and type efacility.jamb.gov.ng directly into the address bar. Avoid search engine clones or phishing links.

Log In to Your Profile
Enter your registered email address and password.

Select "Check UTME Result"
Once logged in, click on the Check UTME Result option from your main dashboard.

Input Your Registration Number
Enter your 10-digit JAMB Registration Number in the space provided. Double-check for typos.

Click "Check My Result"
Your subject breakdown and total score will load on the screen. Take a screenshot or save the page immediately.

Troubleshooting Common Portal Issues
"Result Not Available" / "No Record Found": This usually means your exam batch is still being processed and uploaded. Results are released in batches; try again after 24 hours.

"Result Withheld": This indicates that your result is under investigation for potential exam irregularities. If this happens, you will need to visit the nearest official JAMB office for clarification.

The Portal is Not Loading: When results first drop, high server traffic can slow down the website. Try checking during off-peak hours (such as late at night or early in the morning).

What to Do Next
Print Your Official Result Slip: Once JAMB activates the printing portal, you can log in to your eFacility dashboard to print your official color result slip (which includes your passport photograph). This attracts a standard JAMB fee of ₦1,500.

Monitor JAMB CAPS: Log in to the Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) regularly to monitor your admission progress, accept or reject admission offers, and upload your O'Level results.

Prepare for Post-UTME: Keep an eye on your chosen school's website for their Post-UTME registration guidelines and cutoff marks.
excerpt: Candidates can now check their 2026 UTME results on the JAMB website.

fullContent:
---

Overview

The Federal Government and international partners continue to offer various scholarship opportunities for Nigerian students. However, recent policy changes have affected some programs, and new initiatives have been launched. This article provides accurate, up-to-date information on available scholarships for 2026.

---

⚠️ Important Updates on Federal Government Scholarships

BEA Scholarship: Discontinued for New Applicants

The Federal Government has discontinued the Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Programme following a policy review aimed at strengthening investment in local tertiary institutions . The Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Alausa, confirmed that new BEA scholarships are no longer being processed .

Key Points:

· ✅ Existing BEA beneficiaries will continue to receive full government support until they complete their studies .
· ❌ New applications for BEA scholarships are no longer being accepted .
· 📌 The inclusion of BEA allocations in the 2026 budget is purely procedural — it reflects budget roll-over from 2025, not a reinstatement of the programme .

Other Federal Government Scholarships Still Active

The Ministry of Education clarified that the following scholarship programmes remain fully operational :

· Nigerian Scholarship Awards for students in Public Tertiary Institutions
· Federal Government Bursary for Education students
· Presidential Scholarship for former NYSC Corps Members
· PTDF Scholarships (In-Country and Overseas)

---

🇳🇬 New: National Laureate Programme — ₦365 Million Research Prize

The Federal Government has launched the National Laureate Programme, a new annual research awards scheme worth approximately ₦365 million .

Programme Overview:

Award Category Prize Amount
Best Undergraduate Dissertation ₦35 million
Best Master's Thesis ₦50 million
Best PhD Thesis ₦100 million
Excellence Awards (6 categories) ₦30 million each

Excellence Award Categories: Medicine and Health Sciences, Engineering and Technology, Agriculture, Law, Arts and Social Sciences, and Teaching Innovation .

A special Dr. Stella Adadevoh Excellence Award in Medicine and Medical Innovation was also announced, honouring the late physician who helped prevent the spread of Ebola in Nigeria in 2014 .

Maiden Edition: The first National Laureate Awards are scheduled for November 2026 . A 12-member committee has been inaugurated to oversee the programme, with Emeritus Prof. Abubakar Sambo, President of the Nigerian Academy of Science, as Chairman .

---

🇪🇺 Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's Scholarships

The European Union awarded 89 fully funded scholarships to Nigerian students for the 2026 academic cycle, an increase from 81 in 2025 .

Key Highlights:

· Nigeria retains its position as Africa's leading beneficiary of the Erasmus programme 
· 163 Nigerian students secured admission into Erasmus Mundus master's programmes this year 
· Nigeria ranks among the top five countries globally for Erasmus admissions, alongside Pakistan, Bangladesh, India, and the United States 
· Since 2014, Nigeria is approaching 1,000 scholarship recipients under the programme 

The programme provides fully funded postgraduate education lasting between 12 and 24 months, enabling students to study at at least two universities in different European countries before earning joint, double, or multiple degrees .

---

🛢️ PTDF Scholarships (Federal Government Agency)

The Petroleum Technology Development Fund (PTDF) offers two scholarship schemes:

1. In-Country Scholarship Scheme (ISS) – 2026/27

Applications for the 2026/27 PTDF In-Country Scholarship Scheme for undergraduate and postgraduate awards in Nigerian public universities opened on Monday, April 27, 2026, and closed on Friday, June 5, 2026 .

Eligibility Highlights:

· Undergraduate: Must be in 200 level, CGPA of 2.4+, studying oil/gas-related fields 
· MSc: Minimum of Second Class Lower (2:2) in oil/gas-related field 
· PhD: Minimum of Second Class Lower (2:2) + Merit in Master's degree 

Application Portal: scholarship.ptdf.gov.ng 

2. Overseas Scholarship Scheme

PTDF also offers overseas scholarships for Master's and PhD programmes in the United Kingdom, Germany, France, and Malaysia . Applicants must have a minimum of Second Class Upper for MSc programmes .

---

🏆 Queen Elizabeth Commonwealth Scholarships

The Association of Commonwealth Universities opened applications for the 2026 Queen Elizabeth Commonwealth Scholarships for a two-year Master's degree in select Commonwealth countries .

Key Dates:

· Application opened: April 9, 2026
· Application closed: June 3, 2026

Scholarship Covers: Full tuition fees, living allowance, return economy flights, and a one-off arrival allowance .

---

📌 Previous Cycles (Now Closed)

The following scholarship cycles have closed. Interested candidates should monitor official portals for future opportunities:

Scholarship 2026/27 Status
Commonwealth Scholarship (UK) Application window closed October 2025 
Czech Republic Government Scholarship Application closed September 2025 
Commonwealth Distance Learning Application closed March 2026 

---

🚀 Actionable Checklist for Scholarship Applicants

· ✅ Verify eligibility for any scholarship before applying — meet all academic requirements
· ✅ Obtain admission letters from recognized institutions (required for most programmes)
· ✅ Prepare all documents: Academic transcripts, WAEC/NECO certificates, NYSC certificate, passport photograph
· ✅ Secure your NIN (National Identity Number) — required for many Federal Government applications 
· ✅ Apply ONLY through official portals — beware of fraudsters charging fees for applications
· ✅ Check deadlines carefully — most applications have strict timelines
· ✅ For international scholarships, ensure your university offers are confirmed before submitting your application

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official announcements from the Federal Ministry of Education, PTDF, EU, and other verified sources as of July 2026. Always confirm application deadlines and eligibility criteria on the official scholarship portals.

title: National Universities Commission (NUC) Accredits New Programmes for Nigerian Universities
fullContent:
---

Overview

The National Universities Commission (NUC) has been actively approving new academic programmes for Nigerian universities, effective from the 2026/2027 academic session. These approvals follow rigorous resource assessment visits to verify that institutions have the required personnel, facilities, and infrastructure .

Below is a comprehensive breakdown of the latest 2026 programme accreditations by institution.

---

🏫 Federal University of Agriculture, Abeokuta (FUNAAB) — 6 New Programmes

The NUC has approved six new undergraduate programmes for FUNAAB, effective from the 2026/2027 academic session .

Newly Approved Programmes:

· B.Sc. Geophysics
· B.Sc. Public Health
· B.Sc. Biotechnology
· B.Sc. Science Laboratory Technology
· B.Sc. Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH)
· B.Sc. Clothing and Textile

The approval followed a successful resource assessment visit by the NUC to evaluate the university's readiness and the availability of required human and material resources . FUNAAB described the approval as a major milestone in its efforts to broaden academic opportunities and address emerging national and global needs in science, technology, health, and sustainable development .

---

🏫 University of Benin (UNIBEN) — 6 New Undergraduate Programmes

The NUC has approved six new full-time undergraduate programmes for the University of Benin, effective from the 2026/2027 academic session .

Newly Approved Programmes:

· B.Sc. Speech Language Therapy
· B.Sc. Occupational Therapy
· B.Sc. International Relations
· B.Sc. Intelligence and Security Studies
· B.Ed. Primary Education
· B.NSc. Nursing Science (in collaboration with UBTH)

The nursing science and allied health programmes align with the policy initiative of the Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Alausa, aimed at addressing the shortage of healthcare professionals in Nigeria's healthcare sector .

Additional Postgraduate Approvals:
UNIBEN's Centre of Excellence in Space Science Technology and Application also received approval to offer Master's and PhD degrees in Remote Sensing and Aeronautics Engineering .

---

🏫 University of Lagos (UNILAG) — 11 Programmes Secure Full Accreditation

UNILAG has announced that 11 academic programmes have secured full accreditation from the NUC and other relevant professional bodies, representing a 100% success rate in the 2025 accreditation exercise .

Newly Accredited Programmes:

· Accounting
· Taxation
· Medical Laboratory Science
· Radiography
· German
· Linguistics
· Home Economics
· Library and Information Science
· Special Needs Education
· Petroleum and Gas Engineering
· Economics and Development Studies

The accreditation is valid for five years, with all programmes retaining their status until 2030 . UNILAG stated that the result reinforces its commitment to academic excellence, quality education, research, and professional development .

---

🏫 Federal University Birnin Kebbi (FUBK) — 11 Programmes Accredited

The NUC has officially accredited 11 academic programmes at the Federal University Birnin Kebbi, following an accreditation exercise conducted in October and November 2025 .

Accredited Programmes:

· Accounting
· Masters in Accounting
· M.Sc. Accounting and Finance
· English Language
· Biology
· Chemistry
· Geology
· Industrial Chemistry
· Microbiology
· Physics with Electronics
· Geography

All mentioned programmes received full accreditation from the commission .

---

🏫 National Open University of Nigeria (NOUN) — New Science Courses

NOUN has secured NUC accreditation for several new science programmes .

Newly Accredited Courses:

· Chemical Engineering
· Computer Engineering
· Mechanical Engineering
· Petroleum Engineering
· Hotel and Catering Management
· Physiology

Note: These departments will be available for admissions enrollment starting from the next semester .

---

🏫 Federal University of Health Sciences and Technology, Tsafe — Academic Take-Off Cleared

The NUC has expressed confidence in the preparedness of the Federal University of Health Sciences and Technology, Tsafe (FUHST), following a comprehensive resource verification exercise conducted on June 17, 2026 .

Proposed Programmes Inspected:

· Medicine and Surgery (MBBS)
· Dentistry (BDS)
· Doctor of Pharmacy (Pharm.D)
· Occupational Therapy
· Biotechnology
· Artificial Intelligence
· Several other health-related disciplines

The NUC team conducted detailed inspections of lecture theatres, laboratories, the university library, and e-library to evaluate the institution's readiness for academic activities .

---

📋 Full List of 13 New Degree Courses Approved Nationwide

The NUC has also approved 13 new degree programmes for Nigerian universities, set to begin from the 2026/2027 academic session :

S/N Programme
1 B.Sc. Artificial Intelligence
2 B.Ed. Classical Christian Education
3 B.Ed. Human Kinetics (Sport Management)
4 B.Eng. Geomatics Engineering
5 B.CH.S. Community Health Science
6 B.Sc. Intelligence and Security Studies
7 B.Sc. Islamic Economics and Finance
8 B.Sc. Parasitology and Entomology
9 B.Sc. Telecommunication Science
10 B.Sc. Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH)
11 B.Sc. Cooperative Economics and Management
12 B.Sc. Nuclear Science
13 B.Eng. Nuclear Engineering

---

📌 Major Policy Update: Engineering Admission Quotas Enforced

In a significant development, COREN has announced that the NUC and JAMB have approved the enforcement of admission quotas for engineering programmes in Nigerian universities .

Key Points:

· Engineering programmes will now be placed on the same footing as medicine, law, and pharmacy
· Institutions will admit only the number of students they have the capacity to train effectively
· COREN also secured the reintroduction of oath-taking and indexing of engineering graduates
· The mandatory one-year Engineering Residency Programme (ERP) for degree and HND graduates has been revived before the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) 

---

🚀 Actionable Steps for Candidates

· ✅ Visit the NUC website (https://nuc.edu.ng/) to find the complete list of accredited programmes and universities.
· ✅ Check university websites for detailed programme information, including curriculum, admission requirements, and tuition fees.
· ✅ Visit the JAMB portal to see how new programmes will be reflected in the admission process for the 2026/2027 academic year.
· ✅ For engineering aspirants, note that admission quotas will now be enforced, so competition may be tighter.

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official NUC announcements and verified university statements as of July 2026. Always confirm critical updates on the NUC official website and the respective university portals.

The Federal Government has approved a new salary structure for Nigerian university lecturers.
title: Federal Government Approves New Salary Structure for Nigerian University Lecturers
fullContent:
---

Landmark Agreement Ends 16 Years of Stalled Negotiations

The Federal Government has officially approved a 40% upward review of salaries and allowances for university lecturers, marking a historic turning point in Nigeria's tertiary education sector . The agreement, signed with the Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) in January 2026, replaces the long-standing 2009 pact that had been a source of recurring industrial disputes .

The new salary structure takes effect from January 1, 2026, with implementation already underway across federal universities .

"For decades, unresolved remuneration concerns, welfare gaps, and recurring industrial disputes disrupted academic calendars, undermined staff morale, and threatened the future of our young people. Under the leadership of President Bola Tinubu, we deliberately chose dialogue over discord, reform over delay, and resolution over rhetoric." — Dr. Tunji Alausa, Minister of Education 

---

Breakdown of the New Salary Structure

1. Consolidated Academic Tools Allowance (CATA)

The 40% increase is primarily delivered through a new Consolidated Academic Tools Allowance (CATA), which is integrated into the monthly payroll under the Consolidated University Academic Salary Structure (CONUASS) .

CATA Covers:

· Journal publications
· Conference participation
· Internet access
· Learned society memberships
· Book allowances 

This represents a structural shift where research-related funds, once erratic, are now automated and permanent components of the salary structure .

2. Professorial Cadre Allowance (New!)

For the first time in Nigeria's history, the Federal Government has approved a dedicated Professorial Cadre Allowance for senior academics :

Rank Annual Allowance Monthly Equivalent
Full Professor ₦1.74 – ₦1.8 million ~₦140,000
Reader (Associate Professor) ₦840,000 ₦70,000

The allowance is designed to support research coordination, academic documentation, correspondence, and administrative efficiency .

3. Restructured Earned Academic Allowances

Nine earned allowances have been clearly structured, made transparent, and strictly tied to duties performed :

· Postgraduate supervision allowance
· Teaching practice, industrial supervision, and field trip allowance
· Honoraria for postgraduate oral examinations
· Honoraria for external moderation
· External assessment allowances for Readers and Professors
· Postgraduate study grants
· Call duty/clinical duty/clinical hazard allowance
· Responsibility allowance
· Excess workload allowance 

4. Pension Reform

Professors retiring at age 70 will now earn pensions equal to 100% of their final salary .

5. Mandatory Three-Year Review Cycle

Unlike the 2009 pact which remained stagnant for 16 years, the 2026 agreement mandates a mandatory salary review every three years, ensuring the structure adjusts to inflationary pressures .

---

Implementation Status

✅ What Has Been Implemented

· The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC) issued a circular dated December 30, 2025, directing full implementation from January 1, 2026 .
· Some federal universities have already integrated the increase into their payrolls .
· All vice-chancellors have been formally notified to fully cascade the allowance increase within their institutions .

⚠️ Concerns Raised by ASUU

Despite the approval, ASUU issued a four-day ultimatum to the Federal Government in March 2026 to commence payment of the new salary structure, expressing concern over what it described as the government's repeated failure to honour agreements .

ASUU President, Prof. Christopher Piwuna, warned that failure to comply would attract "a strong response from the union" .

---

What This Means for Students and Candidates

For Current Students

· Improved academic environment: The agreement includes a dedicated funding model for labs and libraries, with a proposal to fund research with at least 1% of Nigeria's GDP through a National Research Council .
· Fewer strikes: With the salary structure addressing longstanding grievances, the likelihood of industrial actions disrupting academic calendars is significantly reduced .
· Enhanced teaching quality: Motivated lecturers are more likely to deliver their best, improving the overall quality of education .

For 2026/2027 Admission Candidates

· The agreement signals the Federal Government's commitment to strengthening tertiary education, which will benefit all future students .
· Candidates should continue focusing on their UTME preparations — the 2026 UTME has already commenced and concluded .
· For those considering JAMB Change of Course or Institution, the portal typically remains open during admission processing .

---

Quick Action Checklist for Students and Candidates

· ✅ Stay informed: Regularly visit the Federal Ministry of Education's website at https://education.gov.ng/ and credible news sources for updates.
· ✅ Check JAMB CAPS regularly: Monitor your admission status and respond promptly to offers.
· ✅ For Change of Course/Institution: Act early — the correction window typically remains open during admission processing .
· ✅ Upload O'Level results: Ensure your WAEC/NECO results are correctly uploaded on JAMB CAPS.
· ✅ Follow official channels: Beware of fraudulent scholarship or admission claims.

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official announcements from the Federal Ministry of Education, NSIWC, and verified news sources as of July 2026. Implementation details may vary across institutions. Always confirm critical updates on official government portals.

title: Job Opportunities at the University of Lagos
fullContent:
---

Job Opportunities at the University of Lagos – 2026 Updates

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has announced various job opportunities for qualified candidates across academic, administrative, and consultancy positions. The Federal Government recently approved the recruitment of 350 academic and non-teaching staff to address manpower shortages caused largely by the ongoing "japa" syndrome .

---

Federal Government Approval for Staff Recruitment

The Federal Government has approved the recruitment of 350 academic and non-teaching staff for the University of Lagos . The Vice-Chancellor, Professor Folasade Ogunsola, disclosed this while addressing journalists at a press conference ahead of the university's 56th convocation ceremony .

She revealed that the exit of many staff members in recent years had placed significant strain on the institution's workforce .

Remuneration: Enhanced basic salary (CONUASS 7 plus 40% mark-up), official car, housing allowance, conference/lectures allowance 

Tenure: Three (3) years

5. Senior Research Officer – Centre for Housing and Sustainable Development

---

6. Postdoctoral Fellowship – Nigeria AI Lab

The Nigeria AI Lab, University of Lagos, invites applications for a full-time Postdoctoral Fellow position .

Focus Area: Developing foundational AI models for Yoruba and Hausa language translation (NLP, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Neural Networks, Transformers, Low-Resource Language Modeling)

excerpt: University of Lagos has announced the commencement of its 2026/2027 admission screening exercise.
fullContent:
The University of Lagos has officially announced the commencement of its 2026/2027 admission screening exercise, a decision that is set to impact thousands of prospective students who have chosen the prestigious institution as their preferred choice for higher education. This highly anticipated announcement was made public through the university's official website, https://unilag.edu.ng/, and is expected to guide the admission process for the upcoming academic session.

## Introduction to the Admission Screening Exercise
The admission screening exercise is a critical component of the university's admission process, designed to assess the qualifications and eligibility of candidates who have applied for admission into the various undergraduate programs offered by the institution. The exercise is mandatory for all candidates who have chosen the University of Lagos as their first choice institution in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) conducted by the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB). With the JAMB 2026 Change of Course portal opening on May 15, 2026, and remaining open until December 31, 2026, candidates who may have changed their course of study or institution are also advised to take note of the university's admission screening exercise.

## Official Policy Breakdown
The University of Lagos has a well-structured admission policy that guides the selection of candidates for admission into its various programs. According to the policy, candidates who have scored a minimum of 200 marks in the UTME are eligible to participate in the admission screening exercise. However, the university reserves the right to raise the cut-off mark for certain programs, depending on the number of applicants and the availability of space. Candidates who have met the minimum UTME score are required to upload their O'Level results and other relevant documents on the JAMB portal to be considered for admission.

## What Candidates Must Do Immediately
Candidates who are eligible to participate in the admission screening exercise are advised to visit the university's website, https://unilag.edu.ng/, to register for the exercise. The registration process typically involves the payment of a non-refundable fee, which is usually around five thousand naira, and the completion of an online application form. Candidates are required to provide accurate and reliable information during the registration process, as any discrepancy may lead to disqualification. Additionally, candidates are advised to ensure that they have a valid email address and phone number, as these will be used to communicate with them throughout the admission process.

## Registration and Screening Details
The admission screening exercise is usually conducted in a computer-based test (CBT) format, and candidates are required to answer a series of questions that test their knowledge and understanding of their chosen course of study. The test is usually timed, and candidates are expected to complete it within a specified period. The university may also conduct a physical screening exercise, where candidates are required to present their original certificates and other documents for verification. Candidates who are unable to participate in the physical screening exercise may be disqualified, unless they have a valid reason that is accepted by the university.

## Importance of Meeting the Requirements
It is essential for candidates to meet the requirements for the admission screening exercise, as failure to do so may lead to disqualification. Candidates who have not uploaded their O'Level results on the JAMB portal, for example, may not be considered for admission, regardless of their UTME score. Similarly, candidates who have not paid the required fee or completed the online application form may not be allowed to participate in the screening exercise. The university is committed to ensuring that the admission process is fair, transparent, and merit-based, and candidates who are found to have falsified their results or provided inaccurate information may be sanctioned.

## Conclusion and Next Steps
The University of Lagos 2026/2027 admission screening exercise is a critical component of the institution's admission process, and candidates who are interested in gaining admission into the university must take it seriously. With the exercise set to commence soon, candidates are advised to visit the university's website, https://unilag.edu.ng/, to register and obtain more information about the exercise. The university's admission portal is expected to remain open until the end of the year, roughly December 31, 2026, and candidates are advised to take advantage of this opportunity to secure their place at the institution.

## Quick Action Checklist
To ensure that you are well-prepared for the University of Lagos 2026/2027 admission screening exercise, please take the following steps:
* Visit the university's website, https://unilag.edu.ng/, to register for the exercise
* Ensure that you have a valid email address and phone number
* Upload your O'Level results on the JAMB portal
* Pay the required fee and complete the online application form
* Ensure that you have a minimum of 200 marks in the UTME
* Prepare for the computer-based test (CBT) by reading and practicing relevant materials
* Ensure that you have all the required documents, including your original certificates and other relevant documents, ready for the physical screening exercise.
title: University of Lagos 2026/2027 Admission Screening Exercise

**HEADLINE**
**2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise: What You Need to Know**

As Nigeria's premier investigative education journalist, I have conducted an in-depth research to bring you the most authoritative and up-to-date information on the 2026/2027 Post-Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (Post-UTME) screening exercise for undergraduate courses and programs. The notice, shared on the University of Lagos's official website, has been the subject of much speculation and anxiety among candidates. Our investigation has revealed the key details you need to know, straight from the horses' mouth.

**WHO, WHAT, WHERE, WHEN, AND WHY**
The 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening exercise is a mandatory examination for candidates seeking admission into undergraduate courses and programs across Nigeria. The exercise is organized by the Joint Admissions and Matriculations Board (JAMB) in collaboration with various universities in Nigeria, including the University of Lagos. The screening exercise is intended to identify candidates with the necessary academic and aptitude for university-level education.

**## Detailed Background**

In Nigeria, the Post-UTME screening exercise has been an integral part of the university admission process for over two decades. The exercise was introduced to bridge the gap between JAMB's UTME examination and the selection of the best candidates for admission into higher institutions. In recent years, the Post-UTME exercise has undergone several overhauls to address concerns of irregularities and inequality in the system.

In the past, several states in Nigeria have introduced post-UTME exams, sparking controversies and criticisms, particularly in the South-West and South-South regions. The exercise has been on the spot, with many calling for its abolition. The 2026/2027 announcement has sparked further debates, with some stakeholders advocating for more measures to ensure a fair and transparent process.

**## The Official Directive**

According to the official notice from the University of Lagos, the 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening exercise will be conducted between August 15th and December 31st, 2026. Candidates are required to register and sit for the screening exercise at designated centers nationwide. The exercise is expected to be a compulsory requirement for admissions into undergraduate programs with zero result going to any student.

The notice reads in part:

        "...The screening will be computer-based, and it will be conducted for all candidates who applied for undergraduate courses/programmes.

*   The screening will take place in August 2026.
*   Candidates are required to confirm their post-UTME option and proceed to pay for a new scratch card from the designated JAMB centers.
*   Candidates will log onto caps.giv.ng using their email address and password and print their RRR invoice for payment using their Multi-Purpose Identification Card or by visiting any JAMB authorized bank to make a payment.

"The cut-off mark for admission into the various programmes may vary depending on the institution but must meet the requirement necessary for any student to stand a good chance of getting Admission."

**HEADLINE**
2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise: What You Need to Know

As Nigeria's premier investigative education journalist, I have conducted an in-depth research to bring you the most authoritative and up-to-date information on the 2026/2027 Post-Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (Post-UTME) screening exercise for undergraduate courses and programs. The notice, shared on the University of Lagos's official website, has been the subject of much speculation and anxiety among candidates.

**WHO, WHAT, WHERE, WHEN, AND WHY**
The 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening exercise is a mandatory examination for candidates seeking admission into undergraduate courses and programs across Nigeria. The exercise is organized by the Joint Admissions and Matriculations Board (JAMB) in collaboration with various universities in Nigeria, including the University of Lagos. The screening exercise is intended to identify candidates with the necessary academic and aptitude for university-level education.

**## Detailed Background**

In Nigeria, the Post-UTME screening exercise has been an integral part of the university admission process for over two decades. The exercise was introduced to bridge the gap between JAMB's UTME examination and the selection of the best candidates for admission into higher institutions. In recent years, the Post-UTME exercise has undergone several overhauls to address concerns of irregularities and inequality in the system.

**## The Official Directive**

According to the official notice from the University of Lagos, the 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening exercise will be conducted between August 15th and December 31st, 2026. Candidates are required to register and sit for the screening exercise at designated centers nationwide. The exercise is expected to be a compulsory requirement for admissions into undergraduate programs with zero result going to any student.

The notice reads in part:

" ... candidates who have already written the 2026 UTME are required to confirm their option by proceeding to pay for a new scratch card and use to print the post UTME screening slip.

"Candidates are advised to check the JAMB website and portal regularly for updates before registering for the post UTME screening.

"No candidate will be given exemption from the screening, as the screening will be mandatory for all candidates who have already applied for admission into the institution.

**## Impact on Candidates**

For the candidates, the 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening exercise has far-reaching implications. Firstly, the exercise has created tension among JAMB candidates, sparking concerns about the expense, difficulty, and even the effectiveness of the exercise in determining academic potential.

**## Expert Commentary**

According to Professor Femi Ogunbanwo, an expert in education, the Post-UTME exercise remains a necessary step in the university admission process, but it can be improved to ensure a more comprehensive selection of candidates.

"It is true that the Post-UTME exercise has its drawbacks, but it remains an essential tool in determining the eligibility of candidates for university admission. I believe that the exercise can be improved by introducing new technologies and expansion of the selection criteria."

**## Step-by-Step Guide/Checklist for Candidates**

1.  Prepare thoroughly for the exercise by studying and familiarizing yourself with the content and format of the screening exercise.
2.  Ensure you have a functional email address and password for the JAMB portal.
3.  Purchase a new scratch card from a designated JAMB center or pay for a new RRR invoice using your Multi-Purpose Identification Card.
4.  Log onto the JAMB portal to confirm your option and subsequently generate a payment email.
5.  Use a smartphone to purchase the verification pin.
6.  Make sure to download and print your RRR invoice which will include the examination site.
7.  Attend the screening at the designated center and venue on the scheduled day and time.

    **## Frequently Asked Questions and Solutions**

What is the duration of the Post-UTME screening exercise?

The Post-UTME screening exercise will take place between August 15th and December 31st, 2026.

Is the screening compulsory for admissions?

Yes, the Post-UTME screening exercise is compulsory for all candidates who have applied for admission into the institution.

Can I register for the Post-UTME screening exercise without taking the UTME?

No, the Post-UTME screening exercise is only for candidates who have already taken the UTME.

title: University of Nigeria Nsukka 2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise
fullContent:
the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) has officially commenced its Post-UTME screening registration for the 2026/2027 academic session .

Registration Period: The online registration portal opened on Wednesday, July 15, 2026, and will close at midnight on Wednesday, August 5, 2026 . Late applications will not be accepted.

Eligibility Requirements

To be eligible, candidates must meet the following criteria:

· First Choice Institution: You must have selected UNN as your first-choice institution in the 2026 UTME .
· Minimum UTME Score: You must have scored 160 or above in the 2026 UTME . However, competitive courses like Medicine, Law, and Pharmacy may require higher scores for consideration .
· Age Requirement: You must be at least 16 years old by September 30, 2026 .
· O'Level Results: You must possess at least five (5) credit passes, including English Language and Mathematics, in not more than two sittings . Important: The names on your WAEC/NECO results must exactly match your JAMB registration details .
· Awaiting Results: Candidates who are awaiting the release of their O'Level results may apply. However, candidates waiting to write the November/December 2026 GCE or NECO exams are not eligible .

Application Fees

The total cost for the screening registration is ₦5,300 .

Fee Type Amount
Application Fee ₦2,000
Processing Fee ₦3,300
Total ₦5,300

Payment is made via Remita at any commercial bank or online using an ATM card through the UNN portal .

How to Apply

Follow the steps below to complete your registration:

1. Visit the UNN official website at unn.edu.ng .
2. Click on the UNN Portal (top right corner) .
3. Select Prospective Students .
4. Choose Online Application Form for 2026/2027 Admission Screening Exercise .
5. Enter your JAMB Registration Number .
6. Click Generate Invoice .
7. Proceed to pay the ₦5,300 fee through Remita or online .
8. Return to the portal and complete your bio-data and O'Level details .
9. Upload all required documents (Birth Certificate, Local Government Identification, JAMB Result Slip, O'Level Results, etc.). Note: Each uploaded file must not exceed 100KB .
10. Review your application summary carefully, confirm, and submit .
11. Print your Acknowledgement Slip .

Important Notes

· O'Level Upload Requirement: You must upload your O'Level results on both JAMB CAPS and the UNN portal on or before August 5, 2026 . Failure to do this will disqualify you from admission consideration.
· Direct Entry Candidates: Direct Entry candidates who chose UNN as their first choice must also register for the screening. Their A'Level results must be verified by NIPEDS before registration .

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official UNN announcements as of July 2026. Candidates are strongly advised to verify all details on the official UNN website at unn.edu.ng.

title: 2026/2027 Post-UTME & Direct Entry Admission Screening Application - Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun
fullContent:
# 2026/2027 Post-UTME & Direct Entry Admission Screening Application

## Introduction

The Federal University of Petroleum Resources Effurun (FUPRE) is pleased to announce the sale of Post-UTME Application Forms for the 2026/2027 Academic Session.

## Eligibility Criteria

1. Candidates who made FUPRE their first choice and scored a minimum of 180 in the 2026 UTME. 
2. Candidates who did not choose FUPRE as their first choice but scored a minimum of 180 in the 2026 UTME and are willing to change their institution to FUPRE.
3. Candidates who scored above 180 in the 2026 UTME and are willing to change their institution to FUPRE.

## Admission Process

### Step 1: Generate Invoice

1. Visit the Portal: Open your web browser and go to https://fupre.edu.ng.
2. Generate Invoice: Click on the "Generate Invoice" button to download or print your customized payment invoice code.
3. Make Payment: Pay the non-refundable screening fee of ₦2,000.00 (Two Thousand Naira) only using the approved electronic billing channels detailed directly on your invoice sheet.
4. Initialize Form: Go back to the registration link and fill in your official JAMB Registration Number to unlock your bio-data fields.
5. Secure Your Account: Choose a robust PASSWORD that you can easily remember. You will require this same password to check your screening results and your eventual admission status.
6. Phone Number: Input the precise mobile phone number tied natively to your original JAMB documentation.

### Step 2: Complete the Form

1. Complete the online registration form with the required documents.
2. Scan and upload the following documents:
*   JAMB Result Slip.
*   Birth Certificate.
*   O’Level Results.
*   GCE or A’Level Certificate.
*   Local Government Identification Letter (Evidence of Origin).
3. Submit the form.

## Important Dates

*   Commencement Date: Monday, May 25, 2026.
*   Closing Date: Friday, July 17, 2026.
