title: ASUU Reaffirms Commitment to Uninterrupted Academic Calendar in Nigerian Public Universities
FG Warns ASUU, 'No Work No Pay' Still In Force
fullContent:
**Federal Government Accuses ASUU of Sabotage, Insists on 'No Work, No Pay' Policy**

In a stern warning to the Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU), the Federal Government has maintained that its "no work, no pay" policy will remain in force in the face of the recent two-week warning strike called by the union. The declaration came as a response to ASUU's decision to embark on the strike, citing unresolved issues with the government on key matters.

## Official Policy Breakdown
According to sources, the federal government reiterated its established stance that all employees of the federal government are entitled to work for the salaries they earn. This policy has been a hallmark of the government's stance in similar disputes with public sector workers' unions. Officials cited existing labor laws that require workers to dedicate hours worked to earn their compensation, as the basis of this stance.

## Consequences of Non-Compliance
Those who have now gone on strike will not receive payment for the period they were absent from work, as the 'no work, no pay' policy remains unchanged. While students, especially in universities, face a probable extension of the holiday season due to industrial actions, it remains unclear how this may affect entire academic years on a grand scale.

## Rationale Behind the Decision
On how ASUU has reacted to this stance, officials did say that while they continue to engage with the union on contentious matters such as salaries, the ruling government remains stringent on worker obligations to earn a salary. On numerous occasions, the government has deployed the no-work-no-pay rule in dealing with unions in the academia, arguing that it is necessary for maintaining order and stability across operations in federal institutions.

## What Candidates Must Do Immediately
While the strike may change the plans of students, candidates who intend to take JAMB this year should gear up. Plan effectively by:

- Keeping abreast of the strike through relevant authorities and periodic updates via your institutions
- Consulting with teachers for updates on how the strike may affect educational activities
- Using the time to effectively contribute to self-directed study ideas that would aid your existing progress 
- Begin contemplating specific self-renewal specially examining alternatives set forth a plan affecting interests in furtherance

title: Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Awards 2025/2026
fullContent:
**HEADLINE:** Nigerian Government Announces Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Awards for the 2025/2026 Academic Session

**LEAD:** The Federal Government of Nigeria has opened the portal for the Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Awards 2025/2026, offering financial assistance to eligible Nigerian students to study in several countries, including Russia, United States, China, Mexico, and Morocco. The scholarship scheme aims to provide opportunities for undergraduate and postgraduate studies in various disciplines for the next academic session. 

## Detailed Background:

The Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Awards have been in existence for decades, designed to promote cultural exchange and education between Nigeria and partner countries. The scholarship program is a testament to Nigeria's commitment to empowering its citizens through education, fostering global relationships, and utilizing resources to achieve this goal. The program has undergone changes over the years, with the current initiative focusing on supporting students in undergraduate and postgraduate degree programs. Nigeria's Ministry of Education, partnering with various institutions around the world, offers these scholarships to deserving students every year, underlining its commitment to nurturing a highly skilled and globally competitive workforce.

## The Official Directive:

According to the Nigerian Government's latest announcement, details have been made available on the JAMB portal (jamb.gov.ng), encompassing guidelines on eligibility criteria, applicable fields of study, and both online and physical means of submission. The portal has been activated for applications, offering a window for potential candidates to take advantage of this opportunity. The scheme covers various disciplines, including sciences, arts, and humanities, allowing students from diverse backgrounds the chance to benefit. Interest in this year's edition of the BEA Scholarship Awards has been high, with a surge in visits to the JAMB portal and inquiries about the opportunities. 

## Impact on Candidates:

The Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarship Awards have a profound impact on aspiring students, as the financial burden of pursuing higher education in foreign institutions is significantly alleviated. For many, it signifies a pathway to achieving their academic dreams, some of which could potentially be life-changing. The opportunity also includes the life-experience of studying and living in a foreign environment, broadening cultural perspectives and building skills globally competitive. There is no doubt the impact of the BEA scholarships extends beyond academic development, reflecting the broadened horizons afforded to its recipients post-graduation.

## Expert Commentary:

Professor Obioma Abali, an Education Expert at Ahmadu Bello University Zaria commended the Nigerian government for keeping up with the tradition of supporting academic pursuits that empower local talent, acknowledging the positiveness of cultural exchange on quality learning outcomes. Speaking further, he highlighted the role these awards have played in shaping foreign policy and reinforcing Nigeria's diplomatic ties with host nations.

## Step-by-Step Guide/Checklist:

To ensure applicants fully grasp the requirements and procedures, we've outlined necessary steps to undertake:

1.  **Eligibility**:
    *   Visit the JAMB portal (jamb.gov.ng).
    *   Browse the sections on Bilateral Education Agreement (BEA) Scholarships.
    *   Carefully review the eligibility requirements.
2.  **Choosing Your Host Country**:
    *   Research the various destinations (Russia, USA, China, Mexico, Morocco) for further information on the scholarship opportunities and specific requirements for each.
    *   Make an informed decision based on personal goals, interests, and future career aspirations.
3.  **Applying on the JAMB Portal**:
    *   Create an account on the JAMB portal, if you don't already have one.
    *   Ensure all required documents and information are accurately filled.
    *   Submit your application electronically through the portal.
4.  **Welcoming Applications**
    *   The scholarship application portal is open, but deadlines for submission can be critical. Verify specific details from the jamb.gov.ng.
    *   Ensure the documents you submit are authentic, signed, and attached to the e-application, as requested.
5.  **Intermittent Offers**
    *   Keep an eye on any updates on your application status.
    *   Confirm if you have been shortlisted for screening or expansion of your profile details.
6.  **Following Through**
    *   Use the details you have, particularly referred to the specific application dates for both filling-out and interviewing stages.
    
## Frequently Asked Questions:

-   Q: What is the focus of this year's Bilateral Education Agreement Scholarship Awards?
A: It emphasizes supporting candidates opting for undergraduate and postgraduate studies across various disciplines.

-   Q: Which countries are eligible for this scholarship program?
A: Applications will be accepted for opportunities in partner countries such as Russia, United States, China, Mexico, and Morocco.

-   Q: What is the url to apply for the scholarship?
A: It is accessible through the Jamb portal.

-   Q: What would you like me to do if I encounter errors on the site while completing my scholarship application?
A: Keep refilling the e-application with careful steps until everything meets the required, bear in mind your profile might also have issues open complaints through reaching out to relevant institutions.

excerpt: BusinessDay reports on the official opening of the JAMB change of institution portal to accommodate students seeking adjustments.
title: BusinessDay Report: JAMB Opens Portal for Change of Institution and Course
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially thrown open its portal for candidates wishing to modify their choice of institution and course for the 2026/27 academic session. The decision is aimed at providing flexibility for students whose performance in the UTME may necessitate a shift in strategy to ensure they remain within the eligibility bracket for their desired academic programs. 

According to reports gathered by BusinessDay, the portal opening is a standard procedure following the release of results, allowing students to align their preferences with their actual UTME scores. This process is particularly vital for candidates who realize that their original first-choice institutions may have set cut-off marks above their attained scores. By changing institutions, students can pivot to universities where their scores fall comfortably within the competitive range. 

Industry experts advise that this period is the most critical time for informed decision-making. Before proceeding with any change, candidates should review the official guidelines provided by the Board. The cost of the change of institution is standardized, and candidates are warned against using third-party agents who may manipulate the process. Ensure that you have all necessary documents, including your registration number and valid email, before starting the application. Candidates should also look out for the 'Admissions Requirements' sections on the websites of the universities they are targeting to ensure they have the correct subject combination. With the portal officially live, the scramble for admission is entering a high-intensity phase where timely action is rewarded.

With the 2026 UTME now concluded and results rolling out, candidates are entering a critical phase of their educational journey. Experts recommend that all students begin by carefully reviewing their performance and checking their eligibility for their chosen courses. The immediate priority should be the confirmation of O'Level results in their JAMB profile, as admission cannot be processed without the successful uploading of WAEC or NECO results. Students who are yet to upload their results must do so at an accredited CBT center as soon as possible.

Furthermore, this is the time for candidates to start preparing for institutional screening. Many universities and polytechnics have already begun the early stages of their post-UTME planning. Candidates should be diligent in monitoring the official portals of their chosen schools for announcements regarding screening registration dates and documentation requirements. Being organized, ensuring all records are accurate, and staying informed through official channels are the best strategies to ensure a smooth admission process. Avoid falling prey to scammers promising 'admission slots' for a fee, as the entire system is digitized and merit-based.
title: Crucial Steps for 2026 UTME Candidates Post-Examination
excerpt: Experts advise on essential administrative steps for students to take following the conclusion of their UTME exams.

title: Educational Reform: JAMB Exempts Agriculture and Education Courses from UTME Threshold
fullContent:
In a strategic move to encourage enrollment in critical sectors of the Nigerian economy, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has announced a significant policy shift. Candidates seeking admission into programs in Education and Agriculture (non-engineering courses) are now being exempted from the standard UTME scoring thresholds that apply to other courses. This policy is designed to revitalize the teaching profession and promote sustainable agricultural practices by incentivizing students to pursue these career paths.

The policy was adopted during the 2026 Policy Meeting led by the Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa. By lowering the entry barriers for these essential fields, the board hopes to increase the workforce in rural development and national education. Interested applicants are advised to visit the JAMB portal to review the specific courses under this incentive program. This reform is part of a broader government effort to align higher education outcomes with national labor requirements, ensuring that the next generation of graduates is equipped to address the country's most pressing socio-economic challenges.
excerpt: To boost national development, JAMB has introduced special incentives for candidates applying for Education and Agriculture.
JAMB has mandated that candidates accept admission offers within four weeks or risk disqualification.
title: Four-Week Admission Window: JAMB Issues Warning to 2026 Candidates
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has instituted a strict four-week acceptance window for all 2026/2027 admission offers. This policy is aimed at minimizing administrative bottlenecks and ensuring that admission spaces do not go to waste. According to official directives, any candidate who fails to accept their admission offer within the stipulated four-week period risks losing the slot entirely. Such instances may lead to the candidate being marked as having 'refused' the offer, which can negatively impact their eligibility for future admission rounds.

This measure is part of the board’s ongoing commitment to transparency and efficiency in the Nigerian education sector. Universities are empowered to request the deletion of such slots to accommodate other qualified candidates who are on waiting lists. JAMB advises all applicants to keep their registered email addresses and phone numbers active and regularly check the Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) portal to monitor their status. Being proactive is essential, as the university admission process is time-sensitive and highly competitive, with little room for delayed responses once an offer has been generated by an institution.

tags: FG Unveils CBT plans for WAEC and NECO by 2026
title: Full Transition To CBT for WAEC and NECO By 2026 Unveiled By FG: Guidelines Released
fullContent:
# Full Transition To CBT for WAEC and NECO By 2026 Unveiled By FG: Guidelines Released

**Published:** July 18, 2026 | **Source:** CampusAI Nigeria

## What's Happening

The Federal Government has made a significant announcement regarding the education sector in Nigeria. The government has unveiled a plan to fully transition West Africa Examination Council (WAEC) and National Examination Council (NECO) to Computer-Based Testing (CBT) by the year 2026. This move is aimed at improving the testing process using modern technology.

## Key Details

- **Date / Registration Timeline:** 2026 pecifies the following timelines:
  • Registration period is from Monday, January 19th, 2026, to Monday, May 25th, 2026.
  • Late registration period is from Tuesday, May 26th, 2026, to Friday, May 29th, 2026.
  • The website will be closed at 11:59 pm on Friday, May 29th, 2026.
  • The last date for submission of the return of the printed RRR Payment Receipt by the exam centers would be on Friday, May 29th, 2026.
- **Fee:** Though not specified in the released guidelines, candidates should check the official NECO portal for the latest information.
- **Eligibility & Requirements:** The National Examination Council's (NECO) BJECRS requires that candidates meet certain requirements before they can be allowed to participate in the CBT:
  • Candidates are required to have a valid physical disability diploma (when necessary).
  • In case of any exceptional candidate who requires taking any exam outside of Nigeria, such candidates will be informed on time, and that will be announced on the NECO portal.
- **Deadline:** Although it's not an application deadline, candidates should register on or before Friday, May 29th, 2026.

## Who Is Affected

The transition to CBT affects all students participating in WAEC and NECO exams nationwide. This includes students preparing to participate in the 2026 edition of these exams.

## What to Do Next

1.  Confirm the details on the NECO portal, neco.gov.ng.
2.  Get familiar with the CBT process and tips.
3.  Ensure you have the appropriate documents and Means of identification (e.g., National ID Card).
4.  Register on or before the deadline and proceed with the payment of the exam fee.
excerpt: Nigeria's Federal Government (FG) has announced the full transition to Computer-Based Testing (CBT) for both West Africa Examination Council (WAEC) and National Examination Council (NECO) by 2026. The development is part of the government's effort to improve education using technology.

fullContent:
## Introduction to Post-UTME Screening
The University of Lagos (UNILAG) is set to commence its Post-UTME Screening Exercise for the 2026/2027 academic session. 

## Eligibility Criteria
Candidates who made University of Lagos their first choice in the 2026/2027 UTME and scored 200 and above are eligible to apply for Post-UTME Online Screening. In addition, candidates must possess five (5) credit passes at one sitting in relevant O/level subjects, including English Language and Mathematics.

## Registration Procedure
Eligible candidates should log on to University of Lagos application portal via: applications.unilag.edu.ng and then, take the following steps:
* Mock Examinations will hold from Monday, June 22 – Sunday, July 26, 2026.
* Post-UTME On-line Aptitude test will hold from Monday, July 27 – Friday, July 31, 2026.

## Official Action Steps for Candidates
1. **Visit the UNILAG Website**: Access the University of Lagos website at unilag.edu.ng
2. **Click on Post-UTME Registration**: Navigate to the Post-UTME registration portal
3. **Fill in Required Information**: Provide all necessary details as required
4. **Submit and Print Acknowledgement Slip**: After submission, print out your acknowledgement slip

## Screening Fee and Payment
The screening fee is N2,000. Candidates are advised to make payments using the approved payment channels.

Key takeaways from the 2026 Policy Meeting on admissions into tertiary institutions.
title: JAMB 2026 Policy Meeting Highlights: What Students Need to Know
fullContent:
The annual policy meeting for the 2026 admission exercise has provided clarity on the standards and expectations for the upcoming academic session. Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa, emphasized the government's commitment to ensuring a transparent and merit-based admission process for all candidates. 

Key highlights from the meeting include the enforcement of the 150 cut-off mark for universities, the adoption of new guidelines for the admission of candidates into Education and Agricultural programs, and a reaffirmation of the use of the Central Admission Processing System (CAPS) as the final arbiter for all admissions. The meeting also highlighted the significant scale of the 2026 UTME, which saw over 2.2 million candidates across 989 centres, demonstrating the high demand for tertiary education in Nigeria. The JAMB board urged institutions to adhere strictly to the guidelines regarding the timing of the admission process to prevent the overlap of sessions. For the students, the takeaway is simple: the process is becoming more digitized and centralized. Candidates must take personal responsibility for their profiles, ensuring that every detail, from contact information to result uploads, is accurate. The policy meeting serves as the foundation for the upcoming session, and students who stay informed about these official guidelines are better positioned to navigate the complexities of the Nigerian higher education landscape.

excerpt: Candidates seeking a change of institution or course for the 2026 admission cycle can now access the JAMB portal.
title: JAMB Activates Change of Course and Institution Portal for 2026 Candidates
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has officially activated the UTME Change of Institution and Course portal for the 2026 session. This service is essential for candidates who have reviewed their UTME results and wish to adjust their academic path based on their performance or newly discovered career interests. Candidates are required to visit accredited Computer-Based Test (CBT) centers to process these changes, as the board emphasizes the need for security and verification during the update process.

When applying for a change, candidates must provide accurate details, including their current course of study, the intended new institution, and their original UTME registration number. It is important to note that changes made during this period are final, and students should conduct thorough research on their preferred universities' admission requirements before initiating the process. Furthermore, candidates are advised not to share their private login credentials with unauthorized third parties to avoid fraudulent modifications to their records. This window serves as a final opportunity to align preferences before the official commencement of post-UTME screening exercises nationwide.

title: JAMB Activates Change of Institution Portal for 2026 UTME Candidates
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially announced the activation of the 2026 change of institution process. This development, confirmed by JAMB spokesperson Fabian Benjamin on Friday, May 15, 2026, marks a critical phase for candidates looking to adjust their preferred schools or courses of study following the release of the 2026 UTME results. JAMB strongly advises all applicants to conduct this process through accredited Computer-Based Test (CBT) centres rather than attempting it via personal devices. 

Accredited centres are equipped to handle necessary biometric verifications, such as fingerprint authentication, which ensures that data integrity is maintained. Self-processing via the portal without professional oversight has historically led to errors, which often necessitate additional payments to rectify. As the admission season gains momentum, candidates are encouraged to monitor their Central Admission Processing System (CAPS) profile closely. This portal is the primary medium through which institutions communicate admission decisions. Prospective students should verify their status regularly, as a timely response to an admission offer is essential for securing a spot in the upcoming academic session. The transition period for the 2026/2027 admission cycle is expected to span from June through September, with specific deadlines dictated by the internal policies of individual institutions. Candidates are urged to remain vigilant and ensure their records are updated correctly.

title: JAMB Activates Portal for Change of Institution and Course
fullContent: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially activated the online facility for candidates to change their choice of institution or course for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). According to a statement issued by the board’s spokesperson, Fabian Benjamin, the process is now open to all eligible candidates who wish to adjust their initial preferences. Candidates are strictly advised to visit any JAMB-accredited Computer-Based Test (CBT) centre to carry out these modifications. This measure is part of the board’s efforts to ensure that candidates exercise their choices within the official framework while preventing potential fraudulent activities associated with unauthorized portals. Applicants are required to provide essential details, including their current course or institution, the target course or institution, and their UTME registration information. It is important to note that this service attracts a processing fee, in addition to any service charges mandated by the specific CBT centre. Candidates should approach this process with care, ensuring that all details are accurate before final submission, as frequent changes may complicate the admission verification process later in the year.
excerpt: Candidates seeking to modify their choice of institution or course for the 2026 UTME can now commence the process.
JAMB has summoned 43 institutions to address over 2,600 outstanding verification requests for Direct Entry candidates.
fullContent:
To expedite the 2026 admission process, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has scheduled an urgent meeting with 43 tertiary institutions to address a backlog of 2,638 outstanding verification requests. This verification process is crucial for Direct Entry (DE) applicants who require the validation of their previous academic credentials to proceed. 

Federal Polytechnic Nasarawa leads the list with 222 pending cases, closely followed by Kaduna Polytechnic and Isa Mustapha Agwai I Polytechnic. This bottleneck, noted as of mid-March, has become a priority for the Board as it seeks to ensure that the 2026/2027 session commences on schedule. Direct Entry applicants whose verifications are stalled are advised to contact their institutions to ensure their documentation has been forwarded to JAMB. Failure to resolve these issues could hinder the ability of candidates to be considered for admission on the CAPS platform. The Board is committed to clearing these hurdles before the peak of the admission cycle. Students affected by these delays should maintain constant communication with their institution’s admissions office and track their progress through the JAMB e-Facility dashboard. This proactive approach will help in mitigating the stress associated with admission uncertainty.
title: JAMB Addresses Verification Backlog for Direct Entry Applicants

fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has confirmed that the official printing of the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) result slips will commence on Monday, May 18th, 2026. This date marks the official availability of the documents required for subsequent stages of the admission process, including post-UTME screenings and registration at various higher institutions across the country. 

Candidates are advised to utilize the official JAMB portal to access their results. Printing the slip is a mandatory requirement, as it serves as the primary document for identifying candidates during their interaction with various university admission offices. To print the slip, candidates should log in to their profile on the JAMB website using their email address and password. A nominal fee may be charged for the printing service, which must be paid strictly through the JAMB integrated payment platform. 

As the admission season gains momentum, students are cautioned against patronizing unauthorized cyber cafes that offer to 'upgrade' results. JAMB has reiterated that its database is secure and any claims of score alteration are fraudulent. The result slip contains the candidate's core data, including their scores in the four subject areas, which will be cross-referenced with the O'Level results uploaded on the CAPS portal. Students should ensure that their uploaded O'Level results (WAEC/NECO/NABTEB) are accurate, as discrepancies between the UTME registration data and submitted documents can lead to immediate disqualification from the admission exercise. Always check the official JAMB portal for updates.
title: JAMB Announces May 18th for Commencement of 2026 UTME Result Slip Printing
excerpt: Candidates who sat for the 2026 UTME can begin printing their official result slips from May 18th, 2026.

title: JAMB Announces Official 2026 UTME Cut-Off Marks for Nigerian Tertiary Institutions
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) and key stakeholders in the tertiary education sector have officially pegged the national minimum benchmark for university admissions at 150 for the 2026/2027 academic session. This decision was solidified during the 2026 annual policy meeting held in Abuja, chaired by the Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa.

Under these new guidelines, institutions are permitted to set their individual cut-off marks higher than the national minimum depending on the competitiveness of their courses. While universities have a 150-point benchmark, polytechnics have been set at 100, and colleges of nursing remain at 150. JAMB Registrar emphasized that this policy is designed to streamline the admission process while maintaining academic standards across the board. Candidates who scored above these thresholds are eligible to proceed with their post-UTME screenings, though they must ensure their O'level results are uploaded to the CAPS portal. The board has further warned institutions against admitting students who fall below these benchmarks, noting that such admissions would be deemed null and void.
excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has set the national minimum admissible score for universities at 150.
The process for 2026 is the same as previous years. Go to efacility.jamb.gov.ng, visit an accredited JAMB CBT centre near you to complete the process in person.

title: JAMB Issues Urgent Warning to 2026 Candidates on CAPS Result Upload Status
fullContent:
## JAMB Urgent Directive on CAPS Verification

JAMB Registrar, Prof. Is-haq Oloyede, has directed all 2026 candidates to verify their O'Level result upload status on JAMB CAPS. He emphasized that the board would not tolerate any excuses from candidates whose admissions are delayed due to unuploaded credentials.

### How to Check Upload Status
1. Log in to your JAMB CAPS profile using your registered email and password.
2. Select 'My O'Level' from the menu panel.
3. If your subjects and grades are listed, your upload is complete. If empty, visit an accredited JAMB CBT centre immediately to upload.

title: JAMB Officially Activates Change of Course and Institution Portal for 2026 UTME
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially activated the portal for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) candidates to commence the change of course and institution. This development follows the conclusion of the main examination cycle and serves as a critical window for students who may have realized their initial choices were not aligned with their performance or career goals. 

According to the official announcement, candidates are advised to visit the JAMB Central Admission Processing System (CAPS) or any accredited Computer Based Test (CBT) center to effect these changes. The process is strictly digital to ensure transparency and prevent exploitation by fraudulent agents. Candidates are reminded that the change of course or institution can only be done a limited number of times, and it is imperative to check the requirements of their new target institutions before finalizing the switch. 

For many students, this window is a lifeline. If a candidate’s score falls short of the cut-off mark for their original first-choice university, this change provides an opportunity to pivot to a school with a more favorable admission requirement. Candidates are strongly encouraged to verify the admission policies of their intended institutions, including subject combinations and departmental cut-offs, before proceeding with the portal transaction. Payment for the service must be made through the approved channels on the JAMB portal to avoid losing funds to third-party scams. Ensure all credentials are saved and printed after the process for future verification during the post-UTME screening exercise.
excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has officially opened its portal for UTME candidates to modify their institution and course preferences.
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has announced 150 as the minimum admissible score for universities nationwide.
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB), under the leadership of Minister of Education Tunji Alausa, has officially pegged the minimum admissible UTME score for the 2026/2027 academic session at 150. This decision, reached during the annual policy meeting, sets a national baseline for all federal, state, and private universities in Nigeria. While the board has established this threshold, it is important to note that individual institutions retain the autonomy to set their own higher cut-off marks based on their specific departmental requirements and applicant competition levels.

Candidates are advised that reaching the 150 mark does not guarantee admission. Many top-tier universities, such as the University of Lagos and the University of Ibadan, often require significantly higher scores for competitive courses like Medicine, Law, and Engineering. Furthermore, the board has confirmed that the 16-year age requirement for admission remains in effect for all candidates entering tertiary institutions. Applicants are encouraged to check the specific admission portals of their target universities to understand their post-UTME screening procedures, as meeting the JAMB cut-off is merely the first step in a broader, highly competitive admission process.

JAMB Retains 150 as Minimum Cut-off Mark for 2026/27 Academic Session
fullContent:
Following extensive consultations with heads of tertiary institutions and the Federal Ministry of Education, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has announced that the minimum cut-off mark for the 2026/27 academic session remains 150 for universities. This decision comes as part of the board’s efforts to balance the need for increased access to tertiary education with the preservation of academic standards. 

While 150 is the national minimum, JAMB emphasized that this is a baseline and not a guarantee of admission. Individual universities retain the autonomy to set their own higher cut-off marks based on their internal capacity and the competitiveness of specific programs. For instance, high-demand courses like Medicine, Law, and Engineering typically require significantly higher scores than the national floor. 

In a strategic policy shift, JAMB also announced special considerations for candidates seeking admission into Agriculture and Education programs. By keeping the floor at 150, the government aims to encourage enrollment in these sectors, which are deemed critical for national development. Students are advised to monitor the official websites of their preferred institutions to understand specific departmental requirements. Prospective candidates must recognize that achieving 150 does not automatically secure a spot; they must still excel in the post-UTME screening processes, maintain a strong O'Level profile, and ensure their subject combinations align perfectly with their chosen course of study. The board remains committed to a merit-based, transparent admission process throughout the 2026 cycle.
excerpt: JAMB has pegged the minimum admissible score for Nigerian universities at 150, sparking discussions on academic standards and admission equity.

title: JAMB Sets 150 as Minimum Admission Benchmark for 2026 Academic Session
fullContent: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB), in collaboration with key stakeholders in the Nigerian tertiary education sector, has officially approved 150 as the minimum admission benchmark for universities for the 2026/2027 academic session. This resolution was finalized during the 2026 annual policy meeting on admissions held in Abuja on Monday. This score, recognized as the National Minimum Tolerable UTME Score, serves as the regulatory floor below which no tertiary institution is permitted to admit candidates. Stakeholders emphasized that this benchmark is intended to maintain standard quality control while ensuring that the admission process remains competitive and transparent. Institutions are now expected to conduct their internal Post-UTME screening and admission exercises in strict adherence to this directive. Candidates are advised to monitor their preferred university's specific requirements, as individual institutions reserve the right to set higher cut-off marks based on their internal carrying capacity and departmental standards. The policy reflects a collaborative effort to streamline the admission process and ensure that students are adequately prepared for the rigorous academic requirements of higher education in Nigeria.
excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has officially announced 150 as the minimum cut-off mark for university admissions.

title: JAMB Sets 150 Minimum Admission Benchmark for 2026/2027 Session
fullContent:
Following the 2026 annual policy meeting held in Abuja, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) and key stakeholders in the education sector have officially established 150 as the National Minimum Tolerable UTME Score for universities. This benchmark serves as the minimum threshold for any candidate to be considered for admission into a degree-awarding institution for the 2026/2027 academic session. 

It is important to note that this figure represents a baseline; individual universities maintain the authority to set higher institutional cut-off marks based on their internal standards and the volume of applications they receive. Candidates who fall below this threshold are strictly ineligible for admission into the university system. During the policy meeting, discussions also covered the need for institutions to streamline their post-UTME screening exercises to reduce the financial burden on parents and candidates. As the admission process transitions into the screening phase, institutions are expected to upload their specific cut-off requirements to the JAMB portal. Students are advised to confirm the specific requirements of their schools of choice, as the minimum benchmark of 150 does not automatically guarantee admission. The competitive nature of the Nigerian university system means that higher scores remain an advantage in the quest for limited slots in high-demand courses.

These scores represent the minimum standard agreed upon by the heads of tertiary institutions across the country, aiming to create a uniform regulatory framework for admission.

While these benchmarks provide a baseline, institutions retain the right to set higher cut-off points based on their internal capacity and the volume of applications they receive. Candidates interested in these institutions are urged to contact their target polytechnics or schools of nursing directly to ascertain their specific departmental requirements. As the 2026 admissions season gains momentum, candidates should focus on maintaining high standards in their secondary school credentials, as these institutions often weigh both the UTME score and the candidate's O'Level results heavily in their final assessment for admission.

fullContent:
In a major policy shift aimed at boosting the enrollment of students into critical sectors of the Nigerian economy, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has introduced exemptions for candidates pursuing specific courses in Education and Agriculture. According to announcements made following the 2026 policy meeting, candidates applying for non-engineering courses within these two faculties will be granted significant concessions. This move is part of the federal government's broader effort to incentivize youth participation in farming and teaching, both of which are currently facing a critical human resource deficit.

The policy, detailed by JAMB spokesperson Fabian Benjamin, clarifies that while the standard UTME remains a prerequisite for most degree programs, these specific pathways will see a more flexible approach to admission requirements. Furthermore, the board reiterated that the minimum age for entry into any Nigerian tertiary institution remains 16 years. Candidates who sat for the exam on April 16, 2026, can continue to check their results via the official SMS platform by texting their details to 55019 or 66019. Students interested in these exempted courses are encouraged to verify the specific entry requirements for their target universities, as some institutions may still apply additional internal screening criteria despite the JAMB exemption.

fullContent: During the 2026 Policy Meeting led by the Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) unveiled a new policy initiative aimed at boosting enrollment in critical sectors. Specifically, candidates seeking admission into selected Education programs and non-engineering Agriculture courses are now exempted from the standard UTME examination requirements. This landmark decision is designed to encourage more students to pursue careers in teaching and agricultural sciences, which are vital for national development. The meeting, held on May 11, 2026, also set guidelines for the 2026 admission exercise into tertiary institutions. While the UTME remains the primary gatekeeper for most university courses, this special exemption highlights the government's strategic intent to incentivize enrollment in fields where there is a significant skill gap. Educators have welcomed the move as a proactive measure to address the declining interest in these core sectors. Further details regarding the specific list of exempted courses and the alternative entry modalities are expected to be disseminated by the board and the respective institutions in the coming weeks. Candidates interested in these fields are advised to stay updated via official communication channels.

fullContent:

A comprehensive guide for 2026 UTME candidates on how to correctly navigate the change of institution portal.
title: Official Guide: Navigating the 2026 UTME Change of Course Portal
fullContent:
With the JAMB Change of Course and Institution portal now active for the 2026 session, thousands of students are expected to log in to refine their choices. To avoid common errors that could jeopardize their admission status, it is essential for candidates to follow a structured approach. The portal, which was officially launched this week, provides a dedicated interface for candidates to rectify initial mistakes or adjust based on their UTME outcomes. 

First and foremost, candidates must verify that their proposed university and course combination is valid. Not all universities offer the same courses, and requirements such as specific O'Level subject combinations can vary wildly between institutions. It is recommended that candidates consult the 'JAMB Brochure'—a document that outlines the specific admission requirements for every accredited program in Nigerian tertiary institutions—before making any final adjustments on the portal. 

Furthermore, keep in mind that the process is final once submitted. There is no 'undo' button after the payment and submission cycle is completed. Candidates are advised to perform the following steps: 1) Log in to the JAMB portal with their unique registration ID; 2) Select the 'Correction of Data' or 'Change of Institution/Course' tab; 3) Carefully input the new institution and course codes; 4) Make payment via the integrated Remita platform; 5) Print the new slip reflecting the changes. By following these steps methodically, students can secure their place in the admissions pipeline. Remember that this service is intended for candidates who are serious about their academic future, and due diligence is required to ensure that the new choices align with the student's actual performance and career aspirations.

Without this original document, candidates may face significant challenges during the physical screening at their prospective universities. The Board emphasized that this date aligns with the broader activation of the admission services. Candidates are encouraged to ensure they print their slips from the official JAMB e-Facility portal. It is recommended to use reputable cyber cafes or accredited CBT centres to avoid complications or print quality issues. The document remains a primary requirement during the admission cycle, which typically runs from June to September. Students should store digital and physical copies of their result slips securely, as they will be required during the final matriculation phase once admission offers are accepted via the CAPS platform.

title: Osun State University 2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise
fullContent:
## Introduction to UNIOSUN Post-UTME
The 2026/2027 Admission Exercise will be based on Central Admission Processing System (CAPS), a system designed by Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) to automate the admissions process to tertiary institutions.

## Eligible Candidates
Candidates who chose Osun State University as their first choice or those willing to change their institution and make UNIOSUN their first choice, who have a minimum score of 160 marks are eligible to apply.

## Screening Fee
The screening fee is N2,000.

## Registration Procedure
Candidates are expected to log in to www.uniosun.edu.ng for the registration procedure.

## Official Action Steps for Candidates
* Log on to the Osun State University website
* Fill in the required information
* Submit and verify your email to activate your account
* Navigate to the “Application Fee” section
* Select the Application Type (Post-UTME)
* Generate a Remita Retrieval Reference (RRR) for the application fee
* Make payment online or at bank branches with the RRR
* After successful payment, return to the portal and confirm your payment
* Complete the application form and submit

It is recommended that candidates review the admission capacity of their intended new institution before making the switch. After the conclusion of the 2026 UTME, candidates are guided on the next steps toward securing university admission.
fullContent:
The completion of the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) marks the end of a grueling preparation phase but serves as the beginning of the crucial admission journey. With the examination behind them, candidates are now entering the phase where document accuracy and institutional alignment become paramount. 

The first step for any candidate is to monitor the JAMB CAPS portal. This platform is the nerve center of Nigerian admissions, and it is where institutions upload the names of successful candidates for the first, second, and third admission lists. Candidates should ensure their email addresses and phone numbers are active, as these are the primary channels for institutional communication. 

Secondly, candidates must prioritize their O'Level results. The integration of the UTME score with O'Level grades is a non-negotiable part of the admission matrix in 2026. Candidates are advised to upload their results to the JAMB portal without delay if they have not already done so. Failure to adhere to the deadline for result uploading often leads to an automatic 'No Admission' status. Furthermore, it is time to research the specific Post-UTME requirements for desired universities. Many institutions in 2026 have introduced more rigorous screening criteria, which may include physical testing or specialized interviews. Engaging in proactive planning—such as keeping track of school-specific deadlines, verifying subject requirements for chosen courses, and maintaining a budget for registration fees—is essential. Stay patient during the waiting period, as the admission process is sequential and takes several weeks to fully unfold across the national landscape.
title: Strategies for 2026 UTME Candidates Post-Examination: What Happens Next?

fullContent:
The Nigerian Journal of Sustainability Research has recently published a commentary on the 2026 UTME policies, emphasizing the long-term impacts of the 150-mark cut-off policy on the Nigerian educational ecosystem. As the nation grapples with a high number of university applicants versus limited carrying capacity, the policy serves as a necessary mechanism for channeling talent into sustainable pathways. 

The research highlights that prioritizing courses in Agriculture and Education, as evidenced by the flexible admission policies, is a vital step toward ensuring that the nation's human capital is directed toward critical developmental sectors. By lowering the entry barriers for these specific programs, the government aims to address the shortage of skilled personnel in fields essential to the national economy. This academic approach aims to foster a system where every qualified student has a path, even if it is not necessarily their 'first-choice' traditional course. 

For students, this means that while the competitive spirit is high, there is a systemic effort to encourage academic diversification. When choosing an institution or course, students are encouraged to look beyond the 'prestige' of traditional degrees and consider the viability of these supported programs. Research suggests that students who align their career paths with national needs not only secure admission more easily but also find greater opportunities for scholarships and industrial training. In the 2026 session, sustainability is not just a buzzword; it is a framework for ensuring that the Nigerian higher education sector remains robust, inclusive, and forward-looking despite the challenges of over-subscription and funding.
title: Sustainability in Nigerian Higher Education: Focus on 2026 Admission Policy

Yaba College of Technology (YABATECH) has commenced the sale of Post-UTME Screening Forms for admission into its Full-Time National Diploma (ND) programmes and B.Sc. (Education) programmes (100 and 200 Levels) affiliated with the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) for the 2026/2027 Academic Session. [...] Eligible candidates should follow the steps below: 
1. Visit the College website: www.yabatech.edu.ng 
2. Click on the ‘POST UTME SCREENING 2026/2027’ link. 
3. Complete the online application form as instructed. 
4. Pay the screening fee of ₦2,000.00 (excluding result verification and transaction charges). 
5. Payments can be made online or through designated banking channels. 
Note: The College does not accept cash payments. Important Instructions 
Candidates must provide accurate and complete information during registration. 
Any false declaration or incorrect information may lead to disqualification. 
Names, photographs, O'Level results, UTME records, and all registration documents must be consistent. 
Any discrepancy in a candidate’s name or photograph may result in disqualification. [...] Interested candidates who chose YABATECH in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) are invited to apply through the College portal. The application portal is now open.

---

IMT Enugu 2026/2027 Admission Guide

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The Institute of Management and Technology (IMT), Enugu, has officially commenced the admission process for the 2026/2027 academic session. Applications are now open for prospective students seeking admission into National Diploma (ND) programmes, Higher National Diploma (HND) programmes, and Degree programmes offered in affiliation with the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN).

---

Cut-Off Marks for 2026/2027 Admission

The Institute of Management and Technology, Enugu, has released its official cut-off marks for the 2026/2027 academic session:

Programme Type Minimum JAMB Score
Degree Programmes (in affiliation with UNN) 160
ND Programmes 100

Note: Departmental cut-off marks may be higher depending on the competitiveness of the programme and the performance of candidates in the screening exercise.

---

Eligibility Requirements

For ND and Degree Programmes

To be eligible for admission, candidates must meet the following requirements:

· JAMB Score: Minimum score as specified above for the chosen programme type
· Choice of Institution: IMT Enugu should be your first choice institution (candidates who did not select IMT must effect a change of institution on the JAMB portal)
· O'Level Requirements: Five (5) credit passes in relevant subjects, including English Language and Mathematics, in not more than two sittings (WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB)
· Age: Candidate must be at least 16 years of age
· Awaiting Results: Candidates with awaiting O'Level results are eligible to apply but must upload their results to the JAMB CAPS portal as soon as they are available

For HND Programmes

Candidates seeking admission into Higher National Diploma (HND) programmes must possess a relevant National Diploma (ND) from IMT or another accredited polytechnic in Nigeria, along with the required O'Level credits.

---

Application Process

Follow these steps to complete your application:

Step 1: Visit the Official IMT Portal

· Open your web browser and navigate to the official IMT application portal: portal.imt.edu.ng
· On the homepage, click on "ONLINE PORTAL" and then select "APPLY"

Step 2: Generate Payment Invoice

· An application form page will open. Fill it out carefully with your correct details to generate a prepayment invoice
· Ensure all information, especially your name and JAMB registration number, is accurate

Step 3: Pay the Application Fee

The application fee is ₦2,000 (Two Thousand Naira only) .

You have multiple payment options:

· Bank Branch: Take the generated invoice to any commercial bank in Nigeria and make the payment
· Online Payment (Interswitch): Pay through the Interswitch platform on the portal
· Quickteller: Visit https://www.quickteller.com/IMT, select "Screening Exam Fee" as the payment option, and follow the prompts to pay with your ATM debit card

Step 4: Complete Your Application

· After successful payment, return to the IMT application portal: portal.imt.edu.ng
· Log in with your details to complete the full application form
· Upload all required documents, including your passport photograph, O'Level results, and JAMB result slip
· Review your application thoroughly to ensure all information is correct
· Submit the form and print your Application Form/Acknowledgement Slip

---

Screening Schedule

The screening exercise is a Computer-Based Test (CBT) conducted at the IMT CBT Centre.

Detail Information
Venue CBT Centre, IMT (Campus 3), Enugu
Time 8:00 AM daily
Screening Dates October 28 – 30, 2026 (for Degree programmes in affiliation with UNN)
Application Deadline October 16, 2026

What to bring to the screening venue:

· A printed copy of your IMT Application Form/Acknowledgement Slip
· A copy of your JAMB Result Slip

---

How to Check Admission Status

Once the admission list is released, candidates can check their admission status through the JAMB Central Admission Processing System (CAPS) portal:

1. Visit: efacility.jamb.gov.ng
2. Login with your registered email address and password
3. Click on "Check Admission Status"
4. Click on "Access my CAPS"
5. Click on "Admission Status" to see if you have been offered admission

If you are admitted: Your passport photo will be displayed with a congratulatory message.

Next Steps After Admission:

1. Accept or Reject the admission offer by sending ACCEPT or REJECT as SMS to 55019 or 66019 using the phone number linked to your JAMB profile
2. Admitted candidates must accept the offer within 4 weeks
3. Print your JAMB Admission Letter after accepting the admission for clearance and registration at IMT

Note: If you are using a mobile phone and cannot see the full CAPS page, enable Desktop Mode on your browser.

---

Important Notes for All Applicants

Requirement Details
JAMB CAPS It is mandatory to upload your O'Level results to the JAMB CAPS portal. Admission cannot be processed for candidates whose results are not on CAPS
Accurate Information Providing false information during registration will lead to automatic disqualification
Communication Use a valid email address and phone number. Any updates regarding the screening will be communicated through these channels
Application Deadline October 16, 2026 — late applications will not be accepted
Change of Institution Candidates who did not choose IMT Enugu as their first choice must effect a change of institution to IMT on the JAMB portal before applying
Support Contacts For any challenges, contact: 07016705460, 08167090026, or email: support@imt.edu.ng

---

Quick Action Checklist

· ✅ Visit the official IMT portal at portal.imt.edu.ng
· ✅ Confirm your eligibility — check JAMB score, O'Level requirements, and age
· ✅ Generate payment invoice and pay ₦2,000 application fee
· ✅ Complete the online application form and upload required documents
· ✅ Print your Application Form/Acknowledgement Slip for the screening
· ✅ Prepare for CBT screening — bring your printed form and JAMB Result Slip
· ✅ After screening, check JAMB CAPS for admission status
· ✅ If admitted, accept the offer within 4 weeks via SMS to 55019 or 66019

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official announcements from the Institute of Management and Technology, Enugu, and verified sources as of July 2026. Always confirm critical information on the official IMT portal at portal.imt.edu.ng.

excerpt: The Federal Ministry of Education has urged all universities to adopt distance learning to enhance access to education.
fullContent:
Federal Government Champions Distance Learning to Expand University Access – 2026 Updates

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The Federal Government, through the Ministry of Education and the National Universities Commission (NUC), has reaffirmed its commitment to expanding access to higher education through technology-driven learning . Minister of Education, Dr Tunji Alausa, has emphasized that institutions leveraging technology to deliver learning will be central to addressing access gaps as demand for higher education continues to outstrip capacity .

---

Government's Position on Distance Learning

Speaking at Miva Open University's maiden convocation in Lagos, Minister Alausa stated that reforms under the Nigeria Education Sector Renewal Initiative focus on science education, technical and vocational training, digital transformation, girl-child education, and reducing out-of-school children .

He described Miva Open University as a model for the future of education, saying: "It represents the democratization of knowledge, educational access at scale, flexibility without compromising quality, and most importantly, it represents the future of what education will look like a century from now" .

The Minister also commended the National Open University of Nigeria (NOUN) for its innovative deployment of Moodle, describing NOUN as having the second-largest open and distance learning network in sub-Saharan Africa . He urged African universities to adopt open-source learning platforms and collaborative digital tools to bridge educational gaps and reduce the digital divide between urban and rural learners .

NUC's Position on Open and Distance Learning

Dr Ayoteju Ogun, representing the NUC Executive Secretary, stressed that open and distance learning should no longer be viewed as a secondary option for those unable to access conventional institutions . She added:

"Democratising education does not mean lowering standards, but creating systems capable of expanding opportunity while maintaining excellence" .

Ogun further noted that the NUC expects universities to explore models that combine innovation with academic rigour .

---

📌 Major Developments in Distance Learning (2026)

University of Ibadan Launches UI eCampus

The University of Ibadan (UI), Nigeria's oldest university, has signed a Memorandum of Agreement with Astria Learning to establish the UI eCampus, a fully integrated digital campus .

Key Details:

· Launch Date: The agreement was signed on May 13, 2026 
· First Cohort: Scheduled to begin in August 2026 
· Programmes: Postgraduate diploma and master's programmes in Business Administration, Education, and Information Technology initially 
· Academic Authority: UI retains full academic authority, faculty oversight, and quality assurance. Every programme is the same accredited UI postgraduate degree offered on campus 
· Platform Features: AI-enabled learning management system, 24/7 academic help desk, and Astria Digital Library with over 170,000 academic resources 

The UI eCampus aims to expand postgraduate access for working professionals, grow enrolment capacity without physical expansion, and extend UI's reach to learners across Nigeria, Africa, and the diaspora .

---

UNILAG's Distance Learning Institute Opens 2026/2027 Admissions

The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has announced the opening of applications for admission into its Distance Learning Institute (DLI) for the 2026/2027 academic session .

Available Programmes:

· B.Sc. Accounting, Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Public Administration
· B.Sc. (Ed.) Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics, Economics
· B.Ed. Early Childhood Education, Educational Management, Guidance and Counselling, Business Education
· B.A. (Ed.) English 

Key Requirements:

· No UTME required 
· Five O'Level credits including English and Mathematics (not more than two sittings) 
· Minimum age of 16 years by October 31, 2026 
· Basic ICT skills required 

Fees:

· UNILAG DLI Application: ₦20,000 
· JAMB Registration: ₦3,500 

Deadline: December 31, 2026 

---

Miva Open University Graduates First Cohort

Nigeria's first online private university, Miva Open University, graduated 1,280 students at its maiden convocation ceremony . Minister Alausa, who attended the event, applauded Miva's model as a reflection of the transformation required in modern education .

The university also announced new academic initiatives, including a degree programme in Artificial Intelligence (AI) .

---

Federal Government's Ongoing Commitment

The Federal Government has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening Open and Distance Learning (ODL) through execution of relevant policies aimed at enhancing education delivery across the country . The government recognizes ODL's potential to extend access to quality education, especially in underserved communities, and to support lifelong learning .

---

📝 Quick Action Checklist for Prospective Students

· ✅ Explore UI eCampus programmes — Visit the University of Ibadan official website for postgraduate distance learning programmes starting August 2026
· ✅ Apply to UNILAG DLI — Applications are open until December 31, 2026, with no UTME required 
· ✅ Consider Miva Open University — Nigeria's first online private university offers technology-driven programmes including AI 
· ✅ Verify accreditation status — Ensure the distance learning programme is accredited by the NUC
· ✅ Prepare required documents — O'Level results, transcripts, and other credentials
· ✅ Check technology requirements — Ensure you have the ICT skills and devices needed for online learning

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official announcements from the Federal Ministry of Education, NUC, University of Ibadan, UNILAG, Miva Open University, and NOUN as of July 2026. Always verify critical information on the official university portals.

---

Bells University of Technology Empowers ND Students through Entrepreneurship Excellence Awards

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

Bells University of Technology has continued its commitment to fostering entrepreneurial excellence among students, with recent achievements highlighting the institution's focus on innovation and business development. The university has consistently recognized and rewarded outstanding performance in entrepreneurship, nurturing an entrepreneurial spirit among its students.

---

Recent Entrepreneurship Achievements

Hult Prize Nigeria National Competition 2025

Team Zero Zone from Bells University of Technology emerged as the overall winner at the Hult Prize Nigeria National Competition held at the University of Lagos .

Key Highlights:

· Competition: Hult Prize Nigeria National Competition
· Theme: "Unlimited" — bold, scalable solutions to global challenges through social entrepreneurship
· Prize: $1 million seed fund for the winning team's venture
· Participating Institutions: 46 institutions competed 

Other Top Winners:

· First Runner-up: Team Nurrad from Landmark University
· Finalists: Team Plastilock Hub, Team Plasticonn, and Team Edugaze 

The Hult Prize is a global initiative that challenges young people to address the world's most pressing issues by launching innovative, impact-driven startups aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals .

This victory underscores Bells University's commitment to fostering a culture of entrepreneurship and providing students with the resources and support needed to thrive in the competitive landscape of social innovation .

21st Matriculation Ceremony (January 2026)

On Saturday, January 17, 2026, Bells University held its 21st Matriculation Ceremony for new students. A total of 1,900 students took the matriculation oath .

Key Highlights:

· Vice-Chancellor's Address: Professor Jeremiah O. Ojediran welcomed incoming students, introducing them to the institution's values, expectations, and available resources.
· Warning Against Vices: The VC admonished students to eschew drug abuse, cyberbullying, and physical assault, emphasizing the university's zero-tolerance policy.
· Focus on Excellence: He reiterated BellsTech's resolve to ensure students receive the very best education and acquire necessary skills to become top graduates relevant in society .

---

About Bells University of Technology

Established on July 1, 2005, Bells University of Technology is Nigeria's first private university of technology. The institution has built a reputation for excellence in technological education and innovation .

Global Recognition

In November 2024, Bells University secured the 41st position in the Times Higher Education (THE) Sub-Saharan Africa University Rankings, advancing from its previous 50–60 bracket. Only 44 universities in Nigeria met the stringent criteria for inclusion .

THE Ranking Evaluation Pillars:

· Access and fairness
· Africa impact
· Teaching skills
· Student engagement
· Resources and finances 

NUC Approval for 11 New Programmes

The National Universities Commission (NUC) has recently approved new programmes at Bells University, including Software Engineering, Cyber Security, Data Science, and Biomedical Engineering . This approval expands academic opportunities for students in cutting-edge fields.

Tuition-Free Programmes

From the 2024/2025 academic session, several departments at Bells University of Technology began offering tuition-free admission, making quality education accessible to deserving students .

Available Tuition-Free Programmes:

· Bachelor of Agriculture (Agronomy, Agribusiness, Fisheries & Animal Science)
· Bachelor of Engineering (Agricultural and Biosystems Engineering)
· Bachelor of Science (Physics with Electronics, Industrial Chemistry)
· Bachelor of Technology (Biotechnology, Building Technology, Quantity Surveying, Urban & Regional Planning) 

Note: Tuition is completely waived; other components of the fee schedule are payable in installments. Programmes are fully residential .

---

What This Means for ND Students

Entrepreneurship Opportunities

ND students at Bells University have the opportunity to:

· Participate in national and international entrepreneurship competitions
· Develop innovative solutions to global challenges
· Access seed funding for startup ventures
· Network with peers and industry professionals

Academic Support

· State-of-the-art facilities and experienced faculty
· Fully residential campus with on-campus housing
· Bookshop, library, supermarket, and cafeterias
· Health centre and counselling services 

---

Next Steps for Students

1. Visit the official university website: www.bellsuniversity.edu.ng for detailed information on entrepreneurship awards, eligibility criteria, and application procedures.
2. Join university entrepreneurship clubs: Many universities have entrepreneurship clubs where students can interact, share ideas, and develop their entrepreneurial skills.
3. Participate in relevant competitions: Engage in entrepreneurial competitions, hackathons, and events organized by the university or other organizations to demonstrate entrepreneurial potential.
4. Explore tuition-free programmes: Check available programmes and admission requirements.

---

Quick Action Checklist for Prospective Students

· ✅ Visit the official website: www.bellsuniversity.edu.ng
· ✅ Check admission requirements: Ensure you meet O'Level and UTME requirements for your chosen programme
· ✅ Explore entrepreneurship opportunities: Look for clubs, competitions, and mentorship programmes
· ✅ Apply through the admissions portal: https://www.bellsuniversity.edu.ng/admissions/

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official announcements from Bells University of Technology and verified sources as of July 2026. Always verify critical information on the official university portal.

WAEC 2010 May/June SSCE Results: A Historical Review

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The West African Examinations Council (WAEC) released the results of the May/June 2010 West African Senior School Certificate Examination (WASSCE) on August 19, 2010. This article provides a historical review of the examination statistics for reference purposes.

A total of 1,351,557 candidates sat for the examination, consisting of 739,664 male and 611,893 female candidates .

---

Key Statistics

Overall Performance

Statistic Number Percentage
Total candidates 1,351,557 100%
Fully processed results 1,278,843 94.62%
Results being processed 72,714 5.38%
Results withheld (malpractice) 77,168 5.71%

Credit Performance Breakdown

Performance Level Number Percentage
2 credits and above 1,058,806 78.33%
3 credits and above 936,470 68.84%
4 credits and above 806,583 59.67%
5 credits and above 677,007 50.09%
6 credits and above 534,841 39.57%

University Admission Eligibility

Only 337,071 candidates (24.94%) obtained credits in English Language, Mathematics, and at least three other subjects — the minimum requirement for admission into tertiary institutions at that time .

Subject-Specific Performance

Subject Candidates with Credit & Above Percentage
English Language 451,187 33.38%
Mathematics 560,974 41.50%

---

Blind Candidates' Performance

A total of 98 blind candidates sat for the examination. Of these, 13 candidates (13.27%) obtained credits in five subjects, including English Language. Blind candidates were exempted from taking Mathematics and Science practicals in WASSCE diets .

---

Examination Malpractice

The results of 77,168 candidates (5.71%) were withheld for alleged involvement in examination malpractice. The Head of the Nigeria National Office of WAEC at the time, Dr. Iyi Uwadiae, announced that reports on these cases would be compiled for presentation to the Nigerian Examination Committee of the Council .

---

Key Notes

· The results were released four weeks ahead of schedule to enable students to use them for registration purposes in higher institutions that had admitted them
· Candidates' errors and omissions during online registration and examination conduct accounted for the 5.38% of results still being processed at the time
· A total of 1,278,843 candidates (94.62%) had their results fully processed at the time of release

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is a historical review based on official WAEC announcements as of August 2010. It is published for reference and archival purposes.

Senate Urges FG to Increase Funding for Education In Nigeria
fullContent:
---

Senate Urges FG to Increase Education Funding to 15% of National Budget

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The Nigerian Senate has passed a resolution urging the Federal Government to increase the allocation to education from the current 6% to at least 15% of the National Budget. The resolution was passed during the Senate's plenary session on July 3, 2026, following the passage of the ₦58.47 trillion 2026 Appropriation Bill .

The Senate described the current allocation as "grossly inadequate" and insufficient to address the critical challenges facing the education sector .

---

Key Details

Detail Information
Date of Resolution July 3, 2026
Current Education Budget ₦3.52 trillion 
Percentage of Budget ~6.1% 
Senate's Demand At least 15% of National Budget
UNESCO Recommendation 15–20% of national budget 

---

The Reality Check

Current Funding Falls Short

The 2026 budget allocated ₦3.52 trillion to education, representing only about 6.1% of the national budget . This remains far below the UNESCO-recommended benchmark of 15–20% that Nigeria, as a signatory, is expected to meet .

Historical Context (2022–2025):

Year Budget Allocation Percentage
2022 ₦1.18 trillion 7.2%
2023 ₦1.54 trillion 7.0%
2024 ₦2.18 trillion 7.9%
2025 ₦3.52 trillion 7.3%
2026 ₦3.52 trillion 6.1% 

Stakeholders have expressed concern over this trend. Dr. Sylvanus Ugoh, chairman of ASUU at Yakubu Gowon University, Abuja, stated: "There is no way 6% allocated to education can address our educational needs. It shows that the government has not fully committed to tackling the decay of infrastructure in Nigerian universities" .

---

Stakeholder Reactions

Education Minister's Push for 20%

During the budget defence session before the Senate and House Joint Committee on Education, Minister of Education Dr. Tunji Alausa urged the National Assembly to support increased funding, warning of critical challenges .

Key Points from the Minister:

· Teacher Shortage: Federal Unity Colleges are grappling with a deficit of over 3,500 teachers, severely affecting science, mathematics, and technical subjects 
· Infrastructure Crisis: Many schools lack perimeter fencing, reliable electricity, and modern security systems 
· ICT Gap: Low penetration of computers, internet connectivity, and digital learning tools 
· Classroom Overcrowding: Outdated laboratories and inadequate library facilities hamper practical learning 

The Minister emphasized: "We aim to convince the National Assembly that a minimum of 20 per cent allocation to education, as recommended by UNESCO, should serve as the benchmark for future budgets" .

Civil Society Advocacy

Auwal Musa Rafsanjani, Executive Director of the Civil Society Legislative Advocacy Centre (CISLAC), warned: "We are witnessing persistent underfunding of education in Nigeria, which has resulted in poor infrastructure, teacher shortages, weak learning outcomes and rising youth unemployment" .

---

Why 15% Matters

The push for 15% is based on UNESCO's long-standing recommendation that developing nations allocate 15% to 20% of their annual national budget to education . According to experts, achieving this benchmark would:

· ✅ Clear debt owed to teachers
· ✅ Rebuild and equip schools with modern facilities
· ✅ Improve security in schools
· ✅ Attract and retain qualified teachers 
· ✅ Reduce poverty and inequality
· ✅ Boost productivity and create employment 

---

Next Steps

Action Status
Senate Resolution Passed ✅ July 3, 2026
Communicate to Executive Pending
Implementation in 2027 Budget To be determined

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official announcements from the Nigerian Senate, UNESCO recommendations, and verified news sources as of July 2026. The Senate's resolution is a call to action; actual implementation will depend on the 2027 budget process.

 Meanwhile, the NPC has announced that the census will take place between January and February 2026. Effective preparation is currently ongoing to ensure a successful exercise.

title: University of Ibadan 2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise
fullContent:
## Introduction to UI Post-UTME
The University of Ibadan’s admission portal will be opened for post-UTME/ Direct Entry Registration from Monday, 22 June to Sunday, 19 July, 2026.

## Eligible Candidates
Candidates who made University of Ibadan their first choice in the 2026/2027 UTME and scored 200 and above are eligible to apply for Post-UTME Screening Exercise. In addition, candidates must possess five (5) credit passes at one sitting in relevant O/level subjects, including English Language and Mathematics.

## Screening Fee
The screening fee is N2,000.

## Registration Procedure
Candidates are required to log into the Admission Portal (using their Jamb Registration Number (e.g. 202612345678EG) as their user name and their surname as their initial password e.g. OYESANMI, Chibuzor Umar.

## Official Action Steps for Candidates
* Log on to the University of Ibadan application portal
* Fill in the required information
* Submit and verify your email to activate your account
* Navigate to the “Application Fee” section
* Select the Application Type (Post-UTME)
* Generate a Remita Retrieval Reference (RRR) for the application fee
* Make payment online or at bank branches with the RRR
* After successful payment, return to the portal and confirm your payment
* Complete the application form and submit
