Opportunities are open for Nigerian students to apply for prestigious international and local scholarships in 2026.
title: Top Opportunities for Nigerian Students: PTDF and Chevening 2026 Scholarships
fullContent:
As the 2026 academic calendar progresses, several high-value scholarship programs have opened their doors for Nigerian students looking to further their education. Notable among these are the PTDF (Petroleum Technology Development Fund) scholarship and the prestigious Chevening Scholarships.

The PTDF Scholarship is specifically designed for postgraduate studies in Engineering, Sciences, and related fields to build local capacity in Nigeria's oil and gas industry. Interested candidates are encouraged to visit the official PTDF portal to review eligibility requirements and application deadlines.

Meanwhile, the Chevening Scholarships, funded by the UK government, offer fully-funded opportunities for Master's degree candidates. These scholarships are highly competitive and require a rigorous selection process, emphasizing leadership, academic excellence, and community involvement. Students are advised to prepare their personal statements and gather all necessary academic transcripts early. These opportunities represent a vital path for Nigerian scholars to gain global expertise and contribute to national development upon their return.

excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially opened its e-facility portal for candidates participating in the 2026 UTME mop-up examination to print their notification slips starting today, June 6.
title: JAMB Commences Printing of 2026 UTME Mop-Up Examination Notification Slips
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially activated its e-facility portal, permitting candidates scheduled for the 2026 UTME mop-up examination to begin printing their notification slips as of Saturday, June 6, 2026. This development follows the board's earlier announcement that the mop-up examination is set to hold on Saturday, June 13, 2026.

According to the official communication from the board’s spokesperson, Dr. Fabian Benjamin, this exercise is specifically designed for candidates who were unable to participate in the main 2026 UTME due to various technical challenges and those whose results were affected by specific examination infractions currently under investigation. The board emphasized that this session represents the final opportunity for any candidate to sit for the 2026 UTME, urging all affected students to treat this phase with utmost importance.

Candidates are advised to log into the official JAMB e-facility portal using their registration details to access and print their personalized examination slips. The notification slip is a mandatory requirement for entry into the examination hall, as it contains critical information including the assigned examination centre, the exact time of the examination, and other necessary guidelines for the candidates. The board has explicitly warned that there will be no extension for the mop-up examination, and no further opportunities will be provided after the June 13 date.

In addition to the printing exercise, JAMB has reiterated its commitment to the integrity of the examination process. While the board continues to address technical issues and resolve pending result cases, it has advised candidates to familiarize themselves with their designated centres before the examination day to avoid complications. Students are encouraged to monitor the official JAMB portal and verified communication channels for any further updates regarding the conduct of the exercise. Candidates facing difficulty while accessing the portal are advised to reach out to accredited JAMB CBT centres or the official help desk for support during the printing window.

title: JAMB Concludes 2026 UTME Mop-Up Examination, Moves to Admission Phase
fullContent:
## Overview of the 2026 Mop-Up Exercise
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) successfully concluded its 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) mop-up exercise on Saturday, June 13, 2026. This final examination phase was specifically curated for candidates who faced technical hitches, biometric verification failures, or other unforeseen challenges during the main examination window in April 2026. 

JAMB spokesperson, Fabian Benjamin, confirmed that the exercise served as the final opportunity for eligible candidates to secure their scores for the current academic session. The Board maintained a firm stance that no further extensions or supplementary examinations will be provided for the 2026 UTME cycle, urging students to proceed with the next steps of their admission journey.

## Critical Implications for Candidates
With the mop-up examination now concluded, the focus shifts entirely to the admission process. Candidates who participated in the mop-up are advised to monitor the official JAMB portal for the release of their results. 

“The mop-up examination represents the final opportunity for eligible candidates to sit the 2026 UTME. We have ensured that the integrity of the process remains uncompromised, and we urge all candidates to prepare for the subsequent post-UTME requirements of their respective tertiary institutions,” stated Fabian Benjamin in an official communication.

## What Applicants Must Know
As the Board transitions into the admission phase, several key points remain pertinent for all 2026 applicants:

1. Admission Cut-off Marks: The policy meeting held on May 11, 2026, established the minimum cut-off marks at 150 for universities and nursing colleges, and 100 for polytechnics. However, applicants should note that many institutions have set higher internal benchmarks for competitive courses.

2. Result Checking: Candidates are reminded to verify their results via the official channels. JAMB has previously advised that for any withheld results under review, candidates should remain patient as investigations into examination infractions continue.

3. Admission Processing: Institutions will now begin combining UTME scores, O-Level requirements, and institutional screening results to compile their respective admission lists. Candidates are encouraged to ensure their O-Level results are correctly uploaded to the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS).
excerpt: Following the successful conduct of the 2026 UTME mop-up examination on June 13, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has finalized its assessment phase to pave way for the 2026/2027 admission cycle.

Following the successful conduct of the 2026 UTME mop-up examination on June 13, JAMB marks the official end of the UTME cycle, transitioning into the critical admission processing phase for Nigerian tertiary institutions.
fullContent:
## Overview of the Mop-Up Exercise

The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) successfully concluded its 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) mop-up exercise on Saturday, June 13, 2026. This critical intervention was designed specifically for candidates who were unable to participate in the main UTME, which ran from April 23 to April 29, 2026, due to unforeseen technical challenges, biometric verification failures, and other logistical disruptions at designated Computer-Based Test (CBT) centres.

JAMB spokesperson, Fabian Benjamin, reaffirmed that this exercise served as the final opportunity for candidates to secure a score for the 2026/2027 admission cycle. With the examination now concluded, the Board has transitioned its focus toward the comprehensive processing of results and the commencement of the post-UTME screening window.

## Official Directive to Candidates

Registrar of JAMB, Professor Ishaq Oloyede, emphasized the finality of the exercise, stating: “Affected candidates were given a fair opportunity to resolve outstanding issues that prevented their initial participation. With the completion of this mop-up, there will be no further opportunity for any candidate to sit the 2026 UTME. We advise all participants to remain vigilant regarding their result status via the official JAMB portal.”

## Critical Implications for Admissions

The completion of the mop-up examination marks a significant milestone in the Nigerian academic calendar. As of June 15, 2026, the focus shifts to:
1. **Result Finalization:** All mop-up results are being processed to be uploaded to the Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS).
2. **Post-UTME Screenings:** Universities, Polytechnics, and Colleges of Education are now authorized to accelerate their institutional screening procedures.
3. **CAPS Integration:** Institutional admissions are now fully underway, requiring candidates to monitor their admission status strictly through the official jamb.gov.ng portal.

## What Applicants Must Know

Candidates must be wary of third-party platforms claiming to offer ‘result upgrades’ or ‘special access’ to admission lists. The Board maintains that official communication is only transmitted through its verified X (Twitter) handle and official website. All candidates are encouraged to regularly check their email addresses and their JAMB profile dashboards for the latest updates regarding their admission status and departmental cut-off marks.
title: JAMB Concludes 2026 UTME Mop-Up Examination: What Candidates Must Know About Results and Next Steps
JAMB Conducts Final 2026 Mop-Up UTME: No Further Opportunities Ahead
fullContent:
## Final Mop-Up Exercise Commences

Today, June 13, 2026, marks the decisive conclusion for candidates awaiting the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially launched the final mop-up examination, providing a critical lifeline for students who faced technical glitches or were unable to participate in the main exercise earlier this year. According to the Board’s spokesperson, Fabian Benjamin, this session is mandatory for all affected candidates, as the Board has explicitly declared that no further examinations will be conducted for the 2026 admission cycle.

## Critical Implications

For thousands of Nigerian students, this examination is a make-or-break event. The inability to participate or secure a passing grade in this final round effectively disqualifies candidates from the 2026/2027 university admission process. The JAMB leadership, under its current administration, has emphasized the need for administrative finality to ensure that the admission timeline for tertiary institutions remains on schedule. Candidates were advised to download their notification slips, which detail specific venue instructions and session times, to avoid being locked out of the exam halls.

## What Applicants Must Know

The Board has reiterated that this mop-up is not a repeat of the entire UTME process but a targeted intervention for those with verified challenges during the primary exercise. Applicants who failed to check their portal accounts for the updated examination notification slips are advised to do so immediately, as the logistics for the examination centers have been finalized. The Registrar’s office has confirmed that all centers are equipped with the necessary biometric verification tools, and strict security measures are in place to ensure the integrity of the results.

## Public Reactions & X (Twitter) Pulses

Social media platforms, particularly X (formerly Twitter), have been flooded with mixed reactions from students and parents. A student, writing under the handle @Sammy_J_Edu, commented: 'Finally, the door is closing. I have been anxious since my center had power issues last month. Hoping for the best today!' Conversely, some users expressed concern over the short notice. @EduWatchNigeria noted: 'The pressure on these students is immense. JAMB must ensure that today’s technical deployment is flawless to avoid a repeat of past hiccups.' Despite the tension, the consensus among educational stakeholders remains that this final mop-up is a necessary step toward stabilizing the academic calendar.
excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially commenced the 2026 Mop-Up UTME for eligible candidates, serving as the final window for the academic session.
Thousands of Nigerian candidates head to CBT centres nationwide today for the final 2026 UTME mop-up examination following technical glitches during the initial exercise.
title: JAMB Conducts Final 2026 UTME Mop-Up Examination Today
fullContent:
## Final Opportunity for 2026 UTME Candidates

Today, June 13, 2026, marks a critical turning point for thousands of Nigerian students as the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) conducts its official mop-up Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). This exercise is specifically designed for candidates who, despite completing their biometric verification during the main examination window (April 23–29, 2026), were unable to participate due to technical hitches, centre-related malfunctions, or other administrative challenges.

In an official statement released by the Board's Public Communication Advisor, Fabian Benjamin, JAMB reiterated that this mop-up exercise serves as the definitive final phase of the 2026 admission process. The Board has urged all affected candidates to ensure they have printed their updated Examination Notification Slips, which contain the specific venue and time for their sessions.

## Critical Implications

For many students, today is more than just a test; it is the gateway to securing admission into their preferred tertiary institutions. With JAMB having previously identified that some candidates faced insurmountable technical difficulties, this mop-up is a corrective measure to ensure equity in the admission system. Applicants are strictly warned that no further opportunities will be provided for the 2026 academic cycle. Candidates are advised to arrive at their designated Computer-Based Test (CBT) centres early to avoid any last-minute biometric identification issues.

## What Applicants Must Know

1. **Verification is Mandatory:** Only candidates who were biometrically verified during the initial window are eligible.
2. **Slip Printing:** If you have not printed your notification slip as of this morning, do so immediately via the official JAMB portal.
3. **Prohibited Items:** As per standard JAMB policy, no electronic devices, including mobile phones and wristwatches, are permitted inside the examination halls.

## Public Reactions & X (Twitter) Pulses

Social media is currently abuzz with student feedback. On X, @Chinedu_Edu commented, 'Finally, the wait is over. My centre in Lagos is open and the process seems smooth so far. Fingers crossed!' Another candidate, Fatima Abubakar, tweeted, 'I hope the technical issues that plagued the last session are fully resolved today. We are ready.' While some students express anxiety regarding the pressure of this final attempt, the overall sentiment remains one of relief that JAMB provided this necessary window to address the systemic challenges faced earlier this year.

## Overview of the 2026 UTME Mop-Up Exercise
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially concluded its mop-up examination for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) cycle. This special exercise, held on Saturday, June 13, 2026, was specifically designed to provide a fair opportunity for candidates who were unable to complete their examinations during the primary window (April 16–23, 2026) due to verifiable technical challenges at designated centers.

## Official Directive
According to Dr. Fabian Benjamin, the spokesperson for JAMB, the decision to hold the mop-up was part of the Board's commitment to ensuring no qualified candidate is disenfranchised due to circumstances beyond their control. "The mop-up examination represents the final phase of the annual UTME exercise and serves as an opportunity to address all outstanding examination challenges involving candidates who duly presented themselves but encountered difficulties in taking the examination," stated Dr. Benjamin.

He further clarified that the list of candidates included those who suffered biometric verification failures or witnessed center-specific technical glitches that disrupted the smooth conduct of the test. Affected candidates were instructed to begin printing their updated Examination Notification Slips from June 6, 2026, to confirm their specific venues and times.

## Critical Implications
For thousands of candidates across the nation, this mop-up window was vital for their admission prospects into Nigerian tertiary institutions for the 2026/2027 academic session. With the primary exams concluded in April, this final phase allows the Board to finalize its database and begin the process of releasing comprehensive results and preparing for the centralized admission placement process via CAPS (Central Admissions Processing System).

## What Applicants Must Know
1. **Result Processing:** Candidates who participated in the mop-up are advised to monitor the official JAMB portal regularly for the release of their results.
2. **Verification:** All candidates must ensure their results are correctly uploaded to the JAMB portal to be visible to their choice institutions.
3. **Post-UTME:** Prospective students should stay alert for announcements regarding Post-UTME screening exercises from their respective universities, as the conclusion of the mop-up marks the transition to the institutional admission phase.

Candidates are warned against engaging with third-party sites claiming to influence scores; only official JAMB channels remain the source of truth for all 2026 admission activities.
title: JAMB Conducts UTME Mop-Up Examination for Affected Candidates
excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) successfully concluded the 2026 UTME mop-up examination on June 13, 2026, for candidates who encountered technical glitches during the main exercise.
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) holds its official UTME mop-up examination today for candidates affected by technical hitches during the main exercise.
title: JAMB Conducts UTME Mop-Up Examination Today, June 13, 2026
fullContent:
## Official Commencement of Mop-Up Exercises

Today, June 13, 2026, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially commenced its scheduled mop-up examination for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). This exercise is specifically designed to accommodate candidates who were biometrically verified but were unable to write their examinations during the main window due to unforeseen technical glitches or system failures at their respective centers.

According to an official statement from the board, the Registrar, Prof. Is-haq Oloyede, emphasized that the decision to conduct this mop-up is in line with the board's commitment to equity and the protection of the academic rights of every candidate. "We are dedicated to ensuring that no student is disenfranchised by local infrastructure challenges or technical anomalies beyond their control," the statement noted.

## Critical Implications for Candidates

Candidates participating in today's exercise are strictly required to reprint their notification slips from the official JAMB portal. These slips contain the current venue and time of the examination, which may differ from the initial registration details. Failure to present the updated slip could result in denial of entry into the Computer-Based Test (CBT) center. The board has also cautioned against any form of examination malpractice, stating that the same high-security measures employed during the main UTME remain in full force for this mop-up session.

## What Applicants Must Know

- Ensure you arrive at the designated center at least 60 minutes before the scheduled time.
- Carry only the required documents; prohibited items like smartphones, wristwatches, and electronic devices are strictly banned.
- Biometric verification is mandatory; candidates must ensure their fingerprints match the registered records during the main exercise.

## Public Reactions & X (Twitter) Pulses

Social media platforms, particularly X (formerly Twitter), have seen a surge in activity as students scramble to confirm their centers. User @EduPulseNG tweeted: 'Relieved to see my name on the list today! Hope the network holds up this time.' Meanwhile, another user, @StudentAdvocate, remarked: 'It is a fair move by JAMB, but they must improve the stability of centers for next year to avoid these repetitions.'

As of this morning, thousands of students across the federation are currently seated for the assessment, marking the final phase of the 2026 admission screening process.

excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially authorized the printing of the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) original result slips, a critical document for prospective students.
title: JAMB Officially Activates Portal for 2026 UTME Original Result Slip Printing
fullContent:
## Official Commencement of Result Printing

The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has officially announced the commencement of the printing exercise for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) Original Result Slips. This development, confirmed on Wednesday, June 17, 2026, serves as a significant milestone for over 2.2 million candidates who participated in this year's examination cycle.

According to the JAMB spokesperson, Fabian Benjamin, the original result slip is a mandatory document required by all tertiary institutions across Nigeria for the purpose of Post-UTME screening, physical verification, and the overall admission selection process. Candidates are encouraged to access the board's e-facility portal to retrieve their documents immediately.

## Critical Implications for Candidates

The original result slip is not merely a score sheet; it contains vital biometric and security features, including the candidate’s photograph and national ranking. These elements are essential for university admissions officers to verify the authenticity of a candidate's credentials. Failure to provide this specific document during institutional screening may lead to disqualification from the admission process. Furthermore, JAMB has emphasized that this document remains valid for all subsequent academic activities throughout the candidate's journey into higher education.

## What Applicants Must Know

Candidates are advised to utilize any internet-enabled device—whether a personal computer at home, an office system, or an accredited Computer-Based Test (CBT) centre—to print their results. JAMB Registrar, Prof. Is-haq Oloyede, has previously urged candidates to ensure all data printed matches their registration profile. 

"We advise all UTME candidates to verify all details on the slip immediately after printing and ensure they maintain multiple copies for future admission-related purposes," Benjamin noted in his advisory. For those who may have lost access to the original phone numbers used during the registration phase, the Board has provided guidelines on its official website regarding the 'Profiled Email' service, which allows candidates to substitute lost SIM credentials to access their profiles. Applicants are strictly warned against patronizing fraudulent third-party websites or social media pages impersonating the board.

title: Leadership Transition: President Tinubu Appoints Professor Segun Aina as New JAMB Registrar
fullContent:
The Federal Government of Nigeria has announced a pivotal change in the leadership of the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB). President Bola Tinubu has appointed Professor Segun Aina as the new Registrar of the examination body. This transition follows the impending conclusion of the tenure of the incumbent Registrar, Professor Ishaq Oloyede, whose service is officially scheduled to end on July 31, 2026.

The appointment was made public in an official statement released on Thursday, May 21, 2026, by the President's Special Adviser on Information and Strategy, Bayo Onanuga. Professor Aina, a 39-year-old academic and professional, is expected to bring a fresh perspective to the management of Nigeria's tertiary admissions process.

Professor Aina brings a wealth of interdisciplinary experience to the role. He is a recognized professional with active memberships in several prestigious organizations, including the Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN), the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE), the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), and the Institution of Engineering and Technology. His background is viewed by the administration as a critical asset that will help drive the Board's technological and operational efficiency.

President Tinubu, in his communication, expressed strong confidence in the ability of Professor Aina to not only maintain the standards set by his predecessor, Professor Ishaq Oloyede—who is widely credited with the digitization and sanitization of the admission process—but to surpass them. The administration remains committed to ensuring that the integrity of the Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) remains intact as the nation transitions into the new academic cycle.

For candidates and stakeholders, the transition period is expected to be seamless. The JAMB management has assured the public that ongoing processes, including the 2026 admission cycle and recent registration extensions, will proceed as planned under the existing framework. Scholars and applicants are advised to remain focused on their academic pursuits and monitor official portals for further guidance on the upcoming policy meetings and admission guidelines.
excerpt: In a major leadership shift within the Nigerian education sector, President Bola Tinubu has appointed Professor Segun Aina to succeed Professor Ishaq Oloyede as the Registrar of the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB).

The Federal Ministry of Education has released official guidelines for the 2026 National Common Entrance Examination (NCEE) into Federal Technical Colleges. Registration is currently ongoing with strict adherence to NIN requirements.
fullContent:
## Overview of the 2026 NCEE

The Federal Ministry of Education, in conjunction with the National Business and Technical Examinations Board (NABTEB), has officially released the registration guidelines for the 2026 National Common Entrance Examination (NCEE). This examination is the primary gateway for admission into Federal Technical Colleges across the country. According to the official 2026 JAMB Bulletin, registration commenced on January 26, 2026, and is scheduled to conclude on May 24, 2026, with the examination slated for June 6, 2026.

## What Applicants Must Know

The Ministry emphasizes that the registration process is strictly digital. Applicants are required to utilize the official NABTEB portal to complete their enrollment. A critical prerequisite for every candidate is the possession of a valid National Identification Number (NIN). The Registrar has reiterated that this measure is non-negotiable, serving as a primary defense against identity fraud and the manipulation of candidate biometrics and photographs, an issue that necessitated tighter controls following the 2025 exercise.

Candidates are advised to ensure that all information provided during the online registration aligns perfectly with their official identity documents to avoid disqualification during the verification process. Failure to provide an accurate NIN or a mismatch in documentation may result in the rejection of the candidate’s application.

## Critical Implications for CBT Centres

The integrity of the examination environment remains a top priority for the Board. In line with updated security protocols, all accredited Computer-Based Test (CBT) centres are mandated to utilize HIKVision NVR/DVR systems with a minimum of 16 channels. These systems must be wired—wireless configurations are strictly prohibited—to ensure high-fidelity surveillance of all areas, including examination halls, server rooms, and entry/exit points.

According to the Director of Press and Public Relations, Mrs. Boriowo Folasade, centres that fail to comply with these rigorous infrastructure standards face severe sanctions, including potential prosecution. The Board remains committed to a level-playing field, reminding all centre operators that professionalism and integrity are the cornerstones of the 2026 examination exercise. Centres are encouraged to maintain continuous supervision of personnel and adhere to the dual-screen setup protocols to ensure transparency throughout the registration and examination phases.
title: Official Guide: 2026 National Common Entrance Examination (NCEE) Registration

fullContent:
## Official Announcement
The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has formally announced the commencement of its Post-UTME screening registration for the 2026/2027 academic session. According to an official statement released by the institution’s communication department, the portal for online registration will be active from Monday, June 15, 2026, through Wednesday, July 8, 2026. This process is mandatory for all prospective undergraduate candidates who selected the university as their first choice.

## Critical Implications
For thousands of candidates, this announcement marks the start of a highly competitive admission phase. The university has set a strict cutoff mark of 200 for eligibility. Applicants must note that meeting the UTME score is only the first step; candidates are also required to present five O'level credit passes in relevant subjects, including English Language and Mathematics, at a single sitting. The total cost associated with the application, comprising the screening fee of N2,000 and the administrative application fee of N3,500, must be processed through the university’s designated payment portal to avoid scams.

## What Applicants Must Know
The University Registrar has emphasized the necessity of accuracy during the registration process. 'Candidates are strictly advised to ensure that their O'level results are uploaded correctly on the JAMB CAPS portal. Any discrepancy in personal data or result details may lead to automatic disqualification,' the Registrar stated in a recent press briefing. Furthermore, candidates are warned against utilizing third-party registration centers that charge exorbitant fees, as the official process is designed to be seamless via the UNILAG admissions website.

## Public Reactions & X (Twitter) Pulses
Social media has been abuzz since the news broke. On X (formerly Twitter), students and parents have expressed mixed feelings ranging from excitement to anxiety. User @Chidi_Campus posted, 'Finally, the wait is over! Time to start the intensive reading for the screening exam.' Conversely, @Femi_Tech warned, 'Candidates should be careful of fake links circulating on WhatsApp. Always stick to the .edu.ng URL to be safe.' Local campus influencers are already organizing virtual study groups to help prospective students prepare for the potential Computer Based Test (CBT) that often follows the registration phase.

## Final Preparation Tips
Applicants are encouraged to visit the official admission office website regularly for updates regarding examination dates and specific venue requirements. Preparing your documents early and verifying payment receipts will ensure a stress-free admission process as UNILAG continues its tradition of academic excellence.
title: UNILAG Announces 2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening: Key Dates and Requirements

title: University of Nigeria Nsukka (UNN) Announces Postgraduate Batch C Mop-up Admission
fullContent:
## Official Notice from the Registry

The University of Nigeria Nsukka (UNN) has officially activated its 'Postgraduate Batch C Mop-up Admission' window for the 2025/2026 academic session. This announcement, published on the university's official news portal, serves as a final opportunity for prospective students who were previously unable to secure a spot during earlier admission rounds to apply or complete their documentation requirements. The Registry has urged all eligible candidates to treat this notice with the utmost urgency to ensure their names are processed within the current academic cycle.

## What Applicants Must Know

The mop-up exercise is specifically designed to fill remaining vacancies across various postgraduate departments. Applicants are advised to visit the official UNN portal at www.unn.edu.ng to download the specific admission instructions. Candidates must ensure that all their credentials and academic records are uploaded correctly to the university’s portal to avoid disqualification. Furthermore, the university administration has emphasized that this is a 'final' exercise, meaning no further extensions will be granted after the specified deadline. Prospective students are encouraged to check their dashboard regularly for status updates.

## Critical Implications

For many applicants, this mop-up exercise represents the last viable path to commencing their postgraduate studies this year. Missing this window could result in a one-year delay for candidates. The Registry has warned against fraudulent actors claiming to offer 'guaranteed' admission slots during this mop-up phase. The school maintains a zero-tolerance policy for intermediaries, and all transactions should be conducted strictly through the university's verified portal and official payment gateways. Failure to comply with these security directives may result in the forfeiture of the admission offer.

## Public Reactions & X (Twitter) Pulses

The announcement has ignited a wave of relief among prospective students on social media platforms. On X (formerly Twitter), user @EduNaijaUpdates shared the news, leading to numerous inquiries from applicants asking about specific department requirements. One student, Chinedu Okoro, commented: 'Finally, some good news! I have been refreshing my dashboard for weeks. I hope this batch is generous with space.' Others, however, expressed frustration regarding the short notice provided by the institution, highlighting the perennial struggle of meeting tight deadlines in the Nigerian academic system. Despite these concerns, the overwhelming sentiment remains one of cautious optimism.
excerpt: The University of Nigeria Nsukka (UNN) has released an official notice inviting candidates to participate in the Batch C mop-up admission exercise for postgraduate programs.

title: SmartJamb Enhances Digital Support for 2026 Prospective Students
fullContent:
As the 2026 admission cycle reaches its peak, the education technology platform SmartJamb has revamped its student support services. The portal, designed to simplify the complex landscape of Nigerian higher education, now provides real-time updates on Post-UTME forms, school-specific cut-off marks, and scholarship application deadlines. This initiative aims to bridge the information gap for students navigating the post-UTME phase.

Students are encouraged to utilize the platform's resources to verify the legitimacy of admission forms, such as those recently released by Mudiame University and Claretian University. Because the educational landscape is subject to rapid policy changes, SmartJamb advises all users to cross-reference portal information with official institution websites before making financial commitments. The platform also aggregates opportunities like the PTDF Scholarship for postgraduate engineering studies and the UK-funded Chevening Scholarships.

By centralizing these resources, SmartJamb aims to reduce the stress associated with the admission process. Students are reminded that while technology facilitates the process, the responsibility of meeting deadlines and maintaining accurate personal documentation lies with the candidate. Consistent monitoring of the official JAMB bulletin remains the primary defense against misinformation during this critical season.
excerpt: SmartJamb updates its portal to provide streamlined access to admission requirements and scholarship opportunities for the 2026 session.
While ASUU enters a period of stability, the non-academic unions threaten a nationwide shutdown over unresolved 2009 agreement renegotiations.
fullContent:
The relative peace in the Nigerian university system is currently under threat as the Senior Staff Association of Nigerian Universities (SSANU) and the Non-Academic Staff Union of Allied and Education Institutions (NASU) have issued a 14-day ultimatum to the Federal Government. This comes despite the Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) signing a landmark 3-year 'no-strike' agreement in mid-January 2026. The non-academic unions are demanding the immediate implementation of the renegotiated 2009 agreement, including the payment of withheld salaries and an increase in budgetary allocations for university infrastructure.

For 2026 admission seekers and current students, an industrial action by SSANU and NASU is particularly disruptive. While lecturers (ASUU) may continue to teach, the non-academic staff control the essential administrative machinery. A strike would lead to the immediate suspension of transcript processing, admission clearance, and the issuance of matriculation numbers. For the 2025/2026 incoming freshmen, this could mean a complete halt to the physical screening and registration processes required to formalize their student status. The timing is critical; as universities prepare to release final admission lists and commence first-semester registrations in February and March, a shutdown of the bursary and registrar's offices would create a massive backlog.

Analysis of the situation suggests that while the government has focused on placating ASUU to avoid classroom disruptions, the 'administrative engine' of the universities has been neglected. If the strike commences in mid-February, it will likely delay the conclusion of the 2025/2026 admission cycle and potentially overlap with the conduct of the 2026 UTME. Candidates are advised to complete any pending online clearance and fee payments immediately before the expiration of the 14-day notice. The potential for an administrative bottleneck in 2026 is high, and students already on campus may find themselves unable to access basic services like hostels, libraries, or health centers if the non-academic staff withdraw their services.
title: SSANU and NASU Issue 14-Day Strike Notice: Administrative Shutdown Looms

excerpt: Higher education stakeholders met recently to conclude the unified standards for the upcoming academic session.
title: Education Stakeholders Finalize 2026/2027 Entry Guidelines
fullContent:
Following a comprehensive review of the educational landscape, stakeholders from across the federation have reached a consensus on the guidelines for the 2026/2027 academic session. This summit, which focused on quality assurance, digital literacy, and admission transparency, resulted in several key policy updates that will guide the actions of Nigerian universities in the coming months.

Among the key decisions, the stakeholders emphasized the importance of standardizing the Post-UTME screening exercises to reduce the financial burden on parents and candidates. There was a unanimous agreement on the need for digitized admission records to ensure that all data is integrated with the JAMB CAPS system for seamless verification. Furthermore, the role of ICT in internal examinations was reinforced, with all institutions expected to maintain high technical standards to curb malpractice.

Additionally, the participants discussed the inclusion of more vocational-based subjects within the general studies curriculum, aligning with national economic goals. Universities were encouraged to invest in infrastructure to support the increasing demand for high-quality tertiary education. This meeting serves as a foundation for a unified academic calendar across the country, ensuring that the 2026/2027 session starts on a stable footing with minimal disruptions.

title: State Universities Accelerate Calendars: ABSU and NAUB Set Early February Matriculation
fullContent: In a significant departure from previous trends, state and specialized universities are leading a 'fast-track' movement in the Nigerian academic landscape. Abia State University (ABSU) has scheduled its 44th matriculation for February 27, 2026, the earliest in over a decade. Similarly, the Nigerian Army University Biu (NAUB) concluded its matriculation on February 16. This acceleration is a strategic response to the stabilized federal calendar, as these institutions seek to conclude their current sessions before the 2026 UTME results are even released. For 2026 candidates, this development positions state universities as highly attractive 'Plan B' or even primary options. The accelerated calendar means that students admitted to these schools for the 2026/2027 session could potentially graduate six to twelve months earlier than their counterparts in slower-moving federal institutions. This speed, however, increases the financial pressure on parents, as school fees and registration deadlines are being pulled forward into the first quarter of the year. For the 2026 admission cycle, candidates should expect state universities to begin their Post-UTME and Direct Entry screenings as early as May 2026, immediately following the conclusion of the main UTME. This 'early-bird' system favors candidates who are ready with their O-Level results. Those awaiting results (AR) may find themselves at a disadvantage if these institutions do not extend their portals. Furthermore, the specialized nature of schools like NAUB is attracting a new demographic of applicants interested in security studies and technology, potentially driving up the cut-off marks for those specific programs. The overall analysis suggests that the 2026 academic year will be one of 'The Great Catch-up,' where the backlog of students from previous strike years is finally cleared, leaving the system fresh for the incoming 2026 batch. Candidates are advised to monitor individual university portals weekly, as the era of 'waiting for JAMB' to dictate all timelines is giving way to institutional autonomy and competitive speed.
excerpt: Abia State University and Nigerian Army University Biu lead a new trend of early matriculations, signaling a fast-tracked 2026 admission cycle.

fullContent:
## SUMAS Set to Screen New Candidates for Medical and Applied Sciences

The State University of Medical and Applied Sciences (SUMAS), Igbo-Eno, Enugu State, has invited eligible candidates to apply for its Post-UTME screening exercise for the 2026/2027 academic session. As a specialized institution, SUMAS offers top-tier programs in medicine, nursing, radiography, medical laboratory sciences, and engineering.

This screening process is critical for selecting the next generation of healthcare professionals and applied scientists under a highly structured environment.

## Admission and Credit Pass Requirements

Due to the specialized nature of the academic programs at SUMAS, eligibility requirements are exceptionally strict:

*   **O'Level Credentials:** Candidates must possess at least five (5) O'Level credit passes in WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB in one sitting for core medical sciences, which must include English, Mathematics, Biology, Chemistry, and Physics.
*   **First Choice Status:** Candidates who chose SUMAS as their first choice or those willing to effect a change of institution to SUMAS on the JAMB portal are eligible to apply.

## Registration and Application Steps

Candidates are required to log on to the SUMAS online application portal, complete the online application form, and pay the administrative screening fee. Accurate data entry during registration is mandatory, as errors in O'Level results and UTME subject combinations can lead to automatic disqualification.

## Quick Action Checklist

*   Visit the official SUMAS application page at sumas.edu.ng/apply.
*   Log in and carefully review the credit level pass requirements for medical sciences.
*   Ensure your O'Level results are fully uploaded on the SUMAS and JAMB portals.
*   Complete the fee payment using the secure online banking channels on the portal.
*   Download and print the completed application receipt and screening slip.
title: State University of Medical and Applied Sciences (SUMAS) Invites Applications for 2026/2027 Screening

A new federal policy taking effect this month will strip funding from public tertiary institutions with fewer than 1,000 enrolled students, potentially forcing mergers and fee hikes.
title: TETFund to Enforce 1,000-Student Minimum Enrollment Rule: Smaller Universities Face Funding Crisis in 2026
fullContent:
In a move to optimize public resource utilization, the Federal Government has announced that starting from the 2026 fiscal year, any public university, polytechnic, or college of education with a total student population of less than 1,000 will no longer receive capital intervention from the Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund). This policy, highlighted by the Minister of State for Education, Prof. Suwaiba Ahmad, aims to eliminate the waste of distributing administrative resources to 'ghost' or under-performing institutions.

The investigation into this policy reveals a direct threat to the admission stability of newer and specialized state-owned institutions. At least 20 tertiary institutions currently fall below this threshold. For 2026 candidates, this creates a significant risk: institutions under the 1,000-student mark are likely to engage in 'aggressive admission' tactics this February and March to meet the quota and secure their funding for the next year. While this might seem like an easy window for admission, candidates should be cautious. These schools may face severe infrastructure deficits or be forced into mergers with larger universities mid-session if they fail to meet the target.

From an admission strategy perspective, this policy reinforces the dominance of larger, established universities. Candidates are flocking away from smaller, 'at-risk' schools to avoid being caught in potential administrative mergers, which is driving up the application numbers at major institutions. This has caused a 10% spike in competitive pressure for 2026 at the state level. Aspirants are encouraged to check the current enrollment statistics of their chosen state university. If the school is on the 'funding risk' list, it is advisable to have a solid 'Plan B' through a JAMB change of institution to a more stable federal university or a well-funded private alternative. The 2026 cycle will be defined by 'flight to quality,' where institutional stability becomes as important as the course of study itself.

title: TETFund Approves N2.52 Billion Intervention Fund for Each Nigerian University in 2026
fullContent: The Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) has approved the disbursement of intervention funds for 2026 to 271 tertiary institutions nationwide. During a stakeholders' meeting on February 12, 2026, Executive Secretary Sunny Echono revealed that each university will receive N2,525,932,228.02. The funding is part of the 2026 intervention cycle aimed at modernizing laboratories, hostels, and establishing new centers for Robotics, Coding, and AI. The funds also introduce the Nigerian Research and Education Network (NgREN) to improve global academic resource access.
excerpt: TETFund has announced the 2026 disbursement guidelines, allocating over N2.5 billion to each public university for infrastructure and research.

title: LASU Tops List of Most Sought-After Universities for 2026 Admission Cycle
fullContent: Data released by JAMB in February 2026 reveals that Lagos State University (LASU) leads the list of the ten most sought-after universities in Nigeria for the current admission cycle, with over 70,000 first-choice applicants. Other top-ranked institutions include the University of Lagos (UNILAG), University of Ilorin (UNILORIN), and Federal University Oye-Ekiti (FUOYE). The ranking highlights a continued preference for first-generation federal and state universities known for academic stability and prestige in the labor market.

The Federal Ministry of Education is recruiting qualified computer-based test administrators and vocational trainers to support the digital upgrade of technical colleges.
title: Federal Government Recruits Technical Instructors and CBT Examiners for TVET Digitization
fullContent: In line with the national shift toward digital assessment systems in Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions, the Federal Ministry of Education has announced an active recruitment drive. The ministry is seeking applications from qualified Computer-Based Test (CBT) administrators, systems engineers, and technical instructors to staff technical colleges nationwide.\n\nThis initiative follows recent successful trials of CBT testing for technical college entrance exams. Successful applicants will assist in modernizing vocational testing methods, establishing secure computer laboratory networks, and training students in technical curriculums designed to meet modern industrial needs. The recruitment portal is open online to all interested professionals holding relevant IT certifications and engineering degrees.

University of Ibadan Opens 2026/2027 Post-UTME Registration Portal, Announces July Screening Dates
fullContent:
The University of Ibadan (UI) has announced the commencement of online registration for its Post-UTME and Direct Entry (DE) screening exercise for the 2026/2027 academic session. According to an official statement from the university administration, the application portal will remain active from Monday, June 22, to Sunday, July 19, 2026. The institution stressed that there will be no extension of the registration window.

To be eligible, candidates must have selected UI as their first choice and scored at or above the designated cut-off marks. Additionally, candidates must have attained the age of 16 years by September 30, 2026. The registration process requires candidates to log onto the official UI admission portal using their JAMB Registration Number as the username and their surname as the password. Prospective students are required to pay a total of N5,000, which includes a portal access fee of N3,000 and a screening fee of N2,000.

The screening examinations are scheduled to take place from Monday, July 27, to Wednesday, July 29, 2026. The university management urged candidates to provide valid and active email addresses and phone numbers during registration to ensure they receive their personalized examination schedules.

fullContent:
The University of Ibadan (UI) has announced the commencement of registration for its Post-UTME and Direct Entry screening exercise for the 2026/2027 academic session. According to an official statement from the institution, the registration portal will remain active from Monday, June 22, to Sunday, July 19, 2026. Prospective candidates who chose UI as their first choice are strictly warned that there will be no extension of this registration period.

Candidates are required to log into the UI Admission Portal using their JAMB Registration Number as their username and their surname as their initial password. The screening process requires a portal access fee of N3,000.00 and a screening fee of N2,000.00, both of which are to be paid through the official portal in favor of the University of Ibadan. Upon successful payment, candidates must print their payment receipts and complete the online bio-data forms.

The university management has scheduled the Post-UTME screening examinations to take place from Monday, July 27, to Wednesday, July 29, 2026. Specific examination dates and venues for individual candidates will be communicated in due course. Candidates must ensure they provide active email addresses and functional phone numbers, as the university will not be liable for missed communications due to invalid contact details. title: University of Ibadan Opens 2026/2027 Post-UTME/Direct Entry Screening Application
fullContent:
The University of Ibadan (UI), Nigeria’s premier university, has officially announced the opening of its Post-UTME/Direct Entry screening registration for the 2026/2027 academic session. This initiative is designed to facilitate the admission of eligible candidates into the institution’s undergraduate programmes.

**Registration Timeline:**

- **Registration Commencement:** Monday, June 22, 2026
- **Registration Closure:** Sunday, July 19, 2026

**Payment Instructions:**

Candidates are required to log into the Admission Portal using their JAMB Registration Number (e.g., 202612345678EG) as their username and their surname as their initial password (e.g., OYESANMI, Chibuzor Umar).

A portal access fee of ₦3,000.00 and a screening fee of ₦2,000.00 should be paid through the Admission Portal in favor of the University of Ibadan. Candidates are expected to print the payment success receipt.

**Examination Schedule:**

The post-UTME examination is scheduled to take place from Monday, July 27 to Wednesday, July 29, 2026. The examination date for each candidate will be communicated in due course.

**Important Notes:**

- Candidates are advised to ensure that all information provided during registration is accurate and complete.
- The University of Ibadan will not tolerate any form of examination malpractice. Candidates found guilty of such offenses will be disqualified.

For further inquiries, candidates can contact the UI Admissions Office via email or phone as provided on the official website.

excerpt: The Governing Council of the University of Ilesa has ratified the establishment of a new faculty, effective March 2026.
fullContent:
In a move to strengthen its academic offerings, the University of Ilesa has officially approved the establishment of a new faculty. The decision was ratified by the university's Governing Council on March 31, 2026, following a thorough review and proposal submitted by the University Senate. This expansion reflects the institution's commitment to growth and its goal of becoming a center of excellence in specialized studies.

University administration confirmed that the new faculty will be equipped with modern laboratories and dedicated lecture facilities to accommodate students who will enroll starting from the 2026/2027 academic session. The establishment of this faculty is a key component of the university’s strategic plan to increase its capacity for research and professional training.

Prospective candidates applying for admission into the University of Ilesa for the upcoming session are encouraged to explore the new programmes offered under this faculty. Detailed information regarding the course structures and admission requirements will be available on the university's official website. This development is expected to significantly increase the student capacity of the university and attract top-tier academic staff to the Ilesa campus.
