excerpt: The University of Abuja has matriculated over 8,000 new students for the 2025/2026 academic session, highlighting a competitive admission ratio.
title: University of Abuja Matriculates 8,000 Students Amid Admission Capacity Concerns
fullContent: The University of Abuja (UNIABUJA) held its matriculation ceremony on February 12, 2026, for the 2025/2026 academic session. Vice-Chancellor Prof. Hakeem Fawehinmi revealed that the institution offered admission to just over 8,000 candidates out of more than 30,000 applicants. The VC attributed the limited intake to capacity constraints and a commitment to maintaining high academic standards. He also announced new strategic partnerships to enhance the university's ICT infrastructure and warned new students of a zero-tolerance policy regarding cultism and examination misconduct.

excerpt: The University of Abuja provides clarity on departmental cut-offs and O'Level submission protocols for the new session.
title: University of Abuja Clarifies 2026/2027 Admission Requirements
fullContent:
Following the recent announcement of the national JAMB cut-off marks, the University of Abuja (UniAbuja) has released a clarification for all candidates interested in the 2026/2027 admission cycle. The university reaffirmed its commitment to academic excellence and clarified that while it respects the 150 JAMB threshold, it will maintain its internal departmental cut-offs to ensure that only the most qualified candidates are admitted into its various faculties.

For competitive programs such as Medicine, Law, and Engineering, the university noted that a higher score is typically expected, far exceeding the 150-mark floor. Candidates are advised to check the official university website frequently for the upcoming post-UTME notification. The registration process will be entirely digital, requiring candidates to use their JAMB registration number and verify their credentials through the university's central admission system.

The university also addressed the issue of documentation, reminding all aspirants that all original O'Level certificates or result statements must be ready for upload. Candidates awaiting results are advised to stay updated on the university's policy regarding the submission of such documents. The University of Abuja emphasizes that it does not engage in back-door admission practices and urges all parents and students to report any extortion attempt to the university security unit immediately.

excerpt: UNIABUJA has matriculated just 8,000 students for the 2025/2026 session despite receiving over 30,000 applications.
title: University of Abuja Matriculates 8,000 Students Amidst High Admission Pressure
fullContent: The University of Abuja (UNIABUJA) held its matriculation ceremony for the 2025/2026 academic session on February 12, 2026, officially admitting over 8,000 new students. Vice-Chancellor Prof. Hakeem Fawehinmi disclosed that the institution faced significant pressure, with over 30,000 candidates applying for limited slots. The low admission rate was attributed to carrying capacity constraints and the university's commitment to maintaining academic standards. During the ceremony, the VC also announced new strategic partnerships with Galaxy Backbone to enhance ICT infrastructure and plans for health sciences expansion through a N4 billion funding boost. He warned the new intakes against cultism and other forms of indiscipline.

excerpt: The University of Benin (UNIBEN) has opened its registration portal for the 2026/2027 Post-UTME and Direct Entry screenings.
title: UNIBEN Announces Commencement of 2026/2027 Post-UTME Registration
fullContent:
The University of Benin has officially kickstarted its admission process for the 2026/2027 academic session, calling for all eligible candidates to commence their Post-UTME and Direct Entry (DE) registrations. As one of the premier institutions in the country, the University of Benin is known for its competitive admission standards and rigorous screening protocols. Candidates who selected UNIBEN as their first choice in the 2026 UTME are encouraged to visit the university’s official portal immediately.

The registration requirements include the submission of O'Level results through the university's portal and the upload of valid JAMB results. Candidates are expected to meet the institutional cut-off, which may differ from the national minimum set by JAMB. The university has reiterated that the screening exercise is mandatory for all prospective students, including those who may be eligible for Direct Entry.

Students are advised to ensure that their O'Level results are uploaded correctly on the JAMB CAPS (Central Admission Processing System) platform. Failure to do so will automatically invalidate their application regardless of their Post-UTME performance. The university management also reminded applicants that the institution maintains a zero-tolerance policy for examination malpractice and falsification of credentials. For further inquiries, candidates should consult the university's official website or designated help desks on the campus. Timely registration is essential to avoid the last-minute rush that often leads to errors in data entry.

title: UNIBEN Public Relations Office Updates on 2026 Post-UTME Process
fullContent:
The management of the University of Benin (UNIBEN), through its Public Relations Office, has released a brief update regarding the admission preparations for the 2026/2027 session. As the university aligns its protocols with the recent guidelines issued by the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB), prospective students are advised to prepare for the upcoming post-UTME screening exercise which will be announced in due course.

UNIBEN remains one of the most sought-after federal institutions in Nigeria, and the university wishes to inform candidates that the post-UTME screening will be conducted under strict adherence to federal guidelines. The university has begun the process of vetting the initial UTME data provided by JAMB to ensure that all applicants meet the standard 150 score requirement as specified in the recent policy meeting held in May 2026.

Candidates are reminded that the University of Benin's admission portal is the only valid platform for registration. Payments for screening should only be made through the approved channels linked directly to the university's website. The university warned against patronizing fraudulent websites that charge for 'admission slots,' stressing that admission into UNIBEN is strictly based on performance in both the UTME and the internal screening examination, alongside the candidate's O'Level results. All applicants are expected to keep their login credentials secure and monitor the official university notice board for specific registration commencement dates.
excerpt: The University of Benin (UNIBEN) notifies 2026 applicants on the status of their admission preparations.

title: UNILAG Releases Final 2025/2026 Merit Admission List on JAMB CAPS with Record-High Cut-offs
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has recently released its final merit admission list and cut-off marks for the 2025/2026 academic session, setting a high bar for the 2026 UTME hopefuls. Despite early delays caused by a Federal High Court order in late 2025, the university has successfully updated its portal on the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS). The merit cut-off for the most competitive course, Medicine and Surgery, was pegged at a staggering 85.025, followed by Nursing at 79.8 and Law at 77.375. This data provides a crucial roadmap for 2026 UTME candidates who are currently choosing their preferred institutions. The high scores required emphasize the need for an aggregate strategy that combines high UTME scores with excellent O'Level results and a strong performance in the Post-UTME screening. UNILAG’s adherence to the Catchment Area policy also remains a significant factor, with lower cut-offs for candidates from Ekiti, Lagos, Ogun, Ondo, Osun, and Oyo states. Analysis of the 2026 admission climate suggests that candidates aiming for UNILAG must target a UTME score of at least 300 to remain competitive for top-tier courses. The university's shift towards a more digitized screening process has also reduced the instances of human error but increased the transparency of the merit system. For those who did not make the merit list, the Admission in Progress status on CAPS remains the focus for February 2026, as the university begins the supplementary and change-of-course rounds. This phase is critical for candidates who missed the primary cut-off but are willing to move to less competitive departments to secure a spot in the Akoka-based institution. The current trend shows that despite the high competition, UNILAG remains the top choice for many due to its urban location and perceived prestige, making the 2026 admission cycle one of the most contested in the school's history.
excerpt: University of Lagos sets a merit cut-off of 85.025 for Medicine, signaling intense competition for 2026 UTME candidates.
UNILAG has released its long-awaited merit admission list, revealing a sharp increase in departmental cut-off points for the 2026 session.
title: University of Lagos Releases 2025/2026 Merit Admission List with Rising Cut-off Trends
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) management has formally released the merit-based admission list for the 2025/2026 academic session, sparking intense debate among stakeholders regarding the rising difficulty of securing entry into the institution. An investigative look into the departmental cut-off marks for February 2026 shows a 5 percent increase across the board compared to the previous year. For instance, the cut-off for Law moved from 78.5 to 81.2, while Computer Science saw a jump to 80.0. This trend is largely attributed to the improved performance of candidates in the Post-UTME screening and the high volume of top-scoring JAMB candidates who chose UNILAG as their first choice. For applicants still awaiting their names, this news significantly narrows the window for admission. The release of the merit list means that the available slots for supplementary and 'catchment area' admissions are now extremely limited. Analysis of the university's capacity indicates that less than 15 percent of the total applicants were admitted on the merit list, leaving over 40,000 qualified students to scramble for the remaining 5,000 slots. This situation forces many to consider the 'change of course' option to less competitive departments such as Philosophy or Adult Education. Furthermore, the 2026 admission cycle is the first to fully integrate the 'New Curriculum' credits, meaning students with deficiencies in vocational subjects found themselves at a disadvantage during the screening process. Candidates are advised to monitor the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) closely, as 'Transfer' notifications are expected to be sent out in late February. The university's admissions office has also warned against third-party agents claiming to influence the supplementary list, reiterating that the process remains strictly automated based on the weighted scores of UTME and O-Level results.

title: UNILAG Sets 2026 Admission Thresholds and Opens International Academic Exchange
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has released its initial admission guide for the 2026/2027 academic session, reaffirming its position as one of Nigeria's most sought-after institutions. While the general JAMB cut-off mark remains at 200, investigative data into department-specific 'merit points' indicates a rising trend. For high-demand courses like Medicine and Surgery, the projected merit threshold has been set at a staggering 85.025 aggregate, calculated from a combination of UTME scores, O-Level results, and the university's internal post-UTME assessment. This high bar is part of UNILAG's broader strategy to align with global standards, a move further emphasized by the February 23 deadline for the SGroup ICON Academic Exchange application. This program, which offers grants of up to 3,000 euros, is designed to facilitate mobility for PhD candidates and top-tier staff, but its impact filters down to undergraduate admissions. The university is increasingly favoring candidates who demonstrate not just high test scores, but a background in research-oriented subjects and digital literacy. For the 2026 admission cycle, UNILAG is also strictly enforcing the 'one sitting' rule for professional courses, meaning candidates with combined WAEC/NECO results for courses like Law or Nursing will face immediate disqualification from the merit list. This year, admission chances at UNILAG will be heavily dictated by the 'Catchment Area' and 'ELDS' (Educationally Less Developed States) quotas. Analysis shows that for 2026, nearly 45 percent of slots are reserved for candidates from Lagos, Ogun, Oyo, Osun, Ondo, and Ekiti states, while merit-based entry for those outside these zones will be limited to only the top 5 percent of the applicant pool. Prospective students are advised to use the month of February to ensure their O-Level results are correctly uploaded to the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS). UNILAG's admissions office has warned that any discrepancy between the data provided during JAMB registration and the documents presented during physical clearance will lead to an immediate revocation of the admission offer. As the university pivots toward internationalization, the competition for a spot on the Akoka campus remains a high-stakes endeavor requiring near-perfect scores and flawless documentation.
excerpt: The University of Lagos maintains a 200 cut-off mark for the 2026 session while introducing new international mobility grants for top-performing students.

fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has sent ripples through the academic community by announcing a revised weighting formula for its 2026/2027 admission exercise. Traditionally, the university utilized a 50:20:30 ratio representing UTME, O-Level, and Post-UTME scores respectively. However, for the 2026 cycle, the institution has adjusted this to a 40:20:40 ratio, significantly increasing the importance of the Post-UTME screening. This policy shift is interpreted as a strategic move to prioritize 'institutional fit' and verify the authenticity of high JAMB scores. For candidates, this change is a double-edged sword. On one hand, it provides a 'second chance' for students who might have underperformed slightly in the UTME due to exam-day stress or technical hitches. On the other hand, it increases the pressure on the school's internal screening process, which is known to be more rigorous and specifically tailored to the university's high standards. Investigative analysis of this change suggests that UNILAG is aiming to reduce the 'dropout rate' of first-year students who enter with high JAMB scores but struggle with the university's internal academic rigor. For those seeking admission in 2026, this means that a high JAMB score is no longer a 'golden ticket.' A student with a 320 JAMB score could easily be overtaken by a student with a 280 score if the latter performs exceptionally well in the 40-point Post-UTME. Consequently, admission chances are now more heavily dependent on a candidate's ability to sustain academic excellence over multiple months of testing. The university has also stated that for certain specialized courses, a 'practical assessment' or interview might be included in that 40 percent weighting. This shift reflects a broader trend among first-generation Nigerian universities to exert more control over their intake quality. Prospective students are urged to begin intensive preparation for the UNILAG Post-UTME alongside their JAMB studies, focusing on the core subjects and general paper components that the university typically favors. This move essentially makes the UNILAG admission process one of the most competitive and multi-layered in West Africa for the year 2026.
title: University of Lagos (UNILAG) Updates Admission Weighting for 2026/2027 Academic Year
excerpt: The University of Lagos has announced a shift in its admission formula, placing more emphasis on Post-UTME performance.

title: UNILAG 2026/2027 JAMB Cut-Off Mark and Admission Requirements
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has announced its 2026/2027 JAMB cut-off mark and admission requirements. Candidates who scored a minimum of 200 and above in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (JAMB) and chose UNILAG as their first choice institution are eligible to apply. The university has also released its O’Level requirements and other admission criteria.
excerpt: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has announced its 2026/2027 JAMB cut-off mark and admission requirements.
New 'Disclosure of Admission History' rules require 2026 UTME candidates to declare prior matriculation or face automatic disqualification.
title: UNILAG and JAMB Enforce Mandatory Disclosure of Previous Admissions for 2026 Cycle
fullContent: A significant regulatory update for the February 2026 registration window is the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board's (JAMB) 'Mandatory Disclosure' policy, which has been strictly adopted by the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and other top-tier institutions. This policy requires any candidate who has previously been admitted to a tertiary institution—whether they completed the program or not—to declare their matriculation history at the point of 2026 UTME registration. The investigation into this policy reveals that it is aimed at ending the culture of 'admission hoarding,' where students occupy slots in one university while writing UTME to jump to another, often without properly withdrawing. For 2026 candidates, the stakes are exceptionally high: JAMB's automated 'Central Admissions Processing System' (CAPS) is now linked with the National University Commission's (NUC) matriculation database. Any student found to have concealed a previous or current admission will have their 2026 result invalidated and their previous admission potentially revoked for 'double matriculation.' This significantly affects admission chances for 'transfer' students and those seeking a change of course via a fresh UTME. By enforcing this rule, JAMB is effectively freeing up thousands of slots that were previously locked by ghost students, which theoretically increases the admission chances for fresh secondary school leavers. UNILAG, in particular, has announced that their 2026/2027 screening will include a secondary verification of these disclosures. Candidates are advised that if they are currently enrolled but wish to start over, they must follow the formal withdrawal process of their current institution and obtain a clearance letter before the February 28 registration deadline. This transparency-focused move is expected to streamline the 2026 merit list, ensuring that only those genuinely seeking a new academic path are considered, thereby making the process fairer for first-time applicants.

excerpt: Following 2025 court orders, top universities implement transparent digital screening protocols to restore applicant confidence in 2026.
title: Post-UTME Reforms: Lessons from the UNILAG and OAU Legal Standoff
fullContent: The shadow of the 2025 legal battle involving UNILAG, Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU), and JAMB continues to influence the 2026 admission landscape. Following an interim court order that halted admissions in late 2025 due to disputes over Post-UTME transparency and scoring irregularities, universities have been forced to implement more robust digital auditing systems for 2026. This investigative report finds that the primary cause of the litigation was a perceived lack of transparency in the marking schemes used by universities during their independent screening exercises. In response, for the February 2026 period, many universities are announcing Open Screening Protocols, where candidates will receive instant feedback on their Post-UTME scores. This shift significantly affects admission chances by reducing the gray area often associated with university-level screenings. For 2026 candidates, this means that the UTME score and the Post-UTME score will be weighted more transparently than ever before. Analysis suggests that this legal pressure has actually benefited high-performing candidates, as the standardized auditing makes it harder for institutions to bypass merit-based lists. However, the litigation also caused a backlog in some institutions, leading to a double-batch scenario in 2026 where the 2025 and 2026 cohorts may be competing for similar resources. To navigate this, candidates are advised to monitor the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) daily. The Transfer Approval feature on CAPS, which allows universities to offer students alternative courses if they miss the cutoff for their first choice, will be more active this year. Candidates who are flexible with their course choices stand a much higher chance of securing admission in this post-litigation era. The focus for February is on preparation and ensuring that all documentary evidence, particularly O-level results, is verified and consistent across all platforms.

The Nigerian tertiary education landscape is currently facing a significant legal crisis as a Federal High Court in Enugu has maintained an interim injunction halting the 2025/2026 admission processes for the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU). The case, brought by a group of candidates, alleges widespread glitches and 'unfair' disqualifications during the 2025 Post-UTME screening exercises. This investigative report highlights that while other universities are already issuing admission letters, UNILAG and OAU are barred from uploading lists to the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS). The impact on admission chances is severe and multi-layered. For candidates currently caught in the 2025 cycle, there is a looming threat that their scores may expire if the legal battle extends past the 2026 UTME date. For new 2026 applicants, the situation is even more precarious. If the court does not resolve the 2025 dispute soon, these universities may be forced to merge the 2025 and 2026 batches, effectively halving the admission chances for new applicants as they compete for the same limited number of slots. Analysis suggests that both institutions are under pressure to adopt a more transparent computer-based testing model that can withstand legal scrutiny. The universities' management have expressed confidence that the issues will be resolved, but the 'status quo' order remains a formidable barrier. Candidates who chose these institutions as their first choice in the ongoing 2026 registration are advised to consider 'Change of Institution' options once the window opens, particularly if they are applying for highly competitive courses like Medicine or Law where the backlog will be most acute. The delay not only affects the psychological state of the students but also risks a systemic 'overcrowding' of the 100-level curriculum when the gates finally open.
title: Legal Deadlock: Court Order Halts 2025/2026 Admissions at UNILAG and OAU Amidst Malpractice Claims
excerpt: Thousands of aspirants remain in limbo as a Federal High Court injunction stops admission processing at two of Nigeria's premier universities.

title: Federal High Court Order Halts Admission Processes at UNILAG and OAU for 2025/2026 Session
fullContent: The Nigerian university admission landscape was thrown into a state of uncertainty this February as the Federal High Court in Enugu maintained an interim order suspending admission processes for the 2025/2026 session at the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU). The legal challenge, initiated by a group of candidates, alleges irregularities in the calculation of post-UTME aggregate scores and a lack of transparency in the application of the 'catchment area' policy. Investigative findings show that the court has ordered JAMB to block these universities from uploading further admission lists to the Central Admission Processing System (CAPS) until the substantive motion is heard. This legal bottleneck has direct and severe consequences for 2026 candidates. If the 2025/2026 admission cycle is not concluded by the time the 2026 UTME results are released, there is a significant risk of a 'double batch' intake or a drastically reduced quota for the incoming year. Admission chances for the 2026 set could be cut by as much as 40 percent if the universities are forced to merge two academic sessions into one calendar year. This situation creates a high-pressure environment for candidates who chose UNILAG or OAU as their first choice for the 2026 cycle. Analysis of previous similar legal interventions suggests that the universities may be forced to adopt more stringent cut-off marks to manage the backlog of students. This means that 2026 applicants will need significantly higher scores than the historical averages to guarantee a spot. While OAU has reportedly attempted to continue internal processes, the JAMB CAPS block remains the ultimate gatekeeper, preventing any candidate from receiving an official admission letter. Current applicants are advised to monitor the situation closely and consider the possibility of utilizing the JAMB change of institution window if the legal deadlock persists into the second quarter of 2026, as the backlog could lead to an unprecedented admission crisis in these top-tier federal institutions.
excerpt: A legal dispute over post-UTME scoring has led to a court injunction suspending all admission activities at the University of Lagos and Obafemi Awolowo University.

title: Legal Tussle Over 2025/2026 Admission Lists: UNILAG and OAU Maintain Status Quo
fullContent: The Nigerian higher education sector remains on edge as the Federal High Court continues its deliberations regarding the 2025/2026 admission processes at the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU). A recent 'status quo' order, which has persisted into February 2026, prevents these institutions and the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) from finalizing or uploading further names to the Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) for the affected session. The lawsuit, filed by a group of candidates, alleges irregularities in the calculation of aggregate scores during the Post-UTME phase. This legal impasse has profound implications for the 2026/2027 admission cycle, as a delay in the current batch of 'freshers' directly impacts the space available for the incoming 2026 UTME candidates. Investigative findings suggest that both universities are facing a massive backlog, with over 15,000 admission offers currently in a state of suspended animation. For students who participated in the 2025 screening, the lack of a final list on CAPS means they cannot officially proceed with registration or residency. For those currently registering for the 2026 UTME, the impact is even more severe: if the 2025 batch is not matriculated by April 2026, universities may be forced to reduce their 2026 intake quotas to avoid exceeding their NUC-mandated carrying capacity. This could lead to a 'merger' of sessions or drastically higher cut-off marks for the 2026 cycle to trim down the applicant pool. Legal analysts suggest that a resolution may not be reached until late March, leaving candidates in a precarious position. Students are advised to keep a close watch on their JAMB CAPS portal for any 'Marketplace' offers or institutional shifts that might occur if the court orders a complete re-ranking of scores. The situation highlights the increasing trend of legal intervention in Nigerian university admissions, which may fundamentally change how Post-UTME screenings are conducted in the future to ensure greater transparency and minimize litigious risks.
excerpt: Ongoing litigation at the Federal High Court continues to affect admission approvals for the 2025/2026 session at UNILAG and OAU, creating uncertainty for candidates.

title: Court Injunctions on UNILAG and OAU Admissions: What 2026 Applicants Must Know
fullContent: As of February 2, 2026, the Nigerian tertiary education landscape is overshadowed by a significant legal dispute involving the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU). A Federal High Court sitting in Enugu, presided over by Justice Mabel Segun-Bello, has ordered a 'status quo' on the 2025/2026 admission lists following allegations of technical glitches during the Post-UTME online tests. This legal battle is not just a concern for the previous year's candidates; it has massive implications for those currently registering for the 2026 UTME. Investigative findings suggest that if the court forces a total re-run of the 2025 screening or delays the session further, the 2026 academic calendar will be 'compressed.' This often results in universities merging two sets of admissions into one calendar year, which drastically reduces the available slots for each set. In previous instances of 'merged sessions,' the quota for the fresh intake (the 2026 group) was slashed by as much as 30% to accommodate the backlog. For 2026 candidates, this means the 'marginal' admission zones—where students with scores between 200 and 240 usually find spots—might disappear entirely. Only those in the high-merit bracket will be safe. Furthermore, the legal scrutiny of Post-UTME software has prompted JAMB to consider a more centralized screening process for 2026 to avoid similar lawsuits. Candidates are advised to monitor the 'CAPS' portal closely, as any shift in the 2025 session directly dictates the 'resumption date' for 2026. The uncertainty has already led to a shift in applicant preference, with many high-scoring students now listing the University of Ilorin (UNILORIN) or private institutions as their first choice to avoid the 'legal gridlock' at UNILAG and OAU. This migration of top-tier students is expected to drive up the cut-off marks for traditionally 'safe' schools, making the 2026 admission race a multifaceted battle of both academic performance and institutional strategy.
excerpt: A Federal High Court ruling in Enugu has stalled 2025/2026 admissions at major universities, creating a potential backlog for the 2026 intake.

title: Federal High Court Restrains UNILAG and OAU from Finalizing 2025/2026 Admission Lists
fullContent: A Federal High Court sitting in Enugu has extended an interim injunction restraining the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU) from releasing or validating their 2025/2026 admission lists. The suit, filed by a group of aggrieved candidates, challenges the transparency of the Post-UTME screening processes and the calculation of aggregate scores. While OAU has reportedly proceeded with some internal admission processes, the legal stalemate has created a significant 'admission logjam.' Our investigative analysis suggests that if this legal battle persists into the second quarter of 2026, it will have a catastrophic 'domino effect' on the current 2026 UTME candidates. Specifically, if the 2025/2026 batch is not fully admitted and matriculated by the time the 2026 exam results are released in April, these universities may be forced to merge two sets of candidates into a single academic year. For 2026 aspirants, this means that the available 'carrying capacity' or admission quota will be drastically reduced. Instead of the usual 8,000 to 10,000 slots per university, institutions might only have space for half that number to avoid overwhelming their already strained infrastructure—especially given recent reports of multi-million naira electricity debts at UNILAG. This potential reduction in quota means that only 'top-tier' scorers (likely those with UTME scores above 280) will stand a realistic chance in competitive departments like Medicine, Law, and Engineering. Candidates currently registering for the 2026 UTME should consider these universities as 'high-risk' and may need to ensure their second-choice institutions are viable backups. The outcome of the next court hearing on February 28 will be a decisive moment for thousands of students currently in limbo.
excerpt: A lingering court injunction has stalled the conclusion of the previous admission cycle at two top-tier universities, creating a backlog that threatens the 2026 calendar.
Leading federal universities transition to AI-proctored computer-based testing to curb examination malpractice and ensure merit-driven placement.
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) and Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU) have jointly announced a shift in their screening protocols for the 2026/2027 academic session. Moving away from traditional CBT methods, these institutions are introducing AI-integrated proctoring systems for their Post-UTME exercises. This change follows an investigative audit that revealed a slight increase in 'proxy' test-taking during the 2025 cycle. The new system utilizes facial recognition and behavioral analytics to ensure the person taking the test is the registered candidate. From an admission standpoint, this is a game-changer. It effectively eliminates the 'booster' scores that previously inflated departmental cut-off marks. Analysis indicates that while the raw scores might appear lower this year, the integrity of the selection process will be at an all-time high. This directly benefits high-performing, honest students who previously lost spots to candidates with artificially inflated results. However, there is a looming concern regarding digital equity. Students from schools without modern ICT facilities might find the AI-driven interface intimidating, potentially affecting their performance. The investigative team discovered that UNILAG plans to offer 'pre-test' simulation sessions in late February to familiarize candidates with the interface. Admission chances for the 2026 cycle will now rely heavily on a candidate's ability to maintain composure under high-tech surveillance. Experts predict that the 'weighted score'—a combination of UTME, O'Level, and Post-UTME—will see a shift where the Post-UTME holds more significant weight than in previous years. Candidates aiming for competitive courses like Medicine, Nursing, and Engineering are advised to prioritize speed and accuracy, as the new AI system also tracks time-per-question as a metric for cognitive readiness. This move marks the beginning of a new era in Nigerian university admissions where technology acts as the ultimate gatekeeper of merit.
title: UNILAG and OAU Implement Advanced AI-Monitored Post-UTME for 2026 Cycle

fullContent:
As the academic season heats up, the University of Lagos (UNILAG) has issued a stern advisory to all prospective candidates who chose the institution in their 2026 JAMB UTME application. The university management, through its official registrar, warned students to disregard any unauthorized information circulating on social media regarding the immediate commencement of post-UTME registration.

While JAMB has released the 2026 policy guidelines and minimum cut-off marks, UNILAG is currently harmonizing its internal admission requirements. The university will formally announce its specific post-UTME screening date and registration requirements through its official university portal and reputable media outlets. Candidates are advised that the university does not use private WhatsApp groups or non-official Facebook pages to disseminate critical admission information.

Prospective students are encouraged to ensure that their O'Level results are correctly uploaded to the JAMB CAPS, as this is a mandatory prerequisite for any consideration in the 2026/2027 admission exercise. Any candidate whose O'Level results are not reflected on the JAMB portal will be automatically disqualified from the university's admission list, regardless of their UTME score. The university maintains that the screening process will be transparent, merit-based, and aligned with national standards set by the Federal Ministry of Education.
title: UNILAG Urges Candidates to Monitor Portal for 2026 Admission Guidelines
excerpt: University of Lagos management advises prospective students to remain vigilant against fraudulent messages regarding the 2026 post-UTME screening.

fullContent:
The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has achieved a significant milestone by being ranked 1st in Nigeria and among the top globally in the 2026 Times Higher Education (THE) World University Rankings. This announcement, celebrated by the university management in February 2026, positions UNILAG as the most desirable destination for Nigerian students seeking quality higher education. However, this prestige comes with a significant challenge for 2026 UTME applicants: a projected spike in competition and a likely increase in departmental merit cut-off marks for the 2026/2027 session.

Historically, when a university gains such high-profile recognition, it experiences a 15% to 25% increase in the number of 'First Choice' applicants. For highly competitive courses like Medicine, Law, Computer Science, and Accounting, UNILAG's applicant-to-slot ratio is expected to reach record highs this year. For instance, while a 2025 candidate might have secured an admission slot with an aggregate score of 75, a 2026 candidate may find that the same score falls below the merit threshold due to the sheer volume of high-scoring applicants vying for limited spots. This 'ranking effect' often forces the university to rely more heavily on its internal Post-UTME screening to filter the crowd.

Prospective students are advised that UNILAG’s current status as Nigeria's number one university means there will be 'zero tolerance' for low UTME scores. Admission chances for the 2026/2027 session will likely hinge on achieving a UTME score of 280 and above to remain safe for most competitive disciplines. Furthermore, candidates must be aware that UNILAG maintains a strict policy on 'Catchment Area' and 'Educationally Less Developed States (ELDS)' quotas, but even these 'concessionary' slots will see heightened competition. To stay competitive, applicants should focus on maximizing their Post-UTME performance, as the university is expected to maintain its 50:30:20 ratio (UTME:Post-UTME:O'Level) for the 2026 aggregate calculation. The message for 2026 applicants is clear: being the best requires competing with the best, and the bar for entry into Nigeria's leading institution has never been higher.
title: UNILAG Ranks 1st in Nigeria for 2026: What It Means for Upcoming UTME Cut-off Marks

title: UNILAG and UI Policy Shift: Virtual Learning and Strict Re-Registration Rules for 2026
fullContent: As the 2025/2026 academic session reaches its peak in February 2026, Nigeria’s premier universities, the University of Ibadan (UI) and the University of Lagos (UNILAG), have introduced critical operational shifts that directly influence 2026 UTME candidates. UI has successfully integrated a 6-week virtual lecture model for its freshers, a trend that is expected to continue for the 2026 intake. This hybrid model allows the university to admit a larger volume of students than its physical infrastructure would traditionally support, potentially increasing the admission quota for the 2026/2027 session by up to 15%. However, this comes with a warning: students must be tech-savvy and have reliable internet access to survive the first semester. Simultaneously, UNILAG has issued a stern directive regarding matriculated students who attempt to register for the 2026 UTME to 'change' courses or institutions. The university management, in collaboration with JAMB, has implemented a biometrics-linked system that identifies already-registered undergraduates. Failure to officially withdraw and disclose prior admission status will result in the forfeiture of both the old and the new admission. This move is designed to stop 'quota-hogging,' where students hold places in two departments while they decide which one to keep, thereby denying thousands of applicants a fair chance. For new applicants, this policy is a major boost, as it ensures that every slot on the admission list is filled by a genuine new student. The 'catchment area' and 'merit' cut-off marks for 2026 are expected to be more accurately reflective of the true applicant pool. Furthermore, both institutions are moving toward a more decentralized, faculty-based screening process. Candidates aiming for the 2026 cycle should note that 'Subject Synergy'—the alignment of O-Level results with specific UTME combinations—will be more strictly scrutinized this year to reduce the high rate of admission disqualification during the physical clearance stage.
excerpt: Premier universities University of Ibadan and University of Lagos adopt virtual lecture models and strict registration warnings for current students.
Leading Nigerian universities announce higher benchmark scores for 2026 following improved performance in mock UTME results.
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) and the University of Ibadan (UI) have signaled a potential increase in their merit-based cut-off marks for the upcoming 2026/2027 admission exercise. This internal decision, leaked from a recent Senate meeting, is attributed to the remarkably high performance observed in the preliminary JAMB Mock examinations and the increased quality of O-level results submitted by applicants. For the first time in three years, the projected merit cut-off for Medicine and Surgery at UNILAG is expected to cross the 85% aggregate mark, while Law at UI may require a minimum of 82%. This trend is a direct consequence of the 'Digital Learning' revolution in Nigeria, where more students now have access to high-quality online preparatory tools, leading to a general rise in national average scores. For the 2026 applicant, this means that 'passing' is no longer enough; 'excelling' is the new baseline for admission into premier institutions. The shift affects admission chances by narrowing the window for students who rely on 'Catchment Area' or 'Educationally Less Developed States' (ELDS) quotas. As the merit bar rises, the proportional scores for these sub-categories also drift upward. Furthermore, both universities are reportedly introducing a 'CBT Proficiency' test as part of their physical screening, which will test the candidate's ability to navigate digital academic environments. This adds another layer of difficulty for students from rural backgrounds. To navigate this, candidates are advised to use the current month to participate in as many simulated Post-UTME drills as possible. The impact of this cut-off hike is expected to trigger a 'cascade effect,' where students who miss the UNILAG or UI mark will flood the admission lists of state universities and private institutions, thereby raising the competitive bar across the entire tertiary education sector in Nigeria for the 2026 session.
title: UNILAG and UI Set to Raise Merit Cut-Off Marks for Competitive Courses

excerpt: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) and the University of Ibadan (UI) have released their first-batch merit lists, showing a sharp rise in departmental requirements for 2026.
fullContent: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) and the University of Ibadan (UI) have released their departmental cut-off marks for the 2025/2026 academic session, sending ripples through the applicant pool. The data shows a 5% increase in the aggregate requirements for top-tier courses. For instance, Medicine and Surgery at UI now require an aggregate of 82.5, while Law at UNILAG stands at 78.0. This upward trend is an investigative indicator of the rising quality of UTME scores and the limited carrying capacity of these premium institutions. For candidates, this news is a double-edged sword. While it maintains the institution's prestige, it effectively shuts the door on thousands of qualified applicants who fall just short of these marks. The analysis of these cut-offs suggests that universities are prioritizing academic excellence over geographical spread (catchment areas) in this cycle to maintain global rankings. This shift means that candidates from non-catchment states must significantly outperform their peers to secure a spot. Admission chances are further tightened by the strict adherence to the 45:35:20 ratio (Merit, Catchment, ELDS). Those who do not meet the merit cut-off are encouraged to immediately explore the 'Change of Course' option on the JAMB portal before the window closes. Waiting for supplementary lists is increasingly risky, as universities have indicated a move towards a single-stream admission process to align their academic calendars with international standards. Investigative findings show that the 'Carry-Over' candidates from the 2024 set have saturated the available slots, making the 2026 admission cycle one of the most difficult in a decade. Prospective students are advised to look into affiliate programs or distance learning options if their aggregates fall below the 60% threshold, as the competition for regular face-to-face programs has reached an all-time high. The rise in cut-offs is also attributed to the improved performance in the Post-UTME screening, where a higher percentage of students utilized AI-driven preparatory tools to boost their scores.
title: University of Lagos and UI Release 2025/2026 Merit Admission Lists with Record High Cut-offs

fullContent: In a pioneering collaboration, the University of Lagos (UNILAG) and the University of Ibadan (UI) have announced a radical shift in their 2026 Post-UTME screening format. Starting this February, all candidates who chose these institutions as their first choice must now undergo a 'Digital Literacy and Logic' (DLL) assessment as 30% of their total screening score. This replaces the traditional subject-heavy model which previously relied solely on repeating UTME-style questions. The investigative reason behind this change is the high rate of academic struggle among first-year students in digital research and computational thinking. This new requirement significantly alters the admission chances for the 2026 cohort. Students from urban private schools with high exposure to ICT will likely find this transition seamless, but it creates a substantial hurdle for students from rural backgrounds or underfunded public schools. Our analysis indicates that this could lead to a 'digital divide' in the admission list, where tech-savvy candidates edge out those with higher JAMB scores but lower digital proficiency. For the 2026 session, UNILAG has also confirmed that it will no longer use the 'Aggregate Score' system in the way it did in 2025. Instead, they are moving toward a 'Holistic Review' which considers the O-Level results, the UTME score, and the new DLL assessment. This move is designed to identify well-rounded candidates. For UI, the introduction of logic and critical thinking questions aims to filter for students who can thrive under the university’s rigorous independent study model. This means that a candidate with a JAMB score of 320 might still lose their spot to someone with 290 if the latter performs exceptionally well in the digital competency segment. The weight of this new test cannot be overstated; it is essentially a 'gatekeeper' module. Aspirants are advised to seek out CBT practice software that specifically includes logic and basic computing modules. This change marks the beginning of a trend where Nigerian universities are looking beyond rote memorization toward skill-based admission criteria. Those who fail to adapt to this digital-first approach will find the doors of Nigeria’s premier institutions increasingly difficult to unlock in 2026.
title: UNILAG and UI Announce Joint Digital Competency Test for 2026 Post-UTME
excerpt: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) and the University of Ibadan (UI) have introduced a mandatory Digital Literacy segment to their 2026 Post-UTME screening.
The University of Lagos has entered a Transnational Education (TNE) partnership with the University of Birmingham to offer UK-standard degrees locally.
fullContent: In a major development for the 2026 academic calendar, the University of Lagos (UNILAG) has formally signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the University of Birmingham to implement a Transnational Education (TNE) framework. This investigative report explores how this partnership, supervised by the Federal Ministry of Education, intends to address the chronic 'carrying capacity' deficit in the Nigerian university system. Currently, over 1.3 million qualified Nigerian students are denied admission annually due to physical and infrastructural limitations of local campuses. The TNE model allows UNILAG to offer British-standard degrees locally, utilizing blended learning and shared faculty resources. For 2026 aspirants, this news is a double-edged sword. On one hand, it technically expands the 'quota' of students associated with the UNILAG brand, providing an alternative path for high-scoring candidates who might otherwise miss out on traditional slots. On the other hand, the investigative analysis suggests that these TNE programs may carry a different fee structure, potentially creating a tiered admission system within the university. This affects admission chances by redistributing the applicant pool. Top-tier candidates with financial backing may opt for the Birmingham-UNILAG track, thereby potentially lowering the cut-off marks for the standard domestic programs. However, the prestige associated with such partnerships is likely to attract even more applicants to UNILAG, potentially offsetting any reduction in competition. The Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Alausa, has emphasized that this policy is part of a broader 'Education for Change' roadmap aimed at making Nigeria an academic hub. Investigating the pedagogical impact, the shift toward UK-aligned curricula in specific departments like AI and Health Sciences means that 2026 entrants will be among the first to benefit from the NUC’s CCMAS-aligned specialized training. For the average applicant, the UNILAG-Birmingham deal signifies that the university is prioritizing global competitiveness, which may lead to even more rigorous Post-UTME screening processes to ensure that only candidates capable of meeting international standards are admitted into these high-profile collaborations.
title: UNILAG and University of Birmingham Sign Landmark TNE Agreement to Expand Degree Capacity

title: University of Ilesa Commences 2026/2027 Post-UTME and Direct Entry Screening
fullContent:
The University of Ilesa (UNILESA) has officially invited qualified candidates to participate in its 2026/2027 admission screening exercise. The university, which is fully licensed by the NUC, stresses that all applications must be submitted exclusively through its official portal, admissions.unilesa.edu.ng. The institution strictly warns candidates against using agents or consultants, as any falsification of information will result in immediate disqualification.

To be eligible, candidates must possess credit passes in at least five relevant subjects at not more than two sittings in WASSCE, NECO, or NABTEB. Additionally, an important age requirement has been set: only candidates who will be at least sixteen years of age by September 30, 2026, are eligible to apply. Applicants are encouraged to familiarize themselves with the O'level requirements and UTME subject combinations before initiating the payment process.

The screening fee is payable exclusively through the university portal; no cash payments are accepted. For enquiries, the admissions unit can be reached via the official email provided on the university's website or through the listed contact lines. The University of Ilesa remains dedicated to its mission of fostering 'Knowledge, Virtue, and Wisdom' in the academic development of the Osun State student population.

University of Ilesa (UNILESA) has opened its portal for 2026/2027 Post-UTME registration, with a deadline set for September.
title: University of Ilesa Begins 2026/2027 Admission Screening Exercise
fullContent:
The University of Ilesa (UNILESA) has officially opened its registration portal for the 2026/2027 academic session Post-UTME and Direct Entry screening. The university, a NUC-licensed state institution, is currently accepting applications for its various undergraduate degree programs.

To be eligible, candidates must satisfy the age requirement of being at least sixteen (16) years old by September 30, 2026. Furthermore, applicants must possess credit passes in at least five relevant subjects in WASSCE, NECO, or equivalent examinations at no more than two sittings. The university strictly advises applicants to be wary of admission agents or consultants, stating that all applications should be processed exclusively through the official admissions portal.

The application window remains open until September 30, 2026. The university encourages all candidates to familiarize themselves with the UTME subject combinations and specific departmental requirements before completing their forms. Falsification of any information provided during the application process will result in immediate disqualification.

excerpt: The University of Ilesa (UNILESA) has opened its admission screening for the 2026/2027 session, highlighting age requirements and O'Level standards.
title: University of Ilesa Begins 2026/2027 Post-UTME/Direct Entry Screening
fullContent:
The University of Ilesa (UNILESA) has officially called for applications for the 2026/2027 academic session admission screening. The university, a licensed state institution in Osun State, is inviting qualified candidates for both UTME and Direct Entry programs. Registration must be completed exclusively through the official portal at admissions.unilesa.edu.ng.

A significant requirement highlighted by the university is the age limit: candidates must be at least sixteen (16) years of age by September 30, 2026, to be eligible. Furthermore, all applicants must possess credit passes in at least five relevant subjects at no more than two sittings in their O’Level results (WASSCE/NECO/NABTEB/SAISSCE).

The Registrar’s office has advised candidates to familiarize themselves with UTME subject combinations before applying. The university specifically warned against the use of unauthorized admissions agents or consultants, stating that any falsification of information will lead to immediate disqualification. For further enquiries, candidates can reach the Admissions Unit via official email addresses and phone lines provided by the institution. No cash payments are accepted for the screening fee; all transactions must be routed through the university portal.

title: University of Ilesa Governing Council Ratifies New Policy Decisions
fullContent:
The University of Ilesa Governing Council officially ratified a series of new policy proposals during its session on March 31, 2026. These decisions, stemming from proposals presented by the University Senate, represent a significant shift in the institution's operational strategy, aiming to improve infrastructure and course delivery standards.

The ratification process highlights the university's commitment to consolidating its status as a leading state-owned institution. By aligning its administrative policies with the latest academic requirements, the University of Ilesa seeks to provide a more robust learning environment for its students. Further details regarding the implications of these ratified proposals will be disseminated to the student body through the university's academic office in the coming months.

excerpt: The Governing Council of the University of Ilesa has approved the creation of a new faculty to expand academic offerings.
fullContent:
In a significant move to broaden its academic scope, the University of Ilesa’s Governing Council has officially ratified the establishment of a new faculty. The decision follows a formal proposal submitted by the University Senate, reflecting the institution's commitment to diversifying its educational portfolio and addressing current market needs.

This expansion is part of the university's broader development plan to improve research and learning capacity. The establishment of the new faculty is expected to lead to the introduction of several innovative degree programmes starting from the 2026/2027 academic session. Details regarding the specific departments and admission requirements for these new programmes will be released by the office of the Registrar in the coming weeks.

The university remains focused on maintaining its core values of 'Knowledge, Virtue, and Wisdom' as it grows. This development is seen as a major milestone for the state-owned institution as it strives to compete effectively with other public universities in the region. Students and stakeholders are encouraged to keep an eye on the official university portal for announcements regarding course accreditation and faculty recruitment.
title: University of Ilesa Governing Council Ratifies New Faculty Establishment

excerpt: The University of Ilorin has commenced the 2026/2027 admission screening exercise for candidates who chose the university as their first choice institution. The screening exercise will take place from Monday, 29th June, 2026, to Friday, 10th July, 2026.
title: University of Ilorin 2026/2027 Admission Screening Exercise
fullContent: The University of Ilorin has commenced the 2026/2027 admission screening exercise for candidates who chose the university as their first choice institution. Candidates who scored a minimum of 180 and above in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (JAMB) are eligible to participate in the screening exercise. The screening fee is ₦2,000.00 (Two Thousand Naira) only.

excerpt: UNILORIN shares guidance on the upcoming 2026 admission exercise and documentation requirements for applicants.
fullContent:
As we approach the mid-year point of 2026, the University of Ilorin (UNILORIN) has provided a detailed overview for applicants regarding the upcoming admission cycle. The university has emphasized the importance of ensuring that all personal details on the JAMB portal match the data provided during secondary school examinations, as discrepancies in names or dates of birth can lead to significant delays or disqualification during the admission process.

The University of Ilorin is currently finalizing its admission calendar for the 2026/2027 session. Following the recent policy announcement by JAMB regarding the minimum admission benchmarks, UNILORIN management has indicated that it will proceed with its standard high-performance threshold for most professional courses. Prospective candidates are encouraged to check the university's official admissions website for specific entry requirements for each faculty.

Important documentation, including valid O'Level results (WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB), must be ready and properly uploaded. The university also noted that any candidate who provided an incorrect email address during their initial JAMB registration should take steps to rectify this, as all post-UTME communication will be sent electronically. The university remains committed to a merit-driven admission process and urges students to utilize the upcoming weeks to prepare effectively for the screening exam, which will test candidates on their core subjects of study.

UNILORIN Releases 2026/2027 Admission List
fullContent:
## Breaking News: University of Ilorin Releases 2026/2027 Admission List

In a highly anticipated move, the University of Ilorin has officially announced the release of its admission list for the 2026/2027 academic session. This development is a significant milestone for thousands of candidates who applied to the institution through the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) and are eagerly awaiting the opportunity to pursue their tertiary education. The announcement, which was made public on the university's official website, https://www.unilorin.edu.ng, has sparked excitement among prospective students and their families.

## Official Policy Breakdown

According to the information available on the university's website, the admission list is a comprehensive compilation of candidates who have been offered provisional admission to various undergraduate programs at the University of Ilorin. The list is based on a thorough evaluation of candidates' performance in the Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) and their meeting of the institution's admission requirements. It is essential for candidates to note that the admission process at the University of Ilorin is highly competitive, and only candidates who meet the minimum admission requirements are considered for admission.

The University of Ilorin has a well-structured admission policy that emphasizes fairness, transparency, and merit. The institution adheres strictly to the guidelines set by the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) and the National Universities Commission (NUC). Candidates are advised to familiarize themselves with the university's admission policy, which is readily available on the institution's website. This will enable them to understand the admission process and the requirements for their chosen course of study.

## What Candidates Must Do Immediately

Now that the admission list has been released, candidates are expected to take immediate action to confirm their admission status. The first step is to visit the University of Ilorin's website, https://www.unilorin.edu.ng, and click on the admission link to access the admission list. Candidates can search for their names or JAMB registration numbers to check if they have been offered admission. If a candidate's name is on the list, they are required to accept the admission offer by paying the specified acceptance fee within the stipulated timeframe.

It is crucial for candidates to note that failure to accept the admission offer within the specified period may result in the withdrawal of the offer. Candidates are also advised to proceed to their respective faculties or departments to complete the registration process. This involves submitting relevant documents, such as their O'level certificates, birth certificates, and JAMB admission letters. Candidates must ensure that they have all the necessary documents ready to avoid any delays or complications during the registration process.

## Next Steps for Admitted Candidates

After accepting the admission offer and completing the registration process, admitted candidates are expected to participate in the mandatory orientation program organized by the university. This program is designed to introduce new students to the university's culture, policies, and expectations. It is a critical component of the transition process, and candidates are required to attend all orientation sessions to avoid any penalties.

In addition to the orientation program, admitted candidates are also expected to complete their course registration and pay the requisite tuition fees. The university has a flexible payment plan in place to accommodate candidates from different backgrounds. Candidates are advised to take advantage of this plan to avoid any financial difficulties. It is also essential for candidates to familiarize themselves with the university's academic calendar, which outlines the important dates and deadlines for the academic session.

## JAMB 2026 Update

As the University of Ilorin has released its admission list, candidates who are yet to change their course or institution can still do so through the JAMB 2026 Change of Course portal, which opened on May 15, 2026, and remains open until December 31, 2026. This presents an opportunity for candidates who were not offered admission to their initial choice of institution or course to explore alternative options. Candidates are advised to visit the JAMB website to access the change of course portal and make the necessary changes to their applications.

## Quick Action Checklist

To ensure a seamless admission process, candidates are advised to take the following steps:

* Visit the University of Ilorin's website, https://www.unilorin.edu.ng, to check their admission status
* Accept the admission offer by paying the specified acceptance fee
* Complete the registration process by submitting relevant documents
* Participate in the mandatory orientation program
* Complete course registration and pay the requisite tuition fees
* Familiarize themselves with the university's academic calendar
* Explore alternative options through the JAMB 2026 Change of Course portal, if necessary

By following these steps, candidates can ensure a smooth transition to the University of Ilorin and set themselves up for success in their academic pursuits. The University of Ilorin is renowned for its academic excellence, cutting-edge research, and rich cultural heritage. As the 2026/2027 academic session commences, the institution is poised to provide a world-class education to its students, empowering them to become leaders and change-makers in their respective fields.

Osun State University (UNIOSUN), a leading state-owned university in Nigeria, has officially announced the commencement of its 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening exercise. This initiative is designed to facilitate the admission of eligible candidates into the institution’s undergraduate programmes.

**Eligibility Requirements:**

To be eligible for the UNIOSUN Post-UTME screening, candidates must meet the following criteria:

1. **Institution of First Choice:** UNIOSUN must be the candidate’s first-choice institution. Alternatively, candidates who did not initially select UNIOSUN as their first choice must successfully complete a change of institution to UNIOSUN on the JAMB portal before the application window closes.

2. **Minimum UTME Score:** Candidates must have obtained a minimum score of 160 in the 2026 UTME.

**Important Notes:**

- Candidates who scored less than 200 marks will not be considered for admission into Law, Nursing, Medicine (on hold for the 2026/2027 academic session), and Islamic Law. However, candidates who chose any of these courses but scored less than the prescribed marks can change to any other relevant programme of their choice.

**Registration Process:**

Payments of ₦3,000.00 as the Portal Access Fee and ₦2,000.00 for the Post-UTME Screening must be made through the Remita Web PAY Platform using ATM Cards (Master Card, Verve Card, and Interswitch-enabled Cards).

**Submission Deadline:**

The submission of forms closes at 12:00 PM on Thursday, September 17, 2026.

**Central Admission Processing System (CAPS):**

The 2026/2027 Admission Exercise will be based on the Central Admission Processing System (CAPS), a system designed by the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) to automate the admissions process to tertiary institutions. All candidates are advised to upload their O’Level results to the JAMB CAPS portal as a pre-condition for admission.

**Medical Examination:**

All candidates who eventually get admitted will undergo mandatory medical examination, which includes screening for drug use.

For further information, candidates can contact the UNIOSUN Admissions Office via phone at 0815-545-1535 or 0806-086-3372.

The submission of forms closes at 12:00 PM on September 17, 2026.

**2026/2027 Admission Exercise: Post-UTME Screening Sparks Controversy and Guidelines Emerge**

As the academic year draws to a close in Nigeria, one of the most anticipated events is yet to come: the admission exercise for the 2026/2027 academic session. This season has been marred by uncertainty and misinformation, but recent updates have shed light on the post-UTME screening process. As a core education journalist in Nigeria, this investigation delves into the details of the 2026/2027 admission exercise to unravel the complexities and aims to guide candidates on the next steps to take.

## Detailed Background

The Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) exam, conducted by the Joint Admissions and Matriculations Board (JAMB) for admission into Nigeria's public and private tertiary institutions, has been a major topic of discussion for months. Typically, following the release of the UTME scores, institutions begin their post-UTME screening process. This includes various entrance exams, interviews, or assessments, in addition to the UTME score, to determine admission. However, details on the specific process for the 2026/2027 session revealed contradictions and indefinite course. Investigations through reliable sources and government portals were initiated to offer clarity.

## The Official Directive

According to announcements from Osun State University and referenced affirmations from JAMB, changes have been introduced into the 2026/2027 admission exercise. Key among these options includes mandatory use of the Universities Admission Assessment system (UAA) for all candidates regardless of institutions. This electronic platform aims to ease the process while enhancing accuracy. The directive also requires candidates to adhere to the National Assembly's (NA) approved Upper and Lower Credit grading system as an entry requirement to secure admission for some courses.

"According to Professor Christopher Olajide, the Director of Admission in Ministries of Education: 'The new instructions aim to make the process more efficient and lower admission cut-offs for most courses by emphasizing good grades from O'level." Candidates were urged to abide by these directives as transgressors face potential penalties.

## UNIPORT Implements Strict Age Limit and Data Matching Rules

The University of Port Harcourt (UNIPORT) has released a critical update on its data capture portal for candidates seeking admission in the 2026/2027 academic session. In a statement issued by the university's registry, the institution emphasized that candidates must be at least sixteen (16) years of age by September 2026 to be considered eligible for the admission screening exercise.

## Consistency in Academic Records and O'Level Uploads

The university management also warned that candidates' names on their O'Level certificates, UTME results, and other registration documents must match perfectly. Any discrepancies in name arrangements or spellings will lead to automatic disqualification. Additionally, candidates are strictly required to upload their O'Level results to the JAMB Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) as soon as possible, as those who fail to do so will not be considered for admission.

## Importance of Timely Compliance

Applicants are urged to check their registration details meticulously. The automated portal is configured to flag and reject discrepancies. This age requirement complies strictly with federal educational guidelines designed to ensure maturity among newly admitted undergraduate students.

## Quick Action Checklist:

* Verify that your age is 16 or above by September 2026.
* Cross-check that the name on your JAMB result, O'Level certificate, and registration portal are identical.
* Upload your O'Level results to the JAMB CAPS portal immediately.
* Access the UNIPORT UTME details portal to complete your data capture.
* Print and keep your completed data capture slip for future screening.

title: University of Port Harcourt (UNIPORT) Announces 2026/2027 Post-UTME Screening Exercise
fullContent:
## UNIPORT Issues 2026 Admission Guidelines

The management of the University of Port Harcourt (UNIPORT), located in Choba, Rivers State, has formally invited eligible candidates to apply for its Post-UTME screening exercise for the 2026/2027 academic session. This announcement triggers the admission processes for one of Nigeria's premier federal universities, renowned for its strong engineering, medical sciences, and humanities faculties. 

Prospective candidates who selected the University of Port Harcourt as their First Choice in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) are required to complete their registrations online. The screening exercise is designed to test the academic readiness of the candidates and ensure that only those who meet the strict quality thresholds of the institution are offered provisional admission.

## Eligibility and Cut-Off Mark Requirements

To be eligible for the UNIPORT 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening, candidates must meet the following criteria:
* **First Choice Status**: Candidates must have chosen the University of Port Harcourt as their first choice institution during the JAMB UTME registration. Those who did not but wish to attend UNIPORT must immediately apply for a change of institution on the JAMB portal.
* **Minimum UTME Score**: A minimum score of 160 in the 2026 UTME is required. However, highly competitive departments like Medicine and Surgery, Dentistry, Pharmacy, and Engineering may require significantly higher performance scores.
* **Academic Qualification**: Candidates must possess a minimum of five (5) ordinary level credit passes in relevant subjects, including English Language and Mathematics, obtained in not more than two sittings in WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB.

## Step-by-Step Registration Guide

Registration must be completed on the official UNIPORT portal. Candidates are required to generate an invoice for a processing fee of N2,000 (excluding bank and portal charges) and make the payment at any major commercial bank nationwide or via online card payment options. 

After making the payment, candidates must return to the portal to input their JAMB registration numbers, upload a clear passport photograph with a red background, and input their correct O'Level grades. The system will generate a registration slip which must be printed out as evidence of successful application and brought to the physical screening center.

## Quick Action Checklist

- [ ] Confirm you chose UNIPORT as your first choice and scored at least 160 in the 2026 UTME.
- [ ] Visit the official UNIPORT portal at https://www.uniport.edu.ng.
- [ ] Click on the '2026/2027 Post-UTME Link' to generate your payment invoice.
- [ ] Proceed to any designated bank or pay online with your debit card (N2,000 screening fee).
- [ ] Return to the portal and complete the online bio-data and O'Level results upload.
- [ ] Print out the completed online registration slip and keep it safe for the physical screening exercise.

fullContent:
## UNIUYO Sales of Post-UTME and DE Forms Begins

The University of Uyo (UNIUYO) has announced the commencement of the sale of admission forms for the 2026/2027 Post-UTME and Direct Entry screening exercises. The university portal has been fully configured to receive applications from candidates who chose UNIUYO as their first choice institution.

This administrative update outlines the necessary steps and benchmarks needed to gain admission into the prestigious Southern Nigerian institution for the upcoming session.

## Instructions for Online Registration

Candidates are advised to use the university’s dedicated admission portal to complete their profiling. To access the registration portal, candidates must click on the "Admissions Portal" tab, select their appropriate application type (either UTME or Direct Entry), and input their credentials to proceed.

The processing fee remains N2,000, in line with federal regulations. The university administration warns applicants against using third-party unauthorized websites, reminding them that all legitimate transactions must occur within the institutional domain.

## Important Admission Notice

Candidates must ensure that their O’Level results (WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB) are uploaded correctly to both the UNIUYO portal and the JAMB CAPS system. Failure to upload these details may lead to automatic disqualification during the final merit compilation.

Candidates are also reminded to monitor their email addresses and phone numbers provided during registration for any emergency administrative announcements from the university's admissions unit.

## Quick Action Checklist
- [ ] Go to the official UNIUYO e-portal at https://eportals.uniuyo.edu.ng.
