excerpt: With many federal universities suffering from brain drain, Governing Councils are coordinating with the NUC to launch academic recruitment campaigns.
fullContent:
## Universities Move to Address Staff Shortages Ahead of New Academic Session

In response to the growing wave of academic migration (the 'Japa' phenomenon) and retiring senior faculty members, several public universities across Nigeria are organizing comprehensive academic recruitment drives. The newly constituted Governing Councils of key federal institutions, working alongside the National Universities Commission (NUC), have authorized university managements to begin compiling academic staff vacancies.

The objective of this initiative is to fill critical vacancies in high-demand departments such as Medicine, Engineering, Computer Science, and Law, where staff-to-student ratios have stretched beyond the acceptable thresholds defined by the NUC.

## Key Qualifications and Requirements for Candidates

Vacancies will range from Graduate Assistant and Lecturer II positions to Senior Lecturer and Professor roles. Candidates with Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) degrees from recognized institutions, strong records of scholarly publications in reputable peer-reviewed journals, and previous academic teaching experience will be prioritized.

The recruitment processes will be highly digitized to check nepotism, and applicants will be required to submit their curriculum vitae and research profiles through central web applications.
title: Federal Universities Face Lecturer Shortages; Governing Councils Plan Urgent Recruitment

fullContent: As the 2026 admission cycle kicks off, the 16-year minimum age requirement has become a central point of debate and investigation. JAMB has reiterated that only candidates who will be at least 16 years old by September 30, 2026, are generally eligible for admission into Nigerian tertiary institutions. However, a significant 'Exceptional Candidate' clause has been introduced for the first time this February. Candidates under 16 may apply for a waiver if they demonstrate extraordinary academic prowess, defined as scoring at least 80% across all qualifying exams, including the UTME, Post-UTME, and SSCE. This policy has a profound impact on admission chances for 2026. Statistical analysis suggests that approximately 12% of annual UTME applicants are under the age of 16. By enforcing this limit, JAMB is effectively 'thinning' the competition for older candidates, particularly in highly competitive fields like Law and Medicine. For the 'underage' students who do not meet the 80% waiver threshold, their results will be withheld until a formal evaluation process is completed at the end of the session. This creates a strategic advantage for mature candidates (17-20 years old), who may find that the effective cut-off marks for their desired courses drop slightly due to the exclusion of younger high-scorers. Furthermore, JAMB Registrar Prof. Oloyede warned that any institution offering 'illegal' admissions to underage candidates outside the CAPS framework will face severe sanctions. This enforcement closes the 'backdoor' through which many private universities previously admitted younger students. Prospective 2026 candidates are advised to calculate their age precisely against the September 30 cutoff. For those who fall short, the recommendation is to utilize the year for vocational training or A-level programs like IJMB or JUPEB, which remain viable pathways for future Direct Entry. The 16-year rule is part of a broader Ministry of Education effort to ensure emotional and psychological readiness for the rigors of university life.
title: JAMB Implements Strict 16-Year Age Limit with Exceptional Candidate Waiver for 2026
excerpt: Candidates must be 16 by September 30, 2026, to qualify for admission, though a new 80% score threshold offers a narrow path for younger 'exceptional' students.

title: Federal Government Enforces 16-Year Age Limit for 2026 Admissions with New 'Exceptional Ability' Clause
fullContent: The Federal Ministry of Education and JAMB have confirmed the continuation of the 16-year minimum age requirement for admission into Nigerian tertiary institutions for the 2026/2027 academic session. Specifically, candidates must be at least 16 years old by September 30, 2026, to be eligible for most undergraduate programs. This policy, which was a point of intense debate in previous years, aims to ensure that students are emotionally and psychologically mature enough for the rigors of university life. However, a significant update for the 2026 cycle is the formal introduction of the 'Exceptional Candidate Assessment.' This clause allows candidates who are younger than 16 to be considered for admission if they demonstrate extraordinary academic prowess. To qualify under this exemption, an underage candidate must score at least 320 (representing 80 percent) in the 2026 UTME and maintain a similar high performance in their Post-UTME or Senior School Certificate Examination (SSCE) results. This nuanced approach addresses the concerns of gifted students while maintaining the general age standard. For the majority of applicants, this policy means that admission lists will prioritize those who meet the age criteria, potentially reducing the overall competitive pool in certain high-demand courses like Engineering and Law, where younger 'prodigies' often apply in large numbers. School administrators have noted that this enforcement is helping secondary schools return to a standard six-year curriculum, discouraging the practice of 'skipping' classes. Candidates who do not meet the age requirement and do not qualify for the 'exceptional' status are encouraged to consider interim programs such as A-levels (IJMB or JUPEB) or vocational certifications, which can be used for Direct Entry (DE) once they reach the required age. The 2026 guidelines also state that the UTME results of underage candidates who fail to meet the 80 percent threshold will be withheld until they complete a mandatory evaluation process or reach the required age in a subsequent session.
excerpt: The 16-year minimum age requirement for university admission remains in force for the 2026 cycle, but with a new provision for exceptionally high-scoring younger candidates.

title: Strategic Admission Planning for Nigerian Students in 2026
fullContent:
With the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) successfully concluded and the minimum cut-off marks for the 2026/2027 session established, students across Nigeria are entering the most critical phase of their tertiary education journey: the admission process. Securing a spot in a university is not just about the score obtained in the exam; it is about strategic planning and timely responsiveness. The Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) is the central nerve center for this process, and every candidate must familiarize themselves with its functions.

First, ensure that your O’Level results (WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB) have been successfully uploaded to the JAMB portal. Without this, your profile will remain incomplete, and you will not be considered for admission by any university, regardless of your UTME score. Second, verify your email and phone number linked to your JAMB profile, as these are the primary channels through which communication will be sent. Many candidates lose their chances because they fail to monitor their profiles for notification of admission offers or requests for additional information from institutions.

Regarding the selection of institutions, if your current UTME score is below the average requirement for your preferred course in your chosen university, now is the time to utilize the 'Change of Institution' window. Do not aim for courses or schools where your score is clearly insufficient, as this will lead to a rejection. Instead, research alternative courses that align with your subjects or state-owned universities that may have a lower threshold while maintaining high academic standards. Finally, be wary of 'admission racketeers' who promise guaranteed placement for a fee. Admission into public universities is merit-based and regulated by JAMB. Anyone asking for money to 'process' your admission is likely a scammer. Stick to official channels to protect your future.

title: UNN Announces Admission for 2026/2027 Academic Session
fullContent:
University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) Announces 2026/2027 Admission Screening

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

The University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) has officially announced the commencement of its Post-UTME and Direct Entry screening registration for the 2026/2027 academic session. The registration portal opens on Wednesday, July 15, 2026, and closes at midnight on Wednesday, August 5, 2026 .

---

Eligibility Requirements

For UTME Candidates

To be eligible for the screening, candidates must:

· Choose UNN as first-choice institution during the 2026 UTME 
· Score 160 or above in the 2026 UTME 
· Be at least 16 years old on or before September 30, 2026 
· Ensure names on WAEC/NECO results exactly match JAMB registration details 
· Upload O'Level results to both JAMB CAPS and the UNN portal on or before August 5, 2026 

Note: Candidates waiting to write November/December 2026 GCE or NECO are not eligible to apply .

For Direct Entry Candidates

Direct Entry candidates who chose UNN as their first choice must also register for the screening. Their A'Level qualification must have been verified through the Nigerian Integrated Post-Secondary Education Data System (NIPEDS) before registration .

---

Application Fee

Fee Type Amount
Application Fee ₦2,000
Processing Fee ₦3,300
Total ₦5,300

Payment is made via Remita at any commercial bank or online using an ATM card through the UNN portal .

---

How to Apply

Follow these steps to complete your registration:

1. Visit the UNN official website: unn.edu.ng 
2. Click on UNN Portal (top right corner)
3. Click Prospective Students
4. Select "Online Application Form for 2026/2027 Admission Screening Exercise"
5. Enter your JAMB Registration Number
6. Click Generate Invoice 
7. Pay the ₦5,300 fee through Remita or ATM card
8. Return to the portal and complete your bio-data and O'Level details
9. Upload all required documents (each file must not exceed 100KB) 
10. Review your application summary carefully
11. Submit and print your Acknowledgement Slip

---

Documents Required

Candidates must upload the following documents :

· Birth Certificate (ensure date of birth is correct — no changes allowed after submission)
· Local Government Identification
· Testimonial
· First School Leaving Certificate
· JAMB Result Slip
· O'Level Result 1
· O'Level Result 2 (for double-sitting candidates)

---

Important Updates for 2026/2027

NUC Approves Unbundling of Mass Communication Programme

The National Universities Commission (NUC) has approved the unbundling of UNN's Department of Mass Communication into five distinct undergraduate programmes, effective from the 2026/2027 academic session .

New Programmes:

· B.Sc. Advertising
· B.Sc. Broadcasting
· B.Sc. Development Communication Studies
· B.Sc. Journalism and Media Studies
· B.Sc. Public Relations

The approval followed a comprehensive resource verification visit conducted by a panel of experts to assess the human and material capacity available for the proposed programmes .

JUPEB Programme Also Open

UNN has also announced admission into the Joint Universities Preliminary Examinations Board (JUPEB) programme for the 2026/2027 academic session. The programme leads to a JUPEB certificate, enabling candidates to gain Direct Entry admission into UNN and other JUPEB-affiliated universities .

Requirements:

· Minimum of 5 Credits in O'Level (including Mathematics and English) in not more than two sittings
· Application fee: ₦20,000
· Subject combinations available in Arts, Social Sciences, Management Sciences, and Sciences 

---

Quick Action Checklist

· ✅ Confirm you meet all eligibility requirements (160+, first choice, 16+ years)
· ✅ Verify names match on JAMB and O'Level records
· ✅ Prepare and compress documents to under 100KB each
· ✅ Visit unn.edu.ng from July 15 to register
· ✅ Pay ₦5,300 through Remita
· ✅ Upload O'Level results to both JAMB CAPS and UNN portal by August 5
· ✅ Print your Acknowledgement Slip
· ✅ Monitor UNN website for screening date updates

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official UNN announcements and verified sources as of July 2026. Always confirm critical information on the official UNN website at unn.edu.ng.

title: UNN Management Reduces Proposed Fee Hike to 60% Following Student Protests
fullContent: NSUKKA - The Governing Council of the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) has reached a consensus with student representatives to review the controversial fee hike for the 2025/2026 academic session. Originally proposing a 100% increment in sundry charges, the management faced widespread protests from students and parents. After several rounds of dialogue, the Acting Public Relations Officer, Mr. Inya Agha Egwu, announced that the increment has been pegged at 60%. The new agreement also includes a provision for students to pay in installments across two semesters to alleviate financial pressure. UNN Governing Council has implemented a 60 percent fee hike on sundry charges affecting both fresh and returning students of Technology, Agriculture, and Sciences.
fullContent:
## UNN Governing Council Implements Fee Hike for Fresh and Returning Students

The Governing Council of the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) has approved a 60 percent increment on sundry charges for both fresh and returning students of the institution. This decision has sparked discussions across the higher education sector as public universities struggle with funding deficits and operational costs.

## Analysis of the Fee Structure Changes

According to the newly approved structure, fees for faculties such as Technology, Science, and Agriculture have seen significant upward reviews. For instance, charges for new students in these faculties have been increased from N79,700 to N163,200, while returning students are also expected to pay higher fees. The university management stated that the increment is necessary to maintain and upgrade educational infrastructure and provide better facilities for learning.

## Impact on Student Registrations

The management has assured that plans are being put in place to ease payment options, such as enabling structured installations where possible, to ensure that the fee hike does not disrupt the academic progress of deserving students who may face immediate financial constraints.

## Quick Action Checklist:

* Visit the official UNN portal to check the updated fee structure for your department.
* Discuss the new payment schedule with your parents or sponsors immediately.
* Ensure you generate the correct payment invoice (RRR) on the portal.
* Complete your school fees payment before the registration deadline.
* File and secure your payment receipts for departmental and faculty clearances.
title: University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) Governing Council Approves 60% Increment on Sundry Charges
UNN Governing Council Approves Up to 60% Increment on Sundry Charges for Returning and New Students
fullContent:
## UNN Governing Council Approves 60% Fee Hike on Sundry Charges

The Governing Council of the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN), has approved a significant upward review of sundry charges for both newly admitted and returning students. This administrative decision, aimed at meeting the rising cost of running the institution, has generated discussions among academic stakeholders.

The university management explained that the adjustment is essential to keep the university's laboratories, libraries, and hostels functional and to maintain academic standards.

## Faculty Breakdown of New Fees

The new fee structure affects several faculties differently. Under the revised guidelines, fees for new students in the Faculty of Technology, Science, and Agriculture have been adjusted upwards from N79,700 to N163,200.

Returning students in these faculties will also experience a corresponding increase. The university authority has assured that the funds generated from these charges will be utilized to improve internet connectivity, hostel power supply, and standard lab gear.

## Reactions and Management Justification

While student union leaders and parents have voiced concerns about the financial burden this brings, the university senate has urged understanding. They pointed out that the current federal allocation to tertiary institutions is insufficient to maintain premium quality academic standards.

Management has also clarified that installment payment structures may be considered for students facing financial challenges, alongside active encouragement to utilize the Federal Government's NELFUND student loan program.

## Quick Action Checklist
- [ ] Visit the official UNN portal to check the exact fees for your department.
- [ ] Print your new invoice containing the updated sundry charges.
- [ ] Review the NELFUND student loan application guidelines if you require financial aid.
- [ ] Ensure you make payments using the approved school portal to avoid cyber fraud.
- [ ] Keep your payment receipts safe for semester clearance.

fullContent: Security operatives at the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) detained an Afia TV correspondent and cameraman on May 1, 2026. The journalists were reportedly covering the university's ongoing exercise to eject students deemed 'illegal occupants' of hostels, an initiative that has faced public scrutiny over living conditions.
title: UNN Security Detains Afia TV Journalists Covering Hostel Evictions

fullContent:
## Breakdown of the New Charges

The Governing Council of the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) has approved a 60 percent upward review of sundry charges for the current academic session. This policy decision was reached during a recent council meeting where the university's fiscal sustainability was analyzed in detail.

The adjustment will affect various student fees, including service fees, laboratory charges, and municipal utilities. The university administration cited the rising costs of maintenance, laboratory consumables, and electricity tariffs as major drivers of this tuition adjustment.

## Reaction from Student Leaders and Stakeholders

The news has generated intense discussion among student representatives, parents, and alumni. While the student union government has appealed for a review or phased payment options to cushion the impact, the management maintains that the adjustment is necessary to prevent a decline in academic standards and infrastructure quality.

- [ ] Plan your financial budget for the upcoming semester payments.
- [ ] Inquire about available installment payment options.
- [ ] Reach out to the student affairs division for updates on campus work-study opportunities.

The University of Ilesa has formally ratified the establishment of a new academic faculty, a move designed to diversify its course offerings and accommodate more students. The decision was officially reached and approved by the University's Governing Council on March 31, 2026, following a formal proposal submitted by the University Senate.

This development is part of the university's strategic plan to meet the growing demand for specialized education in the region. fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has clarified the channels through which candidates can access their 2026 UTME results. According to the board's spokesperson, Benjamin Fabian, the process is streamlined to ensure that candidates receive their scores directly on their registered phone numbers. Candidates are instructed to send the command 'UTME RESULT' to either 55019 or 66019 using the phone number they used during their initial registration.

Since the exam commenced on April 16, 2026, millions of students have participated across the country's 989 approved Computer-Based Test (CBT) centers. The board is currently processing the results in batches. For candidates who have not yet received their scores, the board assures that they are working to conclude the evaluation process for the remaining sets of scripts.

It is essential for candidates to be wary of fraudulent websites claiming to offer result upgrades or unofficial score changes. The official portal, portal.jamb.gov.ng, remains the only verified source for authentic exam information. Candidates experiencing issues with their results are advised to visit the nearest JAMB Zonal Office for support rather than engaging with unauthorized third-party platforms.
title: JAMB Clarifies UTME Result Verification Procedures for 2026 Candidates
excerpt: JAMB has outlined the official procedures for candidates to verify their 2026 UTME results via SMS and digital portals.
A deep dive into the participation statistics for the 2026 UTME, highlighting the record-breaking turnout of candidates.
title: 2026 UTME Records 2.2 Million Candidates: Trends and Insights
fullContent:
The 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) has set a new record in terms of candidate registration and participation. According to data provided by the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board, a total of 2,243,761 candidates registered and sat for the exam, marking a substantial increase from the previous year. This growth is a reflection of the rising population of secondary school leavers in Nigeria and an increased demand for tertiary education, even as the country navigates various economic and social challenges.

The examination, which took place at 989 centres across the country, was managed through the board’s established Computer-Based Test (CBT) infrastructure. The sheer scale of the operation underscores the ongoing commitment to digitizing the Nigerian admissions process. Following the conclusion of the exams, the board has been processing results in batches, with the first results released in late April to ensure that candidates were able to start their post-UTME preparations early. This efficiency is critical for maintaining the academic timeline for the 2026/2027 session.

For the students, the high turnout confirms that competition for available spaces in federal, state, and private universities will remain intense. With millions of candidates vying for limited spots, the significance of the admission cut-off marks and the proper management of the CAPS portal cannot be overstated. Students who did not meet their target scores are encouraged to explore alternative institutions or polytechnics, which provide equally viable paths to degree attainment through the direct entry or transfer programs. The data highlights a robust educational landscape, provided that the current infrastructure can continue to support the increasing demand for admission.

The 2026 admission cycle has introduced a series of strategic waivers designed to provide equitable opportunities for candidates across various states of the federation. As part of its broader reform package, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has implemented measures to cater to students from educationally disadvantaged backgrounds, ensuring that the admission process remains inclusive without compromising quality.

These waivers apply to specific categories of admissions, particularly in institutions located in states where the number of qualified applicants may be lower than the available quota. By adjusting the criteria for these regions, the board aims to bridge the gap in educational development between Northern and Southern Nigeria. Furthermore, the reforms include a push for tertiary institutions to maximize their capacity through the Central Admission Processing System (CAPS), which reduces human interference in the selection process.

Candidates are advised to familiarize themselves with these policies, as they may directly impact their admission chances. It is critical that students maintain a clean record and verify their O'level results on the CAPS portal to ensure seamless processing. As JAMB continues to iterate on its admission policies, the goal remains to harmonize the academic calendar across the country. Students are encouraged to stay tuned to official government bulletins for further clarifications on how these waivers apply to specific university departments.
title: Understanding the 2026 UTME Admissions Waivers and Reforms
excerpt: An in-depth look at the new admission waivers and systemic reforms introduced by JAMB for the current academic session.

fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has announced the release of the first batch of results for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME). A total of 632,788 candidates who participated in the first day of the examination can now access their scores through the board's official channels.

Candidates are encouraged to check their results using the SMS method. By sending 'RESULT' to either 55019 or 66019 using the mobile number associated with their registration profile, candidates will receive their performance notification. The board emphasized that the SMS method is the most reliable way to receive scores immediately upon release. 

For those who sat for the exam on April 16, 2026, and subsequent dates, the board has put in place a streamlined system to ensure prompt delivery of results. It is important for candidates to note that results are being released in batches to ensure thorough verification and data integrity. As the 2026 admission process picks up momentum, students are cautioned against patronizing fraudulent websites promising score upgrades or illegal admissions. JAMB maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards examination malpractice and urges all candidates to rely solely on the official portal for any information regarding their admission status or result clarifications.
title: JAMB Releases Day One UTME Results for Over 600,000 Candidates
excerpt: JAMB has officially commenced the release of 2026 UTME results, starting with the first batch of over 600,000 candidates.
Tertiary institutions across Nigeria are finalizing preparations for the 2026/2027 post-UTME screening exercises.
title: Nigerian Universities Begin Post-UTME Preparations for 2026/2027
fullContent:
With the conclusion of the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME), tertiary institutions across Nigeria are gearing up for the next phase of the admission process: the post-UTME screening exercises. Universities, polytechnics, and colleges of education are currently setting their specific departmental cut-off marks, which are expected to be higher than the national minimum threshold of 150 (for universities) and 100 (for polytechnics). 

Institutional portals are beginning to open for the registration of candidates who selected these schools as their first-choice institutions. Prospective students are advised that the post-UTME screening is a mandatory requirement for admission into most Nigerian universities. During this stage, schools will assess candidates based on their UTME scores, O'Level performance, and, in some cases, additional aptitude tests or oral interviews. 

Educational experts are warning candidates not to be complacent despite meeting the national cut-off. 'The national cut-off is merely a baseline,' notes Dr. Ibrahim Musa, an education analyst. 'Universities in high-demand fields like Medicine, Law, and Engineering will likely set significantly higher benchmarks.' Students are encouraged to start collating their secondary school results, including their WAEC or NECO certificates, and to ensure they have their JAMB registration slips ready. It is also advised that candidates regularly visit the websites of their preferred institutions to stay informed on the specific dates and formats of their post-UTME assessments, as these schedules vary by institution and are subject to change without notice.

excerpt: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has released a new policy update for the 2026 admission cycle. Highlights include strict adherence to approved quotas, changes in admission deadlines, and updates to cut-off marks.
fullContent:
## Official Policy Breakdown

The JAMB policy meeting held on April 5, 2026, brought significant changes to the admission process for the 2026 cycle. The new Directress of Information Technology Services (ITS) department, Mrs. Funmilola F a u zz i y ya h B. Us m a n, has emphasized the importance of adhering to the resolved quotas.

### Key Resolutions and Admission Deadlines

* Candidates who are offered admission will now have a maximum of four weeks to accept or decline their offer from the date of notification.
* Institutions are strictly prohibited from admitting students beyond their approved quotas.
* The new minimum tolerable admission scores have been set as follows: Universities - 150, Colleges of Nursing - 140, Polytechnics, Monotechnics, Colleges of Agriculture, Colleges of Education - 100, and Innovation Enterprise Institutions (IEIs) - 100.

### Implications of the New Policy

The new JAMB policy update has far-reaching implications for institutions and candidates alike.

#### Institutions

* Adherence to Approved Quotas

* Revised Admission Process

* New Minimum Tolerable Admission Scores

#### Candidates

* Shortened Deadline for Accepting Admission Offers

### The Future of Admission in Nigeria

The recent JAMB policy update sets a new precedent for the future of admission in Nigeria. As institutions adapt to the revised admission process and minimum tolerable admission scores, the country's education landscape will likely experience significant changes.

### Verifying Official Details

To guarantee authenticity and accuracy, refer to official JAMB website and their released documents for the 2026 policy meeting.

## Deep Context & Implications

The JAMB policy update directly affects institutions and candidates in Nigeria. For institutions, the emphasis on adhering to approved quotas and the revised admission process ensures a fair and structured admission cycle. New minimum tolerable admission scores, now in place, may impact the selection and recruitment processes within institutions.

### Institutions Prepare for Immediate Action

It's essential for institutions to prepare for this new policy to ensure compliance with the agreed-upon standards.

## Analysis of Key Admission Milestones

JAMB has established critical milestones in the 2026 admission cycle.

* Publication of Resolutions and Quotas

* Start of Admission Registration

* Deadline for Institutions to Admit Students

## Dates to Remem
ber

April 5, 2026, marked the beginning of this new chapter in JAMB's history as they commenced their policy update implementation.

## Relevant Documents

To stay informed about the JAMB policy update, access the official Board documents:

* JAMBulletin_04-05-2026.pdf (found on jamb.gov.ng)

* JAMBulletin_09-02-2026.pdf (also located on jamb.gov.ng)

## Emphasis on Continued Improvement

In jamb.gov.ng, it's evident that there is an ongoing commitment to tertiary education expansion through these Board-approved centres and others that are still in progress.

### Scholarships, Jobs, and Opportunities

While this JAMB policy update primarily addresses academic measures, we urge all our candidates to take note of scholarships, job openings, and opportunities as often they align with this unique academic climate.
title: JAMB Admission Policy Update 2026 Deadline And Key Admission Milestones

fullContent:
WAEC Introduces Reforms to Address "Stranded Results" and Examination Malpractice

Published: July 2026 | Source: CampusAI News

---

Overview

Following the challenges experienced during the 2025 West African Senior School Certificate Examination (WASSCE), where over 192,000 results (almost 10%) were withheld due to malpractice suspicions and technical glitches caused delays, the West African Examinations Council (WAEC) has implemented comprehensive reforms for the 2026 examination cycle. These reforms target both the prevention of malpractice and the timely release of results.

---

Key Reforms for 2026

1. Examination Malpractice Portal

WAEC has introduced a dedicated online portal where candidates accused of malpractice can view the allegations against them and defend themselves, ensuring fair hearing before any final decision is made.

· No payment required to access the portal
· Candidates can check their status and make representations online
· Portal address: waecinternational.org/complaints
· Candidates are advised to check the portal four weeks after results are released

Dr Amos Dangut, Head of WAEC Nigeria National Office, stated: "The candidates alleged to have been involved in examination malpractice will be uploaded on this portal and they are required to defend themselves against such allegations, if they are sure they're innocent".

2. Question Paper Serialisation

WAEC has implemented a new system where all examination questions are scrambled, ensuring no two candidates receive the same question sequence. This makes it significantly harder for candidates to collaborate or share answers during the examination.

3. 45-Day Result Release Commitment

WAEC has committed to releasing results within 45 days after the final examination paper. Certificates will be printed and issued to schools within 90 days.

"We have learnt from our glitches. We have perfected everything, and I speak authoritatively that we will not have a repeat of that glitch" — Dr Amos Dangut, WAEC Head of Nigeria Office

4. Enhanced Infrastructure Requirements

To prevent technical failures that caused delays in 2025, WAEC now requires all examination centres to have:

· At least two functional power sources (national grid, generator, or solar)
· Improved Computer-Based Testing (CBT) systems that can operate stably even with limited internet connectivity

5. Digital Certificate Services (WAEC DigiCert)

WAEC has rolled out the WAEC DigiCert platform across all five member countries: Nigeria, Ghana, The Gambia, Liberia, and Sierra Leone.

Features of DigiCert:

· Access, request, verify, and share digital certificate copies
· Recover lost WAEC examination numbers
· Available as mobile app on App Store and Play Store
· Physical certificates will continue to be issued

---

Additional Federal Government Reforms

The Federal Ministry of Education has introduced complementary measures effective from the 2026 examination cycle:

Reform Details
SS3 Transfer Ban Strict restriction on transfer of final-year students between schools to prevent "exam tourism"
National Continuous Assessment Mandatory submission windows: January, April, and August
Examination Learners' Identification Number New unique ID for tracking candidates through the examination process and beyond

---

2026 WASSCE Statistics

Detail Information
Total Candidates 1,959,636 candidates from 24,207 schools
Female Candidates 1,001,072 (51.08%)
Male Candidates 958,564 (48.92%)
Subjects 37 subjects, 97 papers
Supervisors Approximately 29,000 teachers
CBT Schools Over 450 schools (up from 40 last year)
Examination Period April 21 – June 19, 2026
Results Release Expected August 3, 2026

---

Impact on Candidates

What Candidates Should Know

· Results are final: The 45-day release commitment means candidates will not experience the prolonged delays of previous years
· Transparent process: The malpractice portal ensures candidates can defend themselves if accused
· Digital access: Candidates can now access their certificates digitally through WAEC DigiCert
· No hidden fees: No payment is required to access the malpractice portal

Important Notes on GCE

Dr Amos Dangut also announced that the private candidate examination (GCE) will no longer be held in September. Instead, it will be conducted between September and October, allowing results to be released before the end of the year.

---

Quick Action Checklist for Candidates

· ✅ Visit the WAEC malpractice portal at waecinternational.org/complaints to check your status after results are released
· ✅ Download the WAEC DigiCert app from App Store or Play Store for digital certificate access
· ✅ No scratch card is required to check malpractice status — login with your data
· ✅ Check the portal four weeks after results are released for any updates

---

📌 Editor's Note: This article is based on official WAEC announcements and verified sources as of July 2026. All reforms are effective from the 2026 examination cycle. For the latest updates, visit the official WAEC website at waec.org.
title: WAEC Begins Reform of Stranded Certificates in Nigeria

# Private Universities Record High Admission Demand For 2026 — CampusAI Nigeria

**Published:** July 18, 2026 | **Source:** CampusAI Nigeria

## What's Happening

Private universities in Nigeria have witnessed a significant surge in admission demand for the 2026 academic session. This development is a testament to the increasing popularity of private higher education in the country. As a result, prospective students are advised to plan their applications well ahead of time to avoid any last-minute conflicts.

## Key Details

*   **Date / Registration Timeline:** The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has released the 2026 UTME results, while institutions are allowed to fix their individual cut-off marks but not below the 150 points for Universities and Nursing sciences and the 100 points for Polytechnics. The official admission timeline will be announced by the management on the official portal.
*   **Fee:** The application fee for private universities varies, with most institutions charging between ₦50,000 and ₦150,000. An additional screening fee, if applicable, will be announced by the institution.
*   **Eligibility & Requirements:** To be eligible for admission, candidates must meet the JAMB score cutoff, O'Level sittings, and age requirements specified by each institution. As a general guide, candidates are expected to have a minimum of five credits in relevant subjects, including English and Mathematics.
*   **Deadline:** The official application deadline will be announced by the management of each institution on their official portal.

## Who Is Affected

The surge in admission demand affects prospective students planning to enroll in private universities for the 2026 academic session. This includes UTME, Direct Entry, and change of course candidates.

## What to Do Next

To take advantage of the high admission demand, follow these steps:

1.  **Check the official portal:** Visit the official website of the private university interested in and check for the latest admission updates.
2.  **Meet the eligibility requirements:** Ensure you meet the JAMB score cutoff, O'Level sittings, and age requirements specified by the institution.
3.  **Complete the application form:** Fill out the application form accurately and submit it within the stipulated deadline.
4.  **Pay the application fee:** Make payment of the application fee as specified by the institution.
5.  **Upload required documents:** Upload required documents, including your JAMB result, O'Level certificate, and passport.
6.  **Track your application:** Monitors the status of your application and is kept informed about any updates.

## Important Updates & Policy Changes

To ensure a smooth admission process, prospective students are advised to pay attention to the following policy changes:

*   **JAMB CAPS upload requirements**: Candidates must upload their JAMB result to the JAMB CAPS portal to be considered for admission.
*   **Physical screening policies**: Some institutions may conduct physical screening, while others might offer remote screening, details of which will be provided on the institution's official portal.
*   **Change of institution parameters**: The procedure for changing institutions, if applicable, will be outlined on the official portal.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### Q: What is the application fee for private universities in Nigeria?
A: The application fee for private universities in Nigeria varies, with most institutions charging between ₦50,000 and ₦150,000. An additional screening fee, if applicable, will be announced by the institution.

### Q: What are the eligibility requirements for admission into private universities?
A: To be eligible for admission, candidates must meet the JAMB score cutoff, O'Level sittings, and age requirements specified by each institution. As a general guide, candidates are expected to have a minimum of five credits in relevant subjects, including English and Mathematics.

### Q: What is the expected deadline for the admission application process?
A: The official application deadline will be announced by the management of each institution on their official portal. It is advisable to plan and complete the application process well ahead of the stipulated deadline to avoid any last-minute conflicts.

## Conclusion

The high demand for admission into private universities for the 2026 academic session presents an opportunity for prospective students to enroll in these institutions. To avoid any last-minute conflicts, candidates are advised to plan their applications well ahead of time and follow the steps outlined above to ensure a smooth admission process.

> **📌 Editor's Note:** ​Always verify dates, fees, and guidelines on the official portal (e.g., relevant .edu.ng or .gov.ng domains) before initiating payments or registering.

fullContent: YABATECH Opens Post-UTME Screening Registration for 2026/2027 Academic Session: A Comprehensive Guide for CandidatesYaba College of Technology (YABATECH), Nigeria’s premier tertiary institution, has officially announced the commencement of the online Post-UTME screening registration for its Full-Time National Diploma (ND) and B.Sc. (Edu.) programmes for the 2026/2027 academic session.This guide provides a clear breakdown of the eligibility requirements, application procedures, and key deadlines to help you navigate your registration smoothly.Eligibility CriteriaTo qualify for the YABATECH 2026/2027 Post-UTME screening, candidates must meet the following baseline requirements:JAMB Choice: You must have chosen Yaba College of Technology as your First Choice institution.Note: Candidates who chose YABATECH as a second choice must log into the JAMB portal to complete a Change of Institution to make YABATECH their first choice before they can register.Minimum UTME Score: You must have scored a minimum of 120 marks (for ND programmes) or 180 marks (for B.Sc. Education programmes run in affiliation with the University of Nigeria, Nsukka) in the 2026 UTME.O'Level Requirements: You must possess a minimum of five (5) O'Level credit passes in relevant subjects, including English Language and Mathematics, obtained in not more than two sittings (WAEC, NECO, or NABTEB).Step-by-Step Registration ProcedureOrder is critical when submitting your application. Follow these steps carefully to ensure your registration is valid:1.Visit the Official Portal:Do not use third-party mirror sites.Go to the official YABATECH portal at www.yabatech.edu.ng and click on the "Post-UTME Application" link.2.Generate Payment Invoice:Official fee is ₦2,000.Enter your JAMB Registration Number to generate a payment invoice. The official screening fee is ₦2,000 (excluding bank charges and portal administration fees).3.Make Your Payment:Online or Bank branch options available.Pay online using a debit card (Remita) or print the invoice and pay at any commercial bank branch nationwide. Ensure you receive a payment confirmation receipt.4.Complete the Online Form:Ensure all data matches your O'Level and JAMB slips.Return to the portal, log in with your JAMB Registration Number as your username and your Surname as your password. Carefully fill in your bio-data, O'Level results, and UTME subject scores.5.Upload Required Documents:Clear, color passport photograph required.Upload your passport photograph (clear, on a white background, not exceeding 20KB in size) and digital copies of your O'Level certificate(s) and JAMB result slip.6.Submit and Print Your Slip:Review all fields before final submission.Cross-check all entered information. Click submit, then print your Post-UTME Screening Registration Slip. This slip contains your examination date and must be brought to the screening venue.Screening Schedule & Important DatesRegistration Period: Online registration commences immediately and will run until the designated closing date announced on the college portal.Screening Examinations: The Computer-Based Test (CBT) screening exercises are tentatively scheduled to take place between August 15th and August 22nd, 2026.Venue/Time Details: Your specific screening date, batch, and venue will be printed on your Post-UTME Registration Slip after successful submission.Quick Action Checklist for Candidates[ ] Verify that your 2026 UTME score meets the cut-off mark for your proposed course.[ ] Ensure YABATECH is set as your first choice on the JAMB CAPS portal.[ ] Upload your O'Level results (WAEC/NECO/NABTEB) to the JAMB CAPS portal at an accredited CBT center (compulsory for admission consideration).[ ] Prepare standard, scanned copies of your credentials for easy portal uploading.

title: Yaba College of Technology Opens Post-UTME Screening for 2026/2027 Academic Session
fullContent:
Yaba College of Technology (YABATECH), a leading institution in technical and vocational education in Nigeria, has officially opened the sale of Post-UTME screening forms for the 2026/2027 academic session. This initiative is designed to facilitate the admission of eligible candidates into the institution’s Full-Time National Diploma (ND) programmes and B.Sc. (Education) programmes (100 and 200 Levels) affiliated with the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN).

**Eligibility Requirements:**

To be eligible for the YABATECH Post-UTME screening, candidates must meet the following criteria:

1. **Institution of First Choice:** YABATECH must be the candidate’s first-choice institution.

2. **Minimum UTME Score:** Candidates must have obtained a minimum score of 160 in the 2026 UTME for Full-Time National Diploma (ND) programmes and B.Sc. (Education) Degree Programmes (100 Level) affiliated with UNN.

**Application Process:**

Prospective candidates are required to follow these steps to complete their application:

1. **Visit the College Website:** Candidates should visit the official YABATECH website at www.yabatech.edu.ng.

2. **Access the Portal:** Click on the “POST UTME SCREENING 2026/2027” link.

3. **Complete the Application Form:** Fill in the online application form as instructed.

4. **Pay the Screening Fee:** Pay the screening fee of ₦2,000.00 (excluding result verification and transaction charges). Payments can be made online or through designated banking channels. Note that the College does not accept cash payments.

**Important Instructions:**

- Candidates must provide accurate and complete information during registration. Any false declaration or incorrect information may lead to disqualification.
- Names, photographs, O’Level results, UTME records, and all registration documents must be consistent. Discrepancies in a candidate’s name or photograph may result in disqualification.

The application portal will remain open for four weeks from the date of publication of this notice. For further information, candidates can contact the YABATECH Admissions Office via email at registrar@yabatech.edu.ng or records@yabatech.edu.ng, or by phone at +234-7037431055.
